confirmed.title: Delete 12 dead has*TokensForAgent/Board exports (zero call sites repo-wide)
confirmed.description: Every provider file exports a hasXTokensForAgent + hasXTokensForBoard pair: gdrive.ts:32-40, gmail.ts:31-39, onedrive.ts:28-36, outlook.ts:33-41, github.ts:128-136, slack.ts:90-98. A repo-wide grep (including frontend and tests, excluding node_modules) finds zero importers — the only matches are the 12 definitions themselves. They are 1-line wrappers around hasOAuthToken and mislead readers into thinking external callers depend on these files' token-presence semantics. In github.ts and slack.ts the hasOAuthToken import (github.ts:4, slack.ts:4) exists solely to feed these dead functions, so it goes too.
confirmed.sketch: Delete the 12 functions. In github.ts and slack.ts also drop hasOAuthToken from the database.js import list (gdrive/gmail/onedrive/outlook keep it — their /status handlers use it). Verify with tsc + grep that nothing references them.
confirmed.impact: ~54 lines removed across 6 files, 2 unused imports removed; shrinks each provider file's apparent public API to what is actually consumed (the get*AccessTokenForAgent functions and the router factory)
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as written; extend it to 6 identical dead siblings the proposal missed: hasJiraCredentialsForAgent/Board (api/src/routes/jira.ts:19-27), hasS3CredentialsForAgent/Board (api/src/routes/s3.ts:14-22), hasWordPressCredentialsForAgent/Board (api/src/routes/wordpress.ts:39-47) — same grep methodology shows zero call sites. In all three of those files hasOAuthToken is used only by these dead wrappers (jira.ts:3/21/26, s3.ts:4/16/21, wordpress.ts:3/41/46), so drop that import there too, bringing the total to 18 dead functions and 5 unused imports. No behavior-preservation traps: nothing references any of these symbols, the wrappers have no side effects, and api tsc will catch any missed reference.

confirmed.title: Single resolveScope: one function copy-pasted 8 times
confirmed.description: The byte-identical 5-line agent→board→user scope resolver appears 8 times: gdrive.ts:26-30, gmail.ts:25-29, onedrive.ts:18-22, outlook.ts:23-27, github.ts:73-77, slack.ts:76-80, googleOAuth.ts:103-111, microsoftOAuth.ts:107-115. This is THE core token-scoping rule of the whole plugin system (documented in 4 separate file-header comments), yet a change to it would require 8 synchronized edits. Six of the copies are even untyped (implicit any params) while the two callback files have the typed version.
confirmed.sketch: export function resolveScope(agentId: string | null, boardId: string | null, username: string | undefined): { scopeType: ScopeType; scopeId: string } in api/src/routes/oauthHelper.ts (it already is the shared OAuth utility module). Replace all 8 local definitions with the import. Pure move — identical body, the typed variant.
confirmed.impact: ~45 lines removed; the scope-resolution rule gets exactly one definition next to its documentation; trivially safe first step that the larger proposals below build on
confirmed.guidance: 1) Not a literal 'pure move': with the typed signature, tsc (gate) breaks at the 6 /status call sites that pass uncast query values — gmail.ts:95-96→99, gdrive.ts:96-97→100, onedrive.ts:96-97→100, outlook.ts:97-98→101, github.ts:263-264→267, slack.ts:182-183→186 — because Express types req.query.X as string|string[]|ParsedQs|ParsedQs[], not assignable to string|null even under strict:false. Add the cast pattern already used in the same files' /auth-url handlers: `(req.query.agentId as string | undefined) || null` (cf. gmail.ts:120-121). Casts are compile-time only; runtime identical (including the existing quirk that array-valued query params flow through as truthy scopeIds — do not 'fix' that here). Disconnect handlers use req.body (any) and the two callback files pass typed stateData — no casts needed there. 2) Do NOT absorb wordpress.ts:26, s3.ts:8, jira.ts:13 — that is a different 2-param resolveScope returning `| null` with no user fallback; merging it would change behavior. 3) In oauthHelper.ts use `import type { ScopeType } from '../services/database.js'` to keep the module runtime-dependency-free. 4) username typed `string | undefined` vs the callbacks' `string` is equivalent under strict:false; `string | undefined` is the more honest choice since gmail.ts:97 et al. pass `req.user?.username`.

confirmed.title: createOAuthStateStore factory: 4 copies of HMAC-signed state machinery (~250 lines of security-critical clone)
confirmed.description: The full stateless-state implementation — STATE_TTL_MS, consumedStates Map, fallbackStateSecret, getStateSecret (HKDF from JWT_SECRET), signStatePayload, generateOAuthState, consumeOAuthState — is copy-pasted 4 times: googleOAuth.ts:32-101, microsoftOAuth.ts:34-105, github.ts:15-71, slack.ts:15-74. The only real differences are the HKDF domain string ('pulsarteam:oauth-state:google:v1' etc.) and the payload shape (google adds `service`, microsoft adds `service`+`consumerFlow`). microsoftOAuth.ts:143-151 additionally has a `peekServiceFromState` that re-parses the payload by hand. This is timing-safe-compare + replay-guard crypto code: any future fix (e.g. the consumed-states sweep, TTL handling) must currently be applied 4 times, and the copies have already drifted cosmetically (github.ts:45 compresses the sweep to one line; slack.ts builds `entry` separately). Only googleOAuth's copy has tests.
confirmed.impact: ~250 duplicated lines collapse to ~85 shared + 4 one-line instantiations (net ~-160); the replay/TTL/signature logic gets one audited, tested home instead of four drifting copies
confirmed.guidance: Corrections and traps for the implementer: (1) Keep BOTH consumedStates Map and fallbackStateSecret per-factory-instance (closure-scoped), never module-level shared — the dev fallback path (no JWT_SECRET) has no domain separation, so a shared fallback secret would let a google-issued state verify under another provider's consume, a behavior change. (2) The sketched generic consume(state): T | null glosses over per-provider normalization: google/github/slack return agentId: entry.agentId || null, boardId: entry.boardId || null; microsoft additionally coerces consumerFlow: !!entry.consumerFlow. Do not return the raw JSON.parse result typed as T (it carries nonce/expiresAt and skips the || null coercion); either have the factory take a normalize/project callback or keep the field-mapping in the thin per-provider wrappers. (3) peek must NOT verify the signature — microsoftOAuth.ts:143-151 parses the payload unverified by design (try/catch swallow) so error popups can still be routed; keep the 'onedrive'|'outlook' value whitelist in the microsoft wrapper or call site, not the generic factory. (4) The sketch says to 'port' googleOAuthState.test.ts — unnecessary: with generateGoogleOAuthState/consumeGoogleOAuthState kept as exported wrappers the test file runs unchanged; leave it untouched and optionally add factory-level tests. (5) github.ts:43/53 and slack.ts:43/56 have implicit-any params; call sites github.ts:288 and slack.ts:214 pass req.query.agentId || null (Express qs types). A typed generate(entry: T) will surface tsc errors there — fix with type assertions (as string | null), not runtime coercions like String(), which would change embedded values for array-shaped query params. (6) Preserve sweep-before-insert ordering (sweep runs in generate only, not consume) and key the replay map on the full state string. (7) As the sketch already flags: the four HKDF domain strings must stay byte-identical ('pulsarteam:oauth-state:{google|microsoft|github|slack}:v1') so states issued before a rolling deploy still verify after it when JWT_SECRET is set.

confirmed.title: Shared oauthProviderRoutes + refreshProviderToken: gdrive/gmail/onedrive/outlook are ~85% the same file
confirmed.description:
Quantified absorption for the suspected clone family (the four files total 640 lines):
(1) Token refresh — refreshGdriveToken (gdrive.ts:42-85), refreshGmailToken (gmail.ts:41-84), refreshOnedriveToken (onedrive.ts:38-85), refreshOutlookToken (outlook.ts:43-86) are ~180 lines differing ONLY in provider string, log label, config getter, and token-endpoint URL (onedrive adds the consumerFlow tenant pick at onedrive.ts:53). The subtle invalid_grant-vs-transient deletion policy (the comment at gdrive.ts:63-64 etc.) is maintained in quadruplicate.
(2) GET /status — gdrive.ts:94-113, gmail.ts:93-112, onedrive.ts:94-112, outlook.ts:95-114, plus github.ts:261-278 and slack.ts:180-197: six ~19-line handlers identical except the identity field name (email/login/teamName/username) and github/slack's connected predicate.
(3) POST /disconnect — gdrive.ts:145-153, gmail.ts:147-155, onedrive.ts:154-162, outlook.ts:150-158, github.ts:300-308, slack.ts:226-234: six 9-line handlers identical except the provider string and log label.
(4) GET /auth-url — same scaffold (config guard, agentId/boardId extraction, state, redirect URI, URLSearchParams, res.json) in all six; only scopes/endpoint/extra params differ.
Also: onedrive.ts:24-26 and outlook.ts:29-31 define getConfig() as a pure no-op alias of getMicrosoftOAuthConfig, while github.ts:79-84 and slack.ts:82-88 give the SAME name to a real env-reading config builder — confusing when reading files side by side; the spec object removes the aliases.
NOT absorbable (stays per-provider): onedrive's consumerFlow login_hint block (onedrive.ts:122-149), github's fetchWithRetry + redirect router, slack's comma-joined scopes and fixed env redirectUri, the four getXAccessTokenForAgent exports (consumed by the *Mcp.ts services).
confirmed.impact: Net ~-320 lines across 6 files (640 → ~300 for the four clones, plus ~55 from github/slack handlers); the refresh deletion policy, status semantics, and disconnect logic each get exactly one implementation; adding a future provider becomes a ~35-line spec instead of a 160-line copy-paste
confirmed.guidance: Sketch corrections (all minor, none invalidating): (1) The spec needs TWO not-configured messages, not one: the refresh functions throw short messages ('Google Drive not configured' gdrive.ts:44, 'Gmail not configured' gmail.ts:43, 'OneDrive not configured' onedrive.ts:40, 'Outlook not configured' outlook.ts:45) while the /auth-url 500s return longer setup-instruction messages that ALSO differ between gdrive (gdrive.ts:118, long variant mentioning client reuse) and gmail (gmail.ts:117, short variant) despite sharing a config getter. Do not derive either from the log label ('Gdrive' ≠ 'Google Drive'). (2) Make the spec generic over the config type (OAuthProviderSpec<TConfig>): slack's getConfig returns {clientId, clientSecret, redirectUri} (slack.ts:82-88) and its buildAuthUrl needs config.redirectUri — the sketched {clientId; clientSecret; tenantId?} type would drop it. (3) Preserve the non-equivalent connected predicates as explicit overrides: default hasOAuthToken for the four refresh providers (expired-but-refreshable counts as connected, comment gdrive.ts:102-103), github !!(token && token.accessToken) (github.ts:269), slack !!token (slack.ts:188) — these three are NOT interchangeable; do not harmonize. (4) onedrive parses consumerFlow from req.query for both state generation (onedrive.ts:122,129 — extra 5th arg to generateMicrosoftOAuthState) and URL building (135,146-149 — tenant pick + login_hint + prompt); with generateState(...,req) and buildAuthUrl(req,...) it gets parsed twice, which is fine (pure), but both must read consumer==='1'||'true' identically. (5) onedrive's /status identity field is the requesting username (onedrive.ts:108), not token meta — keep statusFields(token, connected, username) as sketched. (6) Keep thrown-error composition verbatim: data.error_description || data.error || `Token refresh failed (HTTP ${status})`, and the exact warn/log line formats incl. emoji prefixes and scopeType:scopeId. (7) resolveScope is identically duplicated in all six files — move it into the shared module too (pure, zero risk); the sketch implies but doesn't state this. (8) Bonus dead code: all twelve hasXTokensForAgent/Board exports (gdrive.ts:32-40, gmail.ts:31-39, onedrive.ts:28-36, outlook.ts:33-41, github.ts:128-136, slack.ts:90-98) are imported nowhere — delete them in the same pass rather than porting them into specs. (9) No tests cover these handlers; land as two commits (four-clone family first, then github/slack adopting oauthProviderRoutes) to keep diffs reviewable, and rely on tsc + manual diff of each provider's emitted URLs/JSON against the originals. (10) Keep onedrive's oddly-named getAccessTokenForAgent export name as-is (onedriveMcp.ts:4 imports it) — renaming is out of scope for behavior preservation.

confirmed.title: Rename onedrive's generically-named getAccessTokenForAgent
confirmed.description: onedrive.ts:87 exports getAccessTokenForAgent — the only one of six siblings without a provider prefix (getGdriveAccessTokenForAgent, getGmailAccessTokenForAgent, getOutlookAccessTokenForAgent, getGitHubAccessTokenForAgent, getSlackAccessTokenForAgent). At its call sites in onedriveMcp.ts:23, 273, 306 the bare name reads like a generic multi-provider resolver rather than a OneDrive-specific token fetch, and it shadows the mental slot occupied by database.js's resolveAccessToken.
confirmed.sketch: Rename to getOnedriveAccessTokenForAgent in onedrive.ts:87 and update the import + 3 call sites in onedriveMcp.ts (lines 4, 23, 273, 306). Pure rename, internal consumers only.
confirmed.impact: 5 edit sites; eliminates the one misleadingly-generic name in an otherwise uniform six-provider naming scheme
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is accurate as-is. One precision: the function is async, so the renamed declaration is `export async function getOnedriveAccessTokenForAgent(agentId, boardId = null)`. Adjacent observation for the parent survey (do not fold into this rename): all six get*AccessTokenForAgent functions are identical one-line wrappers around resolveAccessToken('<provider>', agentId, boardId, refresh<Provider>Token) — a separate duplication proposal could collapse them, and doing the rename now neither blocks nor is blocked by that.

confirmed.title: Move hardcoded personal email login_hint out of onedrive auth-url code
confirmed.description: onedrive.ts:147 hardcodes params.set('login_hint', 'gvinsot@hotmail.com') — the owner's personal email baked into shared route logic, with a 4-line comment (onedrive.ts:131-134) explaining it pins a specific B2B-guest identity. Any other user clicking the consumer-flow connect gets someone else's email pre-filled. As code, it makes a general-purpose route read like a one-user patch and is the single most surprising line in the nine-file scope.
confirmed.sketch: const consumerLoginHint = process.env.MICROSOFT_CONSUMER_LOGIN_HINT || 'gvinsot@hotmail.com'; (default preserves today's behavior exactly) and params.set('login_hint', consumerLoginHint). Keep the explanatory comment, now describing the env knob. Optionally drop the hint entirely when the env var is unset in a later behavior-reviewed change.
confirmed.impact: 1 magic value becomes configuration; the consumer-flow branch reads as a feature instead of a personal workaround
confirmed.guidance: Read process.env inside the request handler (or extend getMicrosoftOAuthConfig) rather than at module top-level — the existing pattern in api/src/services/microsoftOAuthConfig.ts:28-35 reads env per call. Document MICROSOFT_CONSUMER_LOGIN_HINT in the env-var comment block at microsoftOAuthConfig.ts:11-14, the established home for Microsoft env vars. Note '|| default' means an empty env var cannot disable the hint (falls back to the default); keep it that way for this change and leave hint removal to the later behavior-reviewed step the sketch already proposes. Do not touch the adjacent params.set('prompt', 'select_account') at onedrive.ts:148.

confirmed.title: Unify the Google/Microsoft OAuth callback handlers behind one token-exchange engine
confirmed.description: handleGoogleOAuthCallback (googleOAuth.ts:158-229) and handleMicrosoftOAuthCallback (microsoftOAuth.ts:153-232) are step-for-step the same ~75-line flow: provider-error guard → code/state guard → consume state → config guard → form-urlencoded code exchange with 15s timeout → fetchUserEmail (googleOAuth.ts:113-127 vs microsoftOAuth.ts:117-131, same shape with different URL/fields) → resolveScope → storeOAuthToken with throwOnPersistError → result page; even the comments match ('Must match the redirect_uri sent in the auth URL — derive the same way.'). Real divergences are small and enumerable: token endpoint (fixed Google URL vs tenant/consumerFlow-dependent), profile endpoint/field, meta payload, and the postMessage strategy (Google: per-service message types via googleOAuthResult/MESSAGE_TYPE_BY_SERVICE at lines 129-156; Microsoft: single 'microsoft-oauth-callback' type + service field + peekServiceFromState). Today a fix to the exchange flow (e.g. the timeout, error mapping, persist semantics) must be mirrored by hand.
confirmed.impact: ~150 lines of mirrored flow collapse to ~75 shared + 2 hook objects (net ~-50, but the bigger win is that exchange-flow fixes stop needing manual mirroring); pairs naturally with the state-store factory proposal
confirmed.guidance:
Traps to respect (all verified against the code):

1. sendResult as sketched cannot reproduce Microsoft's early-error behavior. Microsoft calls peekServiceFromState(rawState) on the provider-error branch (microsoftOAuth.ts:158) and the missing-code/state branch (:163), but deliberately NOT on the invalid/expired-state branch (:168) — that message carries no service field, so no widget claims it and the error shows only in the popup (OneDriveConnect.tsx:53 filters on event.data.service). Since 'missing code' can occur with a present raw state, (consumedState, rawState) alone cannot distinguish the peek branch from the no-peek branch. Pass the raw state string AND a stage discriminator to the error-send hook (e.g. sendError(res, stage: 'provider_error'|'missing_params'|'bad_state'|'not_configured'|'exchange_failed'|'internal', consumed: S|null, rawState: string|undefined, error: string)), or keep separate early-error hooks. Google's early branches map to service=null (gmail-oauth-callback fallback, googleOAuth.ts:148-151) — preserve that too.

2. fetchProfileEmail(accessToken) as sketched loses Google's per-service warn label: googleOAuth.ts:113-127 logs `[gmail]`/`[gdrive]` while Microsoft logs `[Microsoft]` (microsoftOAuth.ts:128). Pass the consumed state into the hook: fetchProfileEmail(accessToken, state).

3. Console log labels differ throughout: Google logs `[${stateData.service}]` ('gmail'/'gdrive'), Microsoft logs `[${providerLabel}]` ('OneDrive'/'Outlook'). Add a logLabel(state) hook if you want strict log parity (logs are not wire format, but operators may grep them).

4. provider passed to storeOAuthToken is stateData.service in both — make the generic S carry service, or add a provider(state) hook; do not hardcode.

5. Do NOT pull github.ts (handleOAuthRedirect, api/src/routes/github.ts:179-250) or slack.ts (:105-169) into the same engine in this pass, even though they share the skeleton: their guard ORDER differs (config check before state consume — github.ts:192-200, slack.ts:118-126, vs state-before-config in Google/Microsoft), GitHub uses a JSON body + fetchWithRetry + no AbortSignal timeout + different failure predicate (!response.ok || data.error || !data.access_token) and a different catch-path message, and Slack keys success on data.ok with no timeout and uses config.redirectUri instead of deriving from req. Forcing them in would either change behavior or bloat the hook surface; mention them as possible future consumers at most.

6. No test exercises either callback handler today, so the existing suite will not catch regressions here; add a couple of handler-level tests (mock fetch, assert the exact postMessage payload JSON per branch — type/service/email/error fields byte-for-byte) before or with the refactor.

7. Keep the per-file state machinery (generate/consume, HKDF domain strings 'pulsarteam:oauth-state:google:v1' vs ':microsoft:v1', Microsoft's consumerFlow field) out of this change, as the proposal already says — it is the subject of the separate state-store-factory proposal, and googleOAuthState.test.ts depends on the Google exports staying put.

confirmed.title: getGitHubCredentialsForAgent re-implements the scope-fallback chain with quietly different semantics
confirmed.description: github.ts:149-177 walks agent→board scopes by hand (lines 154-167) to capture meta.login, then calls resolveAccessToken for the user fallback. The hand-rolled walk uses cache-only getOAuthToken while resolveAccessToken's equivalent steps use fetchOAuthTokenWithDbFallback (oauthTokens.ts:284) — so the same provider has two fallback chains with diverging cache/DB semantics: an agent-scoped token persisted by another replica is found by getGitHubAccessTokenForAgent but missed by getGitHubCredentialsForAgent's first loop (it then silently falls through to resolveAccessToken, which finds it — but reports login: null). This check-then-act split exists only because resolveAccessToken returns a bare string and drops the record meta.
confirmed.sketch:
Add to oauthTokens.ts next to resolveAccessToken:

export async function resolveOAuthTokenRecord(provider, agentId, boardId): Promise<{ record: OAuthTokenRecord; scopeType: ScopeType } | null>

(same walk, returns the record instead of the string; resolveAccessToken can delegate to it). getGitHubCredentialsForAgent then becomes ~8 lines: const hit = await resolveOAuthTokenRecord('github', agentId, boardId); return hit ? { token: hit.record.accessToken, login: hit.scopeType === 'user' ? null : hit.record.meta?.login ?? null, provider: 'github' } : null; — the scopeType guard preserves today's login: null on user-scope fallback exactly. The 7 call sites (chat.ts, crud.ts, tasks.ts, tools.ts, terminal.ts, actionExecutor.ts) are untouched.
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines → ~8 in github.ts; removes the second, semantically-drifted copy of the fallback chain; the agent/board DB-fallback inconsistency disappears as a side effect
confirmed.guidance:
Three corrections to the sketch, one declared behavior delta to document:

1. SIGNATURE: resolveOAuthTokenRecord(provider, agentId, boardId) cannot absorb resolveAccessToken's walk as sketched, because the walk interleaves expiry-check + refreshFn + continue-on-failure per scope (oauthTokens.ts:290-299: refresh failure must CONTINUE to the next scope, which is impossible if the walk already returned a record). Amend to resolveOAuthTokenRecord(provider, agentId, boardId, refreshFn?) returning { record, scopeType, accessToken } | null where accessToken is the post-refresh token (=== record.accessToken when no refresh occurred). resolveAccessToken then becomes: const hit = await resolveOAuthTokenRecord(...); if (!hit) throw new Error(`Not connected to ${provider}. Please authenticate first.`); return hit.accessToken; — preserving the exact throw message and the refresh semantics for gmail/gdrive/onedrive/outlook (gmail.ts:87, gdrive.ts:88, onedrive.ts:88, outlook.ts:89 pass refreshFns). Keep the expiry-skip (expired + no refresh → continue) inside the walk; for github it is a no-op since github tokens are stored without expiresAt (github.ts:235-241).

2. BARREL + TEST MOCK: export resolveOAuthTokenRecord from the api/src/services/database.ts barrel (line 48 re-export list), AND add `resolveOAuthTokenRecord: async () => null` to the mock.module namedExports in api/src/services/__tests__/workflow-pipeline.test.ts (lines 19-131, see resolveAccessToken at line 129). The mock is an explicit namedExports list — a missing named import is an ESM link error that fails the whole test file, since github.ts is loaded transitively.

3. KEEP the sketch's scopeType === 'user' ? null : meta?.login guard — it is load-bearing. User-scoped records DO carry meta.login (stored unconditionally at github.ts:240), so dropping the guard would change the user-scope fallback path too.

DECLARED BEHAVIOR DELTA (accept, but note in the commit message): in the cold-cache/cross-replica window, an agent- or board-scoped token found via DB fallback now reports its meta.login instead of null. Observable effect is confined to the git credential username sent to runner-service (runnerExecutionProvider.ts:115/202 → agent_user.py:424-427/467, null → 'x-access-token' fallback); GitHub accepts both per runner-service's own comment, login is its stated preference, and the token is identical either way. Trivial non-issue: the old first loop required tok.accessToken truthy (github.ts:160) while the walk only checks record existence — only distinguishable for empty-string tokens, and every consumer gates on creds.token truthiness (runnerExecutionProvider.ts:83, chat.ts:112, tools.ts:477, terminal.ts:287).

confirmed.title: Unify the wordpress/jira/s3 credential-connector route files into one factory
confirmed.description: Three near-identical 'simple credential connector' modules: identical resolveScope() (wordpress.ts:26-30, jira.ts:13-17, s3.ts:8-12), identical get<Provider>CredentialsForAgent agent-then-board fallback (wordpress.ts:49-62, jira.ts:29-40, s3.ts:24-34), and three structurally identical routers: GET /status (wordpress.ts:67-83, jira.ts:45-61, s3.ts:39-55), POST /connect that probes the credentials then storeOAuthToken with throwOnPersistError + the same `target = agentId ? agent "…" : board "…"` log line (wordpress.ts:85-131, jira.ts:63-103, s3.ts:57-94), and a byte-identical POST /disconnect (wordpress.ts:133-144, jira.ts:105-116, s3.ts:96-107). Only the probe call, meta shape, and status fields differ. Any fix (e.g. the timeout handling that wordpress has at :126-128 but jira/s3 lack) must currently be made three times.
confirmed.impact: ~377 lines across 3 files → ~190 (factory + 3 thin configs); the agent→board scope-resolution policy and the connect/store/verify flow exist exactly once; timeout/error handling becomes uniform
confirmed.guidance: Behavior-preservation traps the sketch must absorb: (1) The connect catch paths diverge and the sketch's impact line "timeout/error handling becomes uniform" is a behavior change as written — today wp maps TimeoutError→504 else 500 (wordpress.ts:124-129), jira always 500 (jira.ts:99-102) even though it HAS the same 15s AbortSignal at jira.ts:76 (it lacks only the 504 mapping), and s3 returns 400 (s3.ts:90-93). Error message prefixes also differ ('Connection failed:' vs 'S3 connection failed:'). Give the factory a per-provider onError(err)=>{status,message} knob to preserve these exactly; unify only with owner sign-off as a separate, declared wire change. (2) storeOAuthToken({throwOnPersistError:true}) throws land in that same catch, so store-failure status also differs today (wp/jira 500, s3 400) — same knob covers it. (3) wp destructures `req.body || {}` (wordpress.ts:86) while jira/s3 destructure `req.body` bare (jira.ts:64, s3.ts:58); a body-less request currently 500s on jira/s3 but 400s on wp — using `|| {}` in the factory flips jira/s3 to 400 on that degenerate input (fine, but note it). (4) The sketch omits two needed config knobs: the log label ('[WordPress]' is not capitalize('wordpress')) and the per-provider success-log suffix (`→ ${cleanUrl} (${me.name || username})` vs `→ ${cleanDomain} (${myself.displayName || email})` vs `→ ${awsRegion} (${bucketCount} buckets)`) — have connect() return a logSuffix alongside accessToken/meta/extra. (5) Per-provider required-field validation and its exact error strings belong inside connect() returning {error, status:400}, as sketched. While in there, delete the confirmed-dead has* exports (wordpress.ts:39-47, jira.ts:19-27, s3.ts:14-22) and the unused resolveAccessToken import (jira.ts:3) — no consumers exist anywhere in the repo. Since zero tests cover these routes, consider one supertest file over the factory as a safety net; the refactor itself is otherwise unguarded.

confirmed.title: Extract shared task execution-state helpers in tasks.ts (stop-executor, clear-state, mem-task lookup)
confirmed.description: tasks.ts repeats three micro-rituals everywhere. (1) 'stop the executing agent and clear actionRunning flags' appears verbatim in PUT /:id (tasks.ts:216-226) and bulk-move (tasks.ts:424-430), and in spirit in agents.ts PATCH (agents.ts:467-471). (2) 'clear execution state after a status change' (startedAt=null, executionStatus=null, delete _pendingOnEnter/_completedActionIdx, completedActionIdx=null, actionRunning=false, delete actionRunningAgentId/actionRunningMode, completedAt if done, setTaskSignal) appears in PUT /:id twice — once on the copy at tasks.ts:291-298 and once on the memTask at tasks.ts:345-357 — again in bulk-move at tasks.ts:458-468, and partially in /:id/stop at tasks.ts:513-524. The lists drift: the copy version at :291-298 omits _pendingOnEnter/_completedActionIdx that the memTask version clears, and a reader cannot tell if that is intentional. (3) the mem-task lookup `mgr._getAgentTasks(agentId)?.find(t => t.id === id)` appears 7 times (tasks.ts:185, 327, 453, 511, 551, 563; agents.ts:463).
confirmed.impact: ~60 lines removed from tasks.ts; the 8-field 'reset a task' invariant is defined once instead of 5 drifting copies; future fields (like completedActionIdx) cannot be forgotten in one variant
confirmed.guidance: Implement narrowed, not as sketched. (1) getMemTask(mgr, agentId, taskId): safe everywhere; _getAgentTasks returns [] never undefined (api/src/services/agentManager/index.ts:478-480); apply to tasks.ts:185, 327, 453, 511, 551, 563 and agents.ts:463, 511, 514, 516, 526, 546, 613. (2) stopTaskExecutor + clearActionRunning: extract for tasks.ts:216-226 and 424-430 only; helper must use `delete` (not `= null`); keep the differing outer conditions at call sites. Do NOT apply to agents.ts:467-471 (different ritual: stops req.params.id directly, never clears actionRunning flags, calls setTaskSignal) or to /:id/stop tasks.ts:513-515 (uses `= null`; delete-vs-null changes the task:updated socket payload emitted via the {...target} spread at tasks.ts:527 — saveTaskToDb is insensitive per database/tasks.ts:179-180, but the wire payload is not). (3) clearExecutionState: restrict to the 8 clear-fields (startedAt=null, executionStatus=null, delete _pendingOnEnter, delete _completedActionIdx, completedActionIdx=null, actionRunning trio) and apply ONLY at the two memTask sites tasks.ts:345-353 and 458-465, which are identical. Do NOT apply the superset to the PUT /:id copy (tasks.ts:291-298): getTask returns a shallow copy (agentManager/tasks.ts:1355-1358) persisted via saveTaskDirectly at tasks.ts:361, and completedActionIdx/_pendingOnEnter ARE persisted columns (database/tasks.ts:177 completed_action_idx, :185 pending_on_enter) — the superset would null them in the DB row where today they are retained (observable after restart rehydration). Keep the copy's shorter clear inline with a comment naming the asymmetry. (4) Keep completedAt OUT of the helper: bulk-move's memTask block never stamps completedAt even when targetColumn==='done' (tasks.ts:453-466); PUT /:id does (:297, :354) — the sketch's done-check would add a new write at bulk-move. (5) Keep setTaskSignal OUT of the helper: PUT /:id signals only inside if(memTask) (:356); bulk-move signals unconditionally outside the guard (:468, fires even with no memTask). (6) Do not reorder bulk-move: saveTaskDirectly at :449 intentionally(?) precedes the memTask clear at :452-466 — leave persistence ordering and the un-cleared copy as-is. (7) /:id/stop becomes zero calls, not one; the 'each become one call per site' claim and the agents.ts file listing in the proposal overreach. Expected savings ~35-40 lines, not ~60.

confirmed.title: Replace 13 hand-rolled voice tool constants in realtime.ts with a tool() builder
confirmed.description: realtime.ts:5-170 is 165 lines of 13 structurally identical OpenAI function-tool literals (DELEGATE_TOOL … CLEAR_ALL_ACTION_LOGS_TOOL), each repeating `type:'function'`, `parameters:{type:'object', properties:{…}, required:[…]}`. Eight of them take the same single `agent_name` parameter. The required array duplicates the property keys by hand. The actual information content — name, description, params — is buried under 10 lines of envelope per tool.
confirmed.impact: realtime.ts 271 → ~150 lines; every tool reads as one expression; adding a voice tool is 1-3 lines instead of 12
confirmed.guidance: Two corrections/notes: (1) There are TWO identical test files importing VOICE_TOOLS, not one — api/src/services/__tests__/realtimeRoutes.test.ts AND api/tests/realtime.test.ts. Both assert reference identity and both remain green; just be aware when running gates. (2) Type the builder's props parameter permissively — e.g. Record<string, { type: string; description: string; [k: string]: unknown }> — because OpenAI function-tool properties may later need enum/items/nested-schema fields; the sketch's strict { type; description } shape would force rework. (3) Preserve key insertion order as in the sketch (type, name, description, parameters; and type, properties, required) to keep JSON.stringify output byte-identical. (4) rollback's `count` property is type 'integer' (realtime.ts:123) — covered by the string-typed `type` field, just don't hardcode 'string'.

confirmed.title: requireBoardAccess(level) middleware to collapse 14 copies of the checkBoardAccess preamble in boards.ts
confirmed.description: Every handler in boards.ts opens with the same two lines: `const access = await checkBoardAccess(req.params.id, req.user.userId, req.user.role, <level>); if (!access.ok) return res.status(access.status).json({ error: access.error });` — at boards.ts:102-103, 129-130, 143-144, 161-162, 175-176, 189-190, 208-209, 226-227, 239-240, 259-260, 271-272, 310-311, 332-333, 350-351. The interesting part of each handler (what it actually does to the board) starts 3-4 lines in, and the access level is an easy-to-miss string argument in the middle of the call.
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines removed in boards.ts (+~14 more in projects.ts if adopted); the permission level moves to the route declaration where reviewers look for it
confirmed.guidance: (1) Do NOT add a new requireBoardAccess — authorizeBoardAccess at api/src/middleware/auth.ts:835-851 already implements exactly this (introduced in security commit 24ae2135, currently dead with 0 call sites). Wire it into the routes instead; writing the sketch's function verbatim would create a duplicate of an existing helper. (2) ORDERING TRAP: the sketch puts the access middleware before validateBody, but today validation runs first (route middleware) and the access check runs inside the handler. To keep responses identical for invalid-body+no-access requests (400-validation today, would become 403/404), order as `validateBody(schema), authorizeBoardAccess(level)` on the 8 routes that have both: boards.ts PUT /:id (127), PUT /:id/workflow (141), PUT /:id/plugins (173), POST /:id/plugins/assign (187), POST /:id/plugins/remove (206), PUT /:id/mcp-auth (224), POST /:id/shares (269), PUT /:id/shares/:userId (308). (3) Known minor wire difference on the 500 path: if checkBoardAccess throws, inline code returns { error: err.message } via the handler's catch, while the middleware returns { error: 'Authorization check failed' } (auth.ts:848). No test pins this (there are no HTTP-level board-route tests; authorization.test.ts tests checkBoardAccess directly), but flag it in the commit message. (4) In converted handlers replace access.board/permission/isOwner with req.boardAccess.* — the middleware attaches no `ok` field. (5) Scope: leave agents.ts:110/122 and tasks.ts:34/60 alone (board id comes from agent/task, not req.params); leave projects.ts:160-176 (dual project+board check) and resolveBoardGitHubAuth (projects.ts:336+, query-param flow fused with token resolution) alone. projects.ts:187, 197, 227, 285 adopt authorizeBoardAccess('read', 'boardId') cleanly.

confirmed.title: Extract the cache-lookup / fetch / stale-fallback pattern shared by the 5 GitHub explorer endpoints in projects.ts
confirmed.description: github-activity (projects.ts:365-415), github-branches (:417-438), github-tree (:440-467), github-file (:469-518) and code-graph (:525-602) all repeat the same skeleton: resolveBoardGitHubAuth → build cacheKey → `if (cached && Date.now()-cached.time < TTL) return res.json(cached.data)` → try { fetch + map + cacheSet + res.json } catch { console.error; `if (cached) return res.json(cached.data)`; 500 }. The serve-stale-on-error policy and the cacheSet bound parameters are restated five times with slightly different max-entries magic numbers (500/500/100/1000/50) and cache-key prefixes ('', 'branches:', 'tree:', 'file:', 'cg:') that exist only because the caches would otherwise collide if merged.
confirmed.impact: ~50 lines removed from a 605-line file; the 'serve stale when GitHub is down' behavior — currently discoverable only by reading 5 catch blocks — is stated once
confirmed.guidance: 1) SCOPE CORRECTION: apply the helper to the 4 GET endpoints only; exclude POST /code-graph/:owner/:repo (:525-602). Its catch block (:598-601) has no stale fallback and returns `Code graph analysis failed: ${err.message}` (err detail in body); its tree-fetch failure path responds 502 inside the try (:546-550). Forcing it into serveCached would add a stale fallback that doesn't exist today, lose err.message, and break on the early 502 (res.json after headers sent / cacheSet(undefined)). 2) The single `label` param can't reproduce current logs: console messages carry per-endpoint context (e.g. `Failed to fetch tree for ${owner}/${repo}@${ref}` :463, `Failed to fetch file ${filePath} for ...` :514) while response bodies use different generic strings (`Failed to fetch file tree` :465, `Failed to fetch file content` :516). Pass two strings (logContext + responseError) to preserve both exactly. 3) Keep capturing `cached` before build() runs (the sketch already does) — current code serves the pre-fetch snapshot as stale fallback even if a concurrent request repopulated the cache mid-await. 4) Do NOT merge the five Map caches; the description's claim that key prefixes exist to prevent collisions is wrong (they are separate Maps, :21-25). One prefix is load-bearing: _fileCache is shared by github-file (`file:` keys, shaped objects) and code-graph's fetchFile (`cg-file:` keys, raw strings, :557-577) — leave untouched. 5) github-file's `if (!filePath) return 400` (:475) and github-activity's forceRefresh parsing (:372) stay outside the helper, passed as key/force. 6) No API tests cover these routes (grep over api/ matches only projects.ts), so verification is tsc + manual; keep the transformation strictly mechanical. Expected savings ~30-35 lines, not ~50.

confirmed.title: Merge internalClaudeTokens.ts and internalCodexTokens.ts into one token-store route factory
confirmed.description: Two routers with the same three endpoints (GET/POST/DELETE /:ownerId) against the same oauth_tokens store, including a copy-pasted 16-line 'read back and tryDecrypt to verify durable persistence' block with its load-bearing comment duplicated (internalClaudeTokens.ts:55-75 vs internalCodexTokens.ts:21-39 — same SQL, same logic, same rationale). Only the provider constant ('claude_code' vs 'codex'), the GET response shape (refreshToken vs meta), and the POST body parsing (expiresIn→expiresAt math vs meta passthrough) differ. internalCodexTokens.ts is also written in a cramped multiple-statements-per-line style unlike every sibling file.
confirmed.impact: 137 lines across 2 files → ~85 in one file; the rotate-token-must-verify-persistence invariant (critical for Claude refresh-token rotation) is implemented and commented once
confirmed.guidance: Sketch corrections and traps: (1) Type the factory's provider param as OAuthProvider (the union exported from services/database/oauthTokens.ts:26), not string — fetchOAuthTokenWithDbFallback and OAuthTokenRecord.provider require it, so `provider: string` fails tsc. (2) Preserve the claude POST expiry precedence exactly: expiresAt (number, epoch ms) wins over expiresIn (seconds-from-now math), internalClaudeTokens.ts:39-44; keep this inside the claude parse hook, do not invent a shared expiry parser. (3) Preserve claude's refreshToken filter `typeof refreshToken === 'string' && refreshToken ? refreshToken : null` (line 51) and codex's meta filter `meta && typeof meta === 'object' ? meta : undefined` (line 20) verbatim in their parse hooks — storeOAuthToken normalizes null/undefined identically downstream, but keeping them verbatim makes review trivial. (4) verifyPersisted must keep the no-pool semantics: getPool() returning null means DB-less deployment → treated as persisted (route returns 200), and the route 500s only when a pool exists and the read-back/decrypt mismatch occurs; keep the catch→persisted=false inside the helper. (5) Keep the merged comment with the claude version's fuller rationale (refresh tokens rotate; a lost rotated token is unrecoverable after restart) — that is the most valuable sentence in either file. (6) The order ownerId-guard → parse(400) → storeOAuthToken → verify(500) → {ok:true} must be unchanged; the Python consumers in runner-service/src/backends/{claude,codex}_token_store.py depend on these status codes and GET body field names (claude: accessToken/refreshToken/expiresAt; codex: accessToken/expiresAt/meta). (7) No JS tests cover these routes, so the only safety net is tsc — diff the two serialize/parse hooks against the original handlers line by line. Minor: specs/api/swarm-and-internal.md cites internalClaudeTokens.ts by filename, but docs are out of scope per the rules.

confirmed.title: Delete six dead has*CredentialsForAgent/Board exports
confirmed.description: hasWordPressCredentialsForAgent / hasWordPressCredentialsForBoard (wordpress.ts:39-47), hasJiraCredentialsForAgent / hasJiraCredentialsForBoard (jira.ts:19-27), and hasS3CredentialsForAgent / hasS3CredentialsForBoard (s3.ts:14-22) are exported but imported nowhere in the repo (verified by grep over api/, frontend/, runner-service/ excluding node_modules — only the definitions match). The get*CredentialsForAgent siblings ARE used (services/jiraMcp.ts:4, services/s3Mcp.ts:17, services/wordpressMcp.ts:6); these boolean variants are vestigial scaffolding that misleads readers into thinking some caller gates on them.
confirmed.sketch: Remove the six functions (and the now-unused hasOAuthToken import in each file if nothing else uses it). No call sites to update. If the connector-factory proposal is implemented, these die as part of it.
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines removed; eliminates a false signal that connection state is queried somewhere outside the /status endpoints
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as written; two refinements. (a) The conditional "remove the now-unused hasOAuthToken import if nothing else uses it" is definite, not conditional: in wordpress.ts, jira.ts, and s3.ts, hasOAuthToken appears only inside the six dead functions — remove it from the import list in all three files or tsc/noUnusedLocals may flag it. (b) The proposal undercounts the dead family: the identical zero-call-site pattern also exists in five more route files — hasGdriveTokensForAgent/Board (gdrive.ts:32-40), hasGmailTokensForAgent/Board (gmail.ts:31-39), hasOutlookTokensForAgent/Board (outlook.ts:33-41), hasGitHubTokensForAgent/Board (github.ts:128-136), hasSlackTokensForAgent/Board (slack.ts:90-98) — verified by grep to have only definition matches. If extending, note the import-cleanup trap differs there: gmail.ts, gdrive.ts, outlook.ts (and onedrive.ts) ALSO call hasOAuthToken directly in their /status handlers (gmail.ts:103, gdrive.ts:104, outlook.ts:105, onedrive.ts:103), so keep the hasOAuthToken import in those files; in github.ts and slack.ts it is only used by the dead functions and can be removed. Gate to re-run after: api tsc + 244 tests.

confirmed.title: Split the 200-line PUT /tasks/:id handler into named phases and share the board-move core with bulk-move
confirmed.description: PUT /tasks/:id (tasks.ts:198-399) is a single handler doing seven things in sequence: stop-running-executor (:216-226), board-move with column revalidation (:238-258), column-only validation (:259-276), scalar field diffing into editedFields (:279-298), history-entry assembly (:300-322), a second field-by-field re-application of the same patch onto the in-memory task (:324-359), then persistence, auto-refine, three emits, and the audit log (:361-393). The memTask sync block restates every assignment from :279-286 a second time, so adding one task field requires touching four places in this one handler. Meanwhile POST /bulk-move (:402-495) reimplements the board-move + history + clear-state + emit pipeline per task with small drift (e.g. bulk history entries get `bulk: true`, fields hardcoded).
confirmed.sketch:
Keep the route, extract phase functions (composing with the clearExecutionState/stopTaskExecutor helpers from the other proposal):

  async function resolveBoardMove(task, { boardId, column }, user): Promise<{ ok: boolean; status?; error?; boardChanged; statusChanged; oldBoardId; oldBoardName; newBoardName }>
  function applyTaskEdits(target, patch): string[]            // returns editedFields; called once for the copy AND once for memTask — kills the duplicated assignment block at :324-359
  function buildHistoryEntry({ now, by, boardMove, statusChange, editedFields, bulk })  // shared with bulk-move (:440-447)
  function emitTaskUpdate(mgr, task)                           // task:updated + agent:updated pair, used at :382-386, :474-478, :527

PUT /:id body becomes ~40 lines of phase calls reading like the comment headers it already has; bulk-move's per-task loop becomes resolveBoardMove + applyTaskEdits + buildHistoryEntry + clearExecutionState + emitTaskUpdate.
confirmed.impact: tasks.ts ~945 → ~820 lines; the copy/memTask dual-write becomes one function applied twice; bulk-move and single-move can no longer drift apart
confirmed.guidance: Descope and fix the sketch before implementing. (1) DROP the resolveBoardMove/buildHistoryEntry sharing with bulk-move; the paths differ on purpose: PUT's column-only change returns a strict 400 on invalid column (:267-272) while bulk silently falls back via validateColumn (:412-414); PUT validates destination access once per request, bulk once per request too — a per-task resolveBoardMove would re-run checkBoardAccess+getBoardById per task, changing query volume and failure surface; PUT pushes history with fields/from only when statusChanged (:317-320), bulk hardcodes fields ['boardId','status']+from unconditionally even when the column didn't change (:441-447) — history is persisted JSON; PUT orders memTask-sync → save → auto-refine, bulk orders save → memTask-sync → auto-refine. (2) setTaskSignal placement trap: PUT calls it INSIDE if(memTask) (:356) so DB-only tasks are never signaled; bulk calls it OUTSIDE (:468). A shared helper must not unify this. (3) bulk's memTask sync is gated on oldStatus !== targetColumn (:452), so a same-column cross-board bulk move never updates memTask.boardId — latent bug; a shared sync would silently fix it (behavior change, flag separately instead). (4) The two clear-execution-state blocks are NOT duplicates: the memTask one (:345-357) additionally deletes _pendingOnEnter/_completedActionIdx and sets completedActionIdx=null; applying that to the copy would persist completedActionIdx=null via saveTaskDirectly(task) where the current DB write leaves it untouched — keep two variants or parameterize. (5) Sketch error: emitTaskUpdate must NOT absorb :527 — /:id/stop emits only task:updated with a constructed { ...target, agentId } payload and no agent:updated; the helper fits exactly :382-386 and :474-478. (6) applyTaskEdits must exclude column/boardId: memTask :331-332 carries the RESOLVED status from validateColumn fallback (:252-254), not the raw patch column; keep status/boardId sync as a separate line after board resolution. With this scoping the effort drops from large to medium and risk from medium to low.

confirmed.title: Move the 77-line agent zod schemas out of agents.ts into schemas/agents.ts, and fold the twin access guards into a factory
confirmed.description: Sibling routes already keep zod schemas in api/src/schemas/ (tasks.ts imports from ../schemas/tasks.js at :7-11, boards.ts from ../schemas/boards.js, projects.ts from ../schemas/projects.js), but agents.ts inlines createAgentSchema/updateAgentSchema at agents.ts:12-88, pushing the first route definition to line 128 of a 726-line file. Separately, requireAgentAccess (agents.ts:104-113) and requireAgentEditAccess (:117-125) are identical except for the 'read'/'edit' literal — the comment at :117-118 is the only thing distinguishing them. Also note the routes at agents.ts:691-724 are dedented two levels relative to every other route in the factory, visually suggesting they sit outside the router when they do not.
confirmed.impact: agents.ts 726 → ~630 lines and starts with routes instead of a schema wall; one access-guard implementation instead of two near-twins; matches the established schemas/ convention
confirmed.guidance: Three traps to respect, all easy: (1) Keep `import { z } from 'zod'` in agents.ts — `err instanceof z.ZodError` at :212 and :234 still needs it after the schema move. (2) Verbatim move ONLY: do not also adopt `validateBody` from lib/validate.js the way tasks.ts does — agents.ts's inline parse + catch produces its own error-response shape, and switching would change the wire format for validation failures. Export both schemas from the new schemas/agents.ts (updateAgentSchema depends on createAgentSchema via .partial().extend(), so they must move together). (3) The guards close over `agentManager`, so the `agentAccess(level)` factory must be defined inside agentRoutes(agentManager), at the same spot the current functions occupy (above the first use at :181). Preserve the two explanatory comments — the 'Agents without a board are accessible to everyone (legacy)' note at :108 and the read-only-shares rationale at :115-116 — by attaching them to the factory and/or the two derived consts. Minor: `const parsed: any` at :200 should stay as-is.

confirmed.title: Flatten the PATCH /agents/:id/tasks/:taskId update-dispatch chain
confirmed.description: agents.ts:452-535 mixes three styles in one handler: (a) the try{ at :453 is followed by a body that is NOT indented (lines 454-521 sit at the same level as the try), so the catch at :522 visually floats; (b) recurrence/taskType/isManual updates are applied imperatively up front (:474-488) while text/repo/storage/status are dispatched through a 30-line else-if precedence chain (:490-519) whose three trailing branches (`else if (recurrence !== undefined) … else if (taskType !== undefined) … else if (isManual !== undefined)`) exist only to re-find the task for the response; (c) if NO recognized field is present the final else silently calls `toggleTask()` (:518) — a surprising hidden behavior that nothing in the route name or comments advertises.
confirmed.impact: ~85-line handler → ~50 lines with one obvious flow; the hidden toggleTask fallback becomes an explicit, commented line; the broken indentation stops misrepresenting scope
confirmed.guidance: Corrections to the sketch: (1) The reviewer missed that `title` (agents.ts:491) is NOT in the :494 else-if chain — a {title}-only body (sent by frontend TaskDetailModal.tsx:111-113 when only the title changed) today runs updateTaskTitle AND THEN toggleTask via the final else, flipping the task's status. Including title in the `touched` array changes this behavior. This is almost certainly a latent bug worth fixing, but it must be flagged to the owner as an explicit behavior change bundled with the refactor, not done silently. (2) Compute `touched` with the SAME predicates as the dispatch branches: status must be truthiness-checked (`else if (status)` today), not `!== undefined`, or {status:null}/{status:''} bodies stop reaching toggleTask — another wire change on a public route. (3) Use the hedged variant: keep text/repo/storage/status as mutually exclusive else-ifs (today {text,status} applies text and silently ignores status); only hoist the response lookup and delete the three no-op tail branches. (4) Keep the `oldTask` guard on the isManual block (:484-488) — it dereferences oldTask directly; the recurrence/taskType guards can be dropped safely since updateTaskRecurrence/updateTaskType null-check internally. (5) Re-find-for-response is identity-safe: all mgr.updateTaskX methods (services/agentManager/tasks.ts:178,250,264,278,293,308) return the live in-memory task object. (6) The empty-body -> toggleTask path MUST survive: frontend api.ts:270-274 depends on it; add the comment the sketch proposes. (7) No route-level tests cover this handler, so nothing breaks in CI — but also no safety net; verify manually with the six frontend payload shapes ({}, {status}, {text}, {title}/{title,text}, {recurrence}, {isManual}, {taskType}, {repoFullName,repoProvider}, {storagePath,storageProvider}).

confirmed.title: One shared getUserBoardIds helper instead of two private copies plus an inline rebuild
confirmed.description: The 'resolve the caller's accessible board IDs, swallowing errors' helper exists twice with different return types: tasks.ts:13-20 returns Promise<string[]> while agents.ts:94-101 returns Promise<Set<string>> — same body otherwise. boards.ts:58-59 re-derives the Set inline from getBoardsByUser. The type split forces readers to check which flavor they hold (tasks.ts uses array for SQL ANY($1), agents.ts uses Set.has).
confirmed.impact: ~16 duplicated lines removed; one canonical 'visible boards' resolution with one error policy instead of three
confirmed.guidance: 1) EXCLUDE boards.ts:58-59 from the dedup. It is inside a try/catch that returns 500 on getBoardsByUser failure; the shared error-swallowing helper would change that failure to a 200 with empty/filtered results. Leave the 2-line inline derivation (or keep calling getBoardsByUser directly there). The dedup is tasks.ts + agents.ts only. 2) Do NOT add the helper to api/src/services/database.ts. Six tests mock the whole module via mock.module('../database.js', { namedExports }) and projectTenantIsolation.test.ts imports routes/tasks.js under that mock — a new export would be undefined and throw at runtime. Put the helper in a new small module (e.g. api/src/lib/boardAccess.ts) that imports getBoardsByUser from services/database.js; mock.module intercepts the resolved database module for the helper too, so existing test mocks keep working unchanged. 3) tasks.ts adaptations beyond the sketched spread: lines 97 and 790 use boardIds.length === 0 and must become .size === 0 (tsc catches a miss); lines 698-699 push boardIds into both summaryParams and dailyParams — spread to [...boardIds] in both. 4) Minor: agents.ts has 12 call sites (129, 139, 152, 159, 165, 361, 375, 565, 693, 701, 709, 718), not 8.

confirmed.title: Share the agent-status handler logic duplicated between leaderTools.ts and agents.ts
confirmed.description: Four endpoints exist twice with the same shape: /all-statuses with its project filter (leaderTools.ts:57-70) vs /statuses (agents.ts:137-148 — identical lowercase-project filter copy-pasted), /swarm-status (leaderTools.ts:73-75 vs agents.ts:164-167), /by-project/:project (leaderTools.ts:78-81 vs agents.ts:151-155), /project-summary (leaderTools.ts:84-86 vs agents.ts:158-161). The only real difference is that the agents.ts versions resolve and pass userBoardIds while the leaderTools versions call the same agentManager methods without the third argument — a scoping difference that is invisible unless you diff the files, and that may or may not be intentional.
confirmed.impact: ~40 duplicated lines collapse to ~25 shared; the leader-vs-user scoping difference becomes explicit; the duplicated project-filter snippet exists once
confirmed.guidance: 1) The scoping difference IS intentional, not an accident: getters.ts:31 ("Fallback: no board info means show all (admin-like)") plus docs/API_REFERENCE.md documenting /api/leader-tools as swarm-leader endpoints. Frame the refactor as documenting this, and write a comment at the leaderTools mount saying the unscoped variant is deliberate (leader sees the whole swarm). 2) Express is 4.21 (api/package.json:22): the sketch makes every handler async, but leaderTools handlers are currently synchronous — a sync throw there reaches Express's error middleware today, while a rejection inside an async handler in Express 4 does not. For strict preservation, have the factory return a plain sync handler when scoped=false (only the scoped variant needs await). 3) getUserBoardIds (agents.ts:94-101) is closure-local and used by ~10 other routes in agents.ts (129, 152, 159, 165, 361, 375, 565, 693, 701, 709, 718); move it to the shared module once and import it back into agents.ts, preserving its catch-returns-empty-Set semantics — do not fork a second copy named getUserBoardIdSet as the sketch implies. 4) The unscoped variant must not call getUserBoardIds at all — passing a real Set would board-scope leader-tools and break swarm-wide visibility (getters.ts:30). 5) Keep route registration order in agents.ts unchanged: /statuses, /by-project/:project, /project-summary, /swarm-status are registered before /:id/status (line 181) and /:id (line 188); extract handlers only, keep mounts in place. 6) Path names differ (/statuses vs /all-statuses) — keep them at the mount points; do not unify URLs. 7) No tests cover any of these eight routes, so smoke-test both route families manually after the change (one scoped user with limited boards, one leader call).

confirmed.title: Delete unreachable tool stubs and unused result flags in executeTool
confirmed.description: executeTool (api/src/services/agentTools.ts:182-208) opens with 8 early-return stubs for report_error, update_task, move_task_to_board, delete_task, list_boards, list_tasks, list_my_tasks, check_status, each returning a flag object (isTaskUpdate, isMoveTaskToBoard, isDeleteTask, isListBoards, isListTasks, isTaskList, isStatusCheck). These branches are unreachable: the only caller is _processToolCalls (api/src/services/agentManager/tools.ts:1263), and every one of those tools is handled inline with `continue` before executeTool is ever reached (tools.ts:490-953). Grep confirms the flags are produced at agentTools.ts:189-207 and consumed nowhere in api/src. They actively mislead readers into thinking executeTool participates in task/board handling. Also dead: the `options = {}` parameter of executeTool is never referenced in its body, and at tools.ts:1261-1262 `const llmConfig = this.resolveLlmConfig(agent)` and `const toolOptions: any = {}` are computed and never meaningfully used (toolOptions is always empty).
confirmed.sketch: 1) In agentTools.ts, delete lines 183-208 (keep only the provider/agentId guard that follows). 2) Drop the `options` parameter from executeTool's signature. 3) In tools.ts:1261-1263, delete the unused `llmConfig` and `toolOptions` locals and call `executeTool(call.tool, call.args, agent.project, this.executionManager, agentId)`. Run api tsc + tests to confirm nothing references the flags.
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines removed; executeTool's real contract (file/shell tools only) becomes obvious; removes 7 phantom flags from the mental model.
confirmed.guidance: Two precision notes for the implementer. (a) The sketch's "delete lines 183-208" includes the report_error stub at agentTools.ts:184-187 whose isErrorReport flag IS live elsewhere: chat.ts:1138/1149-1150/1174 consume it, produced by the inline handler at tools.ts:535-541. Deleting the unreachable stub is safe, but do NOT touch isErrorReport in tools.ts or chat.ts. (b) Removing the `options` parameter and the 6th argument at tools.ts:1263 must land in the same commit or tsc fails on arity. Optionally also delete the now-stale comment at agentTools.ts:183 ("report_error and update_task don't need sandbox access") — it is inside the cited range but worth calling out explicitly. handoff.test.ts auto-mocks the whole module, so no test changes are needed.

confirmed.title: Extract shared runtime-teardown helper from reloadContext/restartRuntime (and fix dead ternary)
confirmed.description: reloadContext (conversation.ts:62-147) and restartRuntime (conversation.ts:171-242) contain a byte-for-byte copy of the same 4-step concurrent teardown block (refreshLlm / disconnectMcp / refreshTree / closeCli, ~45 lines each at 96-136 and 189-231), differing only in the log prefix ('[ReloadContext]' vs '[Restart]'). The shared prologue (stopAgent, _activeStreams.delete, _chatLocks.delete, retry-flag deletes) is also duplicated (66-77 vs 175-185). Bonus dead code: restartRuntime's addActionLog ternary at lines 233-239 has identical strings in both branches ('Agent restarted — runtime reset, conversation preserved'), so the `terminalClosed` distinction is silently lost — clearly a copy-paste slip.
confirmed.impact: ~80 lines removed from conversation.ts (335 → ~255); the actual semantic difference between the two operations (wipe history vs preserve it) becomes visible at a glance instead of buried in 70 identical lines.
confirmed.guidance: Two corrections to the sketch. (1) Helper boundary: do NOT fuse the prologue and the Promise.all block into one _teardownRuntime — reloadContext awaits clearHistory between them (conversation.ts:80), and reordering clearHistory before/after the network teardown changes task-signal timing (setTaskSignal 'stopped' to watching loops), the agent:updated emit point, and the in-flight-stream abort ordering. Instead extract only the four-step Promise.all block (96-136 / 189-231) as e.g. _teardownRuntimeConnections(this, agentId, agent, logTag) → Promise<boolean /* terminalClosed */>, parameterized by logTag ('ReloadContext' / 'Restart') so every console.warn string stays byte-identical; keep terminalClosed as the return value instead of closure mutation. The 12-line prologue can be a second small helper or stay inline — it is contiguous and identical in both, so extracting it separately is safe; fusing it with the teardown is not. (2) The dead ternary at 236-238: for strict behavior preservation, collapse it to the single existing string 'Agent restarted — runtime reset, conversation preserved' (pure dead-code removal). Restoring a '... and CLI restarted' variant when terminalClosed=true is a user-visible action-log change — nothing tests or string-matches it (verified), so it is safe, but ship it as an explicitly flagged one-line behavior fix, not silently inside the refactor.

confirmed.title: Single source of truth for CLI runner IDs (4 duplicated Sets, 2 duplicated isCliRunner)
confirmed.description: The list of CLI runners is a stringly-typed set re-declared in four places: chat.ts:935 (CLI_RUNNERS inside _streamAndContinue), tasks.ts:8 (CLI_RUNNERS module const), actionExecutor.ts:19 (CLI_RUNNERS), terminal.ts:36 (TERMINAL_RUNNERS — same 6 ids). tasks.ts:10-12 and actionExecutor.ts:29-31 also duplicate the identical isCliRunner(agent) helper. tasks.ts:1252 adds SELF_COMPLETING_RUNNERS = the same list minus 'claudecode'. routes/agents.ts:77 hand-maintains the same ids in a z.enum, and executionManager.ts:14-17 in a union type. Adding a runner today requires touching 6+ files and it's easy to miss one (the sets already disagree in element order, hinting at copy-paste drift).
confirmed.impact: ~25 lines removed, 6 files converge on one constant; eliminates a whole class of 'new runner works in chat but not in task loop' drift bugs.
confirmed.guidance: Traps to respect: (1) The Sets are duplicated but the membership checks are NOT uniform — chat.ts:936 (CLI_RUNNERS.has(agent.runner), raw/case-sensitive), terminal.ts:239-240 (String(agent.runner||''), no lowercase), and tasks.ts:1253 (raw executor.runner) differ from the lowercasing isCliRunner in tasks.ts:10-12/actionExecutor.ts:29-31. Replace only the Set data and unify only the two identical isCliRunner copies; do NOT route the raw-check sites through the lowercasing helper — that would change behavior for non-canonical-case runner values from legacy DB rows. (2) zod is ^4.3.6; derive the agents.ts:77 enum as one enum including 'sandbox': z.enum(['sandbox', ...CLI_RUNNER_IDS] as const) — the 'as const' is required for literal inference, and the changed option order only alters zod's 400 error message text (no test depends on it). (3) terminal.ts:36 TERMINAL_RUNNERS has a distinct comment/intent ('only these runners get a terminal'); the sets are identical and CLI runner ≡ has-PTY in this architecture, so fusing is correct, but an alias export (TERMINAL_RUNNERS = CLI_RUNNERS) preserves the call-site name at zero cost if desired. (4) executionManager.ts:14-17 derivation is optional and type-only; _normalizeProviderType must keep the same 7-value membership and 'sandbox' fallback. (5) Sketch nit: 'z.enum(CLI_RUNNER_IDS).optional() plus sandbox' must be a single enum containing 'sandbox', not a union of two schemas.

confirmed.title: Collapse five copy-pasted updateTaskX methods into one field-edit helper
confirmed.description: updateTaskTitle (tasks.ts:253-265), updateTaskText (267-279), updateTaskRepo (281-294), updateTaskStorage (296-309) and updateTaskType (311-323) are five near-identical methods: get agent → find task → capture oldValue → assign → push {type:'edit', field, oldValue, newValue} history entry → saveTaskToDb → emit agent:updated. Only the field name and (for repo/storage) a paired provider default differ. 70 lines of boilerplate where the only signal is 5 field names.
confirmed.impact: tasks.ts loses ~45 lines; the history-entry convention lives in exactly one place, so the next field (there will be one) is a 2-line addition.
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is sound; three fidelity fixes: (1) Use `const oldValue = task[field] || null` in the helper, not `?? null` — four of five originals coerce with `||` (title:258, repo:286, storage:301, type:316); updateTaskText:272 captures task.text uncoerced, but routes/agents.ts:495 rejects empty text so the divergence is unreachable. The `?? null` in the sketch would record '' instead of null in history oldValue if a field were ever empty-string (also unreachable in practice since assignments coerce, but use || for strict identity). (2) The updateTaskType wrapper must pass `taskType || null` as the value AND thread its `by` param (actionExecutor.ts:653 passes agent.name as by); the title/text wrappers pass the raw param uncoerced (agents.ts:492 already sends `title.trim() || null`). (3) Do NOT add updatedAt stamping or a task:updated emit to the helper — these five methods emit only agent:updated, unlike setTaskStatus (tasks.ts:181-251); copying that pattern in would change frontend-observable behavior. Keep applyExtra running after the field assignment and before the history push (matches original ordering; provider fallback reads of task.repoProvider/storageProvider are unaffected since the edited field differs). Keep returning null for missing agent and missing task — workflow.test.ts:691-696 asserts both.

confirmed.title: Replace six hand-rolled cross-agent task scans with the existing _findTaskAcross helper
confirmed.description: index.ts:483-489 already provides _findTaskAcross(predicate) returning { task, agentId }, but the same manual `for (const [creatorId, tasks] of this._tasks) { const found = tasks.find(t => t.id === taskId || t.id.startsWith(taskId)); ... }` loop is re-implemented inline at: tools.ts:224-233 (applyTaskExecutionComplete explicit lookup), tools.ts:551-560 (update_task), tools.ts:673-677 (move_task_to_board), tools.ts:727-731 (delete_task), tasks.ts:476-479 (addTaskCommit), tasks.ts:507-510 (removeTaskCommit). tools.ts:301-305 additionally rediscovers the owner with `(tasks as any[]).includes(inProgressTask)` even though the lookup that found the task could have returned the owner. Every copy re-states the exact/prefix matching rule, and they subtly disagree (some accept prefixes, some don't).
confirmed.impact: ~45 lines removed across tools.ts/tasks.ts; one canonical answer to 'how do we resolve a task id (or prefix) to its owner'.
confirmed.guidance: Two corrections to the sketch: (1) update_task — keep the own-agent priority lookup at tools.ts:548-549 and replace only the cross-agent fallback loop at 551-560 with the helper; replacing the whole lookup changes prefix disambiguation from "calling agent's task wins" to "Map-insertion-order wins". (2) Deleting the includes() re-scan at tools.ts:301-305 requires capturing the owner in ALL four branches that set inProgressTask, not just the explicit-taskId branch: convert auto-detect Priority 1 (tools.ts:237-240) and Priority 2 (244-247) to _findTaskAcross(...) (they are already exactly that shape, and it returns { task, agentId }), and use agentId itself as owner for Priority 3 (251). ownerAgentId feeds saveTaskToDb({ ...task, agentId: ownerAgentId }) and the task:updated emit at tools.ts:389-409 — a wrong owner persists the task under the wrong agent_id in the DB. If doing the branches together feels too invasive, keep the includes() re-scan and only dedup the lookups. Also: document in _findTaskByIdOrPrefix's comment that its global exact-pass-then-prefix-pass equals the inline per-agent exact-or-prefix scans only because task ids are full uuidv4 (tasks.ts:114) — never a strict prefix of one another. In addTaskCommit/removeTaskCommit the agentId parameter becomes a dead default after the change (the ownerAgentId = agentId init is unreachable: not-found returns null); keep the signatures unchanged since callers pass it.

confirmed.title: Extract _markTaskStopped from stopAgent's three near-identical stop blocks
confirmed.description: stopAgent (status.ts:235-358) contains three consecutive scan loops that each end with the same 12-line ritual: set executionStatus='stopped', null startedAt, push a {type:'stopped', by:'user'} history entry, saveTaskToDb, setTaskSignal(id,'stopped',true), emit task:updated — at lines 267-285 (agent's own active tasks), 289-319 (tasks whose actionRunningAgentId is this agent), and 326-346 (tasks owned elsewhere but assigned to this agent). The duplication hides the one real difference per loop (which tasks qualify) under 3x of identical mutation code, and the blocks have already drifted (only block 3 guards on executionStatus !== 'stopped').
confirmed.impact: status.ts stopAgent drops from ~120 to ~75 lines; the three stop-eligibility rules become the visible content instead of the mutation boilerplate.
confirmed.guidance: The sketched helper must be narrowed — as written it changes behavior in four ways. (1) SERIOUS: block 2 (status.ts:301) mutates only when _isActiveTaskStatus(t.status); the sketch's executionStatus!=='stopped' guard would mark non-active tasks (e.g. status 'done' with a stale actionRunning flag) as stopped, null their startedAt, and persist — corrupting completed-task records. (2) Only block 3 (line 331) has the executionStatus!=='stopped' idempotency guard; adding it to blocks 1/2 changes persisted state on double-stop (currently a duplicate 'stopped' history entry is pushed and saved). (3) Block 2 (line 316) emits { agentId: creatorAgent.id, task: t } — the raw live task with no agentId injected — while blocks 1/3 emit { ...t, agentId } copies; the uniform emit changes block 2's socket payload shape. (4) In block 1, setTaskSignal+emit sit inside the active-status guard; in blocks 2/3 they fire for all loop-matched tasks — a helper containing them cannot reproduce all placements. CORRECTED SKETCH: extract only the mutation ritual — _markTaskStopped(this: any, t: any, ownerAgentId: string, stopTimestamp: string) { t.executionStatus='stopped'; t.startedAt=null; if (!t.history) t.history=[]; t.history.push({ status: t.status, at: stopTimestamp, by: 'user', type: 'stopped' }); saveTaskToDb({ ...t, agentId: ownerAgentId }); } — and at each call site keep the existing guard, setTaskSignal, and emit exactly as-is: block 1 calls it inside the _isActiveTaskStatus check; block 2 keeps its flag-clearing and calls it under its own _isActiveTaskStatus check with unconditional signal + the existing raw-t emit; block 3 keeps its predicates and calls it under its executionStatus!=='stopped' guard. Net savings is ~20 lines, not the claimed 45, but the single-source history-entry shape and visible per-block eligibility rules are the real value. Do not normalize the guards or emit payloads in the same change; if the owner wants idempotency guards in blocks 1/2 (probably a fix), do it as a separate, explicitly behavior-changing commit.

confirmed.title: Deduplicate the 50-line stream-consumption loop in _streamAndContinue
confirmed.description: _streamAndContinue (chat.ts:929-1108) runs the provider stream twice with a copy-pasted for-await body: initial pass at chat.ts:981-1034 and the auto-continuation pass at 1051-1098. Both handle thinking-chunk buffering + emit, text accumulation + streamCallback, and an identical 'done' block (usage accounting, _recordUsage vs _recordUsageDirect choice, runnerSessionId persistence, finishReason capture) — ~50 lines each, differing only in the context-tokens variable and a log suffix. Any fix to chunk handling (e.g. the recent cost-reporting change) must be applied twice and has already produced slightly divergent runnerSessionId guards (1026-1029 vs 1091-1093).
confirmed.impact: ~50 lines removed from chat.ts; continuation logic reads as a 10-line loop instead of a second wall of chunk plumbing; future stream-event changes are single-site.
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is sound; respect these traps: (1) Shared accumulation — thinkingBuffer, fullResponse, totalOutputTokens accumulate ACROSS both passes, and chat.ts:1044 pushes the CUMULATIVE fullResponse as the continuation assistant message; have the helper return per-call { text, thinking, finishReason, outputTokens } deltas and concatenate in the caller (or use the sketched thinkingRef), keeping the cumulative variable in _streamAndContinue. (2) runnerSessionId guard — pass the session id actually sent in that call's streamOpts (initialRunnerSessionId for pass 1, agent.runnerSessions?.[sessionKey] read at call time for continuations) and compare chunk.runnerSessionId against that parameter; both existing guards reduce to this. (3) Two log-only divergences will change if blindly unified: the no-usage console.warn (chat.ts:1023-1024) and the 🔑 session log (1028) exist only in the first pass — either gate them on an isContinuation/logSuffix param to preserve byte-identical logs, or accept the extra continuation logs as deliberate (no test asserts them). (4) Keep messages.push (1044-1045) and the two messages.pop() (1099-1100) in the caller so abort-throw skips the pops exactly as today. (5) streamOpts are identical except maxTokens and runnerSessionId — parameterize only those two; temperature/contextLength/isReasoning/signal/taskId stay shared. (6) Incidental: the declared _streamAndContinue return type at api/src/services/agentManager/index.ts:80 is already stale (missing outputTokens/durationMs); fixing it is optional and out of scope — do not let the refactor widen into typing cleanup.

confirmed.title: Split the 960-line _processToolCalls if-chain into a per-tool handler table
confirmed.description: _processToolCalls (tools.ts:424-1383) is a single function dispatching ~15 tools via sequential `if (call.tool === 'X') { ...; continue; }` blocks: task_execution_complete (490-519), report_error (522-543), update_task (546-662), move_task_to_board (665-716), delete_task (719-744), list_boards (747-765), list_tasks (768-833), list_projects (836-844), list_my_tasks (847-887), check_status (890-954), search/create/update/delete_skill (957-1080), mcp_call (1083-1210), then the generic executeTool path (1225-1379). Each block is independent (own validation, own results.push, own logging) — exactly the shape a dispatch table clarifies. The file also mixes in two other separable concerns: git commit detection (lines 11-189) and applyTaskExecutionComplete (192-422). Within the blocks there is internal duplication: the 'append **[agent]** detail block + history edit entry' ritual appears twice (tools.ts:283-298 and 633-647), and the board-name resolution loop appears twice (813-819 and 869-878).
confirmed.sketch:
api/src/services/agentManager/tools/
  index.ts            — toolsMethods: env binding (455-485), the dispatch loop, the generic executeTool path + commit auto-link
  commitDetection.ts  — _isGitMutatingCmd/_isGitNoop/_isGitError/_isGitSuccess/_detectCommitHashes (lines 11-189, already free functions)
  completeTask.ts     — applyTaskExecutionComplete (192-422)
  taskHandlers.ts     — update_task, move_task_to_board, delete_task, list_boards, list_tasks, list_my_tasks, check_status, list_projects
  skillHandlers.ts    — search/create/update/delete_skill
  mcpHandler.ts       — mcp_call incl. JSON repair + plugin gate

type HandlerCtx = { mgr: any; agent: any; agentId: string; call: any; streamCallback: any; dedup: Record<string, boolean> };
type ToolHandler = (ctx: HandlerCtx) => Promise<any /* result to push */ | null /* skipped */>;
const HANDLERS: Record<string, ToolHandler> = { update_task: handleUpdateTask, ... };

Loop body: `const h = HANDLERS[call.tool]; if (h) { const r = await h(ctx); if (r) results.push(r); continue; } /* generic path */`. While moving, extract appendTaskNote(task, agentName, text) for the two detail-block copies and resolveBoardNames(tasks) for the two name-loop copies.
confirmed.impact: tools.ts 1385 lines → ~6 files of 100-350 lines; the dispatch loop becomes ~60 readable lines; ~30 further lines removed via the two intra-file dedups; each tool handler becomes individually testable.
confirmed.guidance: Corrections and traps to respect: (1) Do NOT register list_dir in the handler table — tools.ts:1213-1223 is a cache pre-check that FALLS THROUGH to the generic executeTool path on cache miss; the sketch's "null = skipped → continue" convention would make a list_dir handler silently never execute the tool. Keep the pre-check inline in the generic path, along with the post-execution cache write (1317-1320). (2) The two "detail block" copies are not identical: the applyTaskExecutionComplete copy (283-298) also sets task.updatedAt; the update_task copy (633-647) deliberately does not because setTaskStatus (648) follows. appendTaskNote must parameterize or omit updatedAt, leaving it to callers. (3) The two board-name loops are not identical: list_my_tasks (871-877) try/catches per board with fallback to boardId; list_tasks (814-819) has no try/catch — a getBoardById throw there fails the whole list_tasks call via the outer catch (829-831). resolveBoardNames must preserve this difference (e.g. a swallowErrors flag) or skip deduping this pair. (4) applyTaskExecutionComplete is called externally as agentManager.applyTaskExecutionComplete (swarmApiMcp.ts:636) and mocked in swarmApiMcp.test.ts — after moving to completeTask.ts it must still be spread into toolsMethods so it stays on the prototype with `this` semantics intact. (5) check_status's dynamic `await import('./index.js')` (tools.ts:904) avoids the module cycle with agentManager/index.ts; in tools/taskHandlers.ts keep it dynamic and fix the path to '../index.js'.
