(6) Preserve result envelopes verbatim — list_boards/list_my_tasks/check_status/list_projects push `args: []` while others push `args: call.args`, and field names vary (isTerminal, isErrorReport, isErrorReport only on report_error) — handlers must return their own complete result objects, never a normalized wrapper. (7) The per-response dedup flags (431-433) become a per-invocation ctx.dedup created once before the loop; keep the set-flag-before-await ordering (e.g. 491-495). (8) Since _processToolCalls has zero direct test coverage, the only gate is tsc — do the extraction as pure copy-move, no opportunistic cleanups in the same change.

confirmed.title: Extract chat lock/abort/status cleanup helpers in sendMessage; rename misleading toolsExecuted
confirmed.description: sendMessage (chat.ts:22-372) repeats the same failure ritual four times — abortControllers.delete, metrics.errors += 1, currentThinking = '' + agent:thinking emit, setStatus('error'), saveAgent, _chatLocks.delete(if top-level), throw — at chat.ts:172-179 (pre-stream catch), 311-319 (compaction retry failure), 345-355 (stream retry failure, with an idle-vs-error twist), and 363-370 (final catch). The success ritual (setStatus('idle') + abortControllers.delete + chatLocks.delete) similarly appears at 256-258, 262-264, 286-288 and again in _processPostResponseActions at 1280-1282. Forgetting one line of this ritual is exactly the 'agent stuck busy with lock held' bug class the comments warn about. Separately, `toolsExecuted` (declared chat.ts:183) is set to true unconditionally at line 253 BEFORE any tool has run — it actually means 'post-processing started'; the retry guard `!toolsExecuted` (line 331) and the log 'tools already executed' (line 358) read as if it tracked real tool execution, which misleads anyone debugging the retry path.
confirmed.impact: ~35 lines removed; the four-way retry/error branching in sendMessage's catch becomes scannable; eliminates the most likely source of future 'lock leaked on one exit path' regressions.
confirmed.guidance: 1) REJECT the sketch's alternative for toolsExecuted ('set it inside _processPostResponseActions only after toolResults.length > 0') — that changes behavior. If post-processing throws with zero tools executed, the current !toolsExecuted guard (line 331) blocks a stream retry; moving the flag would enable a retry whose conversationHistory.pop() at line 338 removes the already-persisted ASSISTANT entry (pushed line 197, saved line 250) instead of the user message, then re-sends and duplicates the turn. Rename only (e.g. postProcessingStarted or pastSafeRetryPoint), keep the assignment exactly at line 253; update the console.log string at 358 but leave the user-facing addActionLog string at 359 untouched. 2) The two rituals have OPPOSITE internal order — error path deletes the abortController BEFORE setStatus, success path calls setStatus BEFORE the delete. The sketch preserves this per-helper; do not merge them or normalize the order. 3) _failChat typed ': never' gives no TS reachability narrowing through `this: any`; prefer a cleanup-only helper with explicit `throw err`/`throw retryErr` kept at each of the four call sites (also keeps the catch-block control flow visible). 4) saveAgent is async and deliberately NOT awaited in the error blocks (it IS awaited at line 163) — the helper must not add await. 5) Keep the delete-flag lines (delete agent._compactionRetried at 310, delete agent._streamRetryCount at 345/361) OUTSIDE the helper. 6) The double lock-delete on the compaction path (298 then 318) and double release on the rate-limit path (1280-1282 then 286-288) are intentional/idempotent — preserve them. 7) _releaseChat's `status: null` option is only needed if you also use it for the agent-not-found exit at lines 44-47 (which has no setStatus); otherwise drop the param and leave 44-47 as-is. Risk is low-to-medium with these constraints; gates to run: api tsc + jest (tests mock sendMessage, so only the tsc gate exercises the new helpers' types).

confirmed.title: Share prompt-section builders between _buildSystemPrompt and buildRunnerInstructions
confirmed.description: buildRunnerInstructions (chat.ts:637-739) is documented as the 'no chat protocol' variant of _buildSystemPrompt (chat.ts:378-621), and several sections are verbatim or near-verbatim copies: the available-agents roster mapping (383-390 ≡ 648-655, byte-identical 8 lines), RAG document rendering (419-424 vs 672-677), Active Plugins rendering (444-453 vs 683-690), Agent Credentials block (456-463 vs 693-700), and the Relevant Tasks render incl. TASK_TEXT_MAX_CHARS truncation + overflow hint (568-583 vs 703-728). The recency comparator `new Date(b.updatedAt || b.createdAt || 0) - new Date(a.updatedAt || ...)` is also pasted three times (534-538, 561-565, 707-712). When one prompt gets a fix (as happened repeatedly during the reliability campaign per the inline comments), the sibling silently diverges.
confirmed.sketch:
New api/src/services/agentManager/promptSections.ts with pure functions:
  identityHeader(agent, todayStr): string
  agentRosterLines(agents: any[], excludeId: string): string[]   // the 383-390 mapping
  ragDocsSection(docs: any[]): string
  pluginsSection(resolvedPlugins: any[]): string
  credentialsSection(credentials: Record<string,string>): string
  relevantTasksSection(ranked: any[], totalActive: number, isActive: (s: string) => boolean, overflowHint: string): string
  byRecency(a: any, b: any): number

_buildSystemPrompt and buildRunnerInstructions keep their distinct structure/wording for protocol-specific parts (tool definitions, @-syntax, MCP listing) and compose the shared sections. Snapshot-test both outputs before/after to guarantee byte-identical prompts.
confirmed.impact: ~80 lines removed from chat.ts (1292 → ~1210); the genuine deltas between chat-protocol and CLI-runner prompts become explicit instead of diffable-only; future prompt fixes land in both surfaces by construction.
confirmed.guidance: Three corrections to the sketch for strict behavior preservation: (1) RAG label already diverged — chat.ts:421 uses `doc.type === 'url' ?` while runner 674 uses `doc.type === 'url' && doc.url ?`; a url-doc missing `url` renders `name (source: undefined)` in chat but `name` in runner. The sketched `ragDocsSection(docs)` cannot produce both — parameterize the label rule (e.g. a requireUrl flag) rather than silently unifying. Keep the URL-refresh loop (405-418) and the `agent.ragDocuments.length > 0` vs `Array.isArray(...)` guards in the callers. (2) Active Plugins: chat collects plugin.mcpServerIds into pluginMcpIds inside the render loop (448-450, consumed at 479) and resolves allSkillIds (agent+board, 427-443) while runner resolves only agentSkills — pluginsSection must take pre-resolved plugins and the chat caller must keep mcp-id collection as a separate pass. (3) Relevant Tasks: only the render loop and byRecency comparator are shared; ranking stays caller-side (chat's semantic+recency 3-branch at 531-566 vs runner's deliberate recency-only at 702-713, with its 'no embedding pass here' comment). Also note guard asymmetries stay in callers: roster gated by isLeader && delegationDepth===0 (382) vs isLeader only (647); _listAvailableProjects un-caught in chat (398) vs try/caught in runner (662-665). RECENT_TASKS_LIMIT/TASK_TEXT_MAX_CHARS (513-514, 703-704) can move to promptSections.ts as exported consts. Snapshot-test both prompt outputs before/after as sketched.

confirmed.title: Unify the five parallel tool-name lists in agentTools.ts into one TOOL_SPECS registry
confirmed.description: parseToolCalls maintains tool metadata across five parallel structures that must be kept in sync by hand: KNOWN_TOOLS (agentTools.ts:506, gates <tool_call> JSON parsing), SINGLE_ARG_TOOLS / READ_FILE_TOOLS / MULTI_ARG_TOOLS / THREE_ARG_TOOLS (664-667, drive @-syntax arg splitting), and the 60-line jsonToToolCall switch (509-569, maps JSON arg aliases to positional args). They have already diverged: 'list_projects' is in SINGLE_ARG_TOOLS (line 664) but missing from KNOWN_TOOLS and jsonToToolCall, so a JSON-format list_projects call is silently dropped while the @-syntax form works. The THREE_ARG splitting code (711-739) also re-implements the same top-level-comma walk as the READ_FILE branch (693-710).
confirmed.impact: ~70 lines removed (775 → ~705); adding a tool becomes a one-entry change instead of editing 4-5 lists; fixes the live list_projects JSON-parsing gap.
confirmed.guidance: 1) FALSE PREMISE in verification plan: there are NO existing parseToolCalls tests (checked api/tests/ and api/src/services/__tests__/ — zero references). Write characterization tests against the current implementation BEFORE refactoring; cover at minimum: 3-arg read_file (third arg IS sanitized, line 703), mcp_call 3-arg (third arg is RAW JSON, NOT sanitized, line 722), mcp_call 2-arg padding to '{}' (724), mcp_call single-JSON-object fallback (726-736), MULTI_ARG triple-quote stripping with unsanitized raw second arg (745-748), the unbalanced-paren last-paren-before-newline fallback (678-685), and dedup on tool+args[0] (754-756). 2) BEHAVIOR TRAPS the sketch glosses over: sanitizeArg application differs per branch — a uniform 'split then sanitize all parts' helper would corrupt mcp_call/update_task/task_execution_complete third args; arg-array SHAPES differ (read_file 2-arg yields 2 elements, three-arg tools always yield 3); the JSON-object fallback exists only for THREE_ARG tools. splitTopLevel should return raw trimmed parts; keep sanitization and defaults in per-arity code. 3) list_projects fix is a behavior CHANGE, not preservation: deriving KNOWN_TOOLS from Object.keys(TOOL_SPECS) silently widens the phase-1 gate. Either land it as a deliberate, separately-tested step (executor at agentManager/tools.ts:836 ignores args, so fromJson: () => [] is safe), or for strict preservation gate phase-1 on entries having fromJson. 4) Copy the || alias chains verbatim (e.g. args.start_line || args.startLine || '') — do not 'modernize' to ?? ; a numeric 0 start_line currently coerces to '' and that must stay. 5) Non-traps confirmed: regex alternation order from Object.keys is safe (required '\(' plus backtracking; no tool name is a prefix of another), and phase-1 case-sensitive gating vs phase-2 case-insensitive regex is preserved as long as registry keys stay lowercase.

confirmed.title: Remove identity-scan re-derivation of agentId when the id is already in scope
confirmed.description: Two spots in chat.ts re-discover an agent's id by scanning the agents Map for object identity: sendMessage's pruning block at chat.ts:203 — `const agentId = [...this.agents.entries()].find(([, a]) => a === agent)?.[0]` — even though the function's own `id` parameter (line 22) is exactly that value (agent was fetched via this.agents.get(id) at line 43); and _assembleMessages at chat.ts:761 with the same scan, although agent objects carry their own `.id` (loadFromDatabase stores them keyed by agent.id, index.ts:237, and status.ts freely uses agent.id). The subsequent null-checks on agentId (chat.ts:204-207, 766, 776) imply the lookup can fail, which is impossible — a genuine head-scratcher for readers tracing the pruning/scoping logic.
confirmed.sketch: In sendMessage's pruning block (chat.ts:203): replace with `const agentId = id;` (or inline `id` and drop the `agentId ? ... : null` guards). In _assembleMessages (chat.ts:761): `const agentId = agent.id;` and delete the entries() scan; the `if (!activeTask && agentId)` guards simplify to unconditional. No behavior change since the scan always succeeded.
confirmed.impact: ~8 lines removed, 2 misleading 'can this fail?' branches eliminated; the task-scoped history logic in _assembleMessages becomes straightforward to follow.
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as written; two refinements. (1) Use `agent.id` at BOTH sites (rather than `id` at chat.ts:203 and `agent.id` at 761) — same value, but consistent and self-evidently tied to the object being pruned; or simply inline. (2) Behavior-preservation caveat to note in the commit message, not to code around: if an agent is deleted mid-stream (crud.ts:296-305 clears both this.agents and this._tasks during the long await at chat.ts:187), the old scan returned undefined and the guards forced the no-active-task pruning branch, whereas the literal id lets _findTaskAcross (chat.ts:206/767) match a creator-owned task with assignee === deleted-id and take the task-scoped branch. This only changes how a detached dead object's conversationHistory is pruned before saveAgent(agent) at chat.ts:250 (which resurrects the deleted row either way — pre-existing wart), and is arguably more correct; no tests assert on pruning. Guard collapses are exact: chat.ts:204 `agentId ? ... : null` → direct find; chat.ts:205/766 `if (!activeTask && agentId)` → `if (!activeTask)`; chat.ts:776 `if (!isTaskExecution && agentId)` → `if (!isTaskExecution)`; chat.ts:786-789 uses of agentId unchanged.

confirmed.title: Delete globalTaskStore.ts — the entire 399-line module is dead
confirmed.description: globalTaskStore.ts (399 lines: file-backed JSON task store with add/update/delete/restore, getStats analytics, getTimeSeries charts) has exactly one reference in the whole repo: `import { globalTaskStore } from '../services/globalTaskStore.js'` at api/src/routes/agents.ts:3 — and `globalTaskStore.` is never used anywhere in that file (verified via grep: only the import line matches). The system moved to the DB-backed tasks store (api/src/services/database/tasks.ts); this whole module, its `data/global-todos.json` persistence, and its 170 lines of stats/time-series math are vestigial. Anyone reading it wastes real time assuming it is live (it even has careful atomic-write and corrupt-file-backup logic).
confirmed.sketch: 1) Remove the unused import at api/src/routes/agents.ts:3. 2) Delete api/src/services/globalTaskStore.ts. 3) Run api tsc + tests. Optionally note in a commit message that data/global-todos.json is orphaned runtime data.
confirmed.impact: ~400 lines removed, 2 files touched; eliminates an entire phantom subsystem from the mental model
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Two small additions: (1) the strongest safety argument — worth stating in the commit message — is that tsc already elides the unused import, so dist/routes/agents.js has no reference to globalTaskStore and the module never loads at runtime; the deletion cannot change behavior even in error paths. (2) Stale build artifacts (api/dist/services/globalTaskStore.js/.d.ts/.map) will disappear on the next clean build; no manual dist cleanup needed. The orphaned data/global-todos.json runtime file is only ever touched if the module loads, which it does not — leave it alone or mention it in the commit message as the reviewer suggested.

confirmed.title: users.ts: collapse the Google/Microsoft/GitHub copy-paste triple into provider-parameterized helpers
confirmed.description: api/src/services/database/users.ts:100-236 is the same three functions pasted three times: getUserByGoogleId/createGoogleUser/linkGoogleId (100-144), getUserByMicrosoftId/createMicrosoftUser/linkMicrosoftId (146-190), getUserByGitHubId/createGitHubUser/linkGitHubId (192-236). The only difference between the triples is the column name (google_id / microsoft_id / github_id). Any fix (e.g. the 23505 handling, avatar COALESCE) must be applied 3 times today.
confirmed.impact: ~90 lines removed in users.ts (137 → ~50); provider logic exists once
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct; respect these traps: (1) Prefer the wrapper option (keep the 9 old export names as one-line delegations) — both test files stub the barrel via node:test mock.module('../database.js', { namedExports }) which replaces the ENTIRE export surface; if auth.ts is switched to import getUserByProviderId/createProviderUser/linkProviderId, both test files' namedExports blocks MUST gain those names in the same change or auth.ts import fails at test time (authorization.test.ts:31-57, workflow-pipeline.test.ts:~80-95). (2) Preserve exact console.error strings by deriving them from the column name ('Failed to get user by google_id:', 'Failed to link github_id:') — derive as `Failed to get user by ${col}` / `Failed to link ${col}`. (3) Do not name the create param 'email': auth.ts:676 passes a synthesized username (`login@users.noreply.github.com`) for GitHub, and the value lands in users.username — name it 'username'. (4) Preserve the per-function error asymmetry exactly: getUserByProviderId and linkProviderId catch → return null; createProviderUser maps 23505 to 'Username already exists' and rethrows everything else (no console.error in create). (5) The provider column must also be interpolated into the RETURNING lists of create (`RETURNING id, username, role, display_name, ${col}, avatar_url, created_at, updated_at`) and link (`RETURNING id, username, role, display_name, ${col}, avatar_url`) — they differ between create and link; don't unify them. (6) Keep linkProviderId's parameter binding order ($1=userId, $2=externalId, $3=avatarUrl) as in users.ts:134-137.

confirmed.title: Remove unused exports from taskStateMachine.ts and agentSelector.ts
confirmed.description: Grep over api/src (including tests) shows these exports have zero callers outside their own definition + the index.ts re-export: isTerminalStatus (taskStateMachine.ts:52-54, and with it the TERMINAL_STATUSES set at line 14), getFirstColumn (67-69), getOnEnterTransitions (159-164), getConditionalTransitions (169-178) — both subsumed by getMatchingTransitions which is what workflowEngine actually uses — and isAgentBusy (agentSelector.ts:106-109). They are advertised as public API in index.ts (lines 13, 16, 19-20, 36), so every reader assumes external consumers exist and traces them in vain.
confirmed.sketch: Delete the five functions plus the TERMINAL_STATUSES set, and drop the corresponding lines from the export blocks in api/src/services/workflow/index.ts. tsc will confirm nothing references them.
confirmed.impact: ~55 lines removed across 3 files; the state-machine public surface matches what is actually consumed
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as written. Traps to respect: (1) keep INACTIVE_STATUSES (taskStateMachine.ts:15) — only TERMINAL_STATUSES is dead; (2) keep Trigger — Trigger.CONDITION is still used by getWorkflowManagedStatuses (line 201) and workflowEngine, even though Trigger.ON_ENTER may become locally unused after deleting getOnEnterTransitions; (3) do NOT touch the similar-looking refreshLock/touchAgentBusy/hasLockForTask in agentSelector.ts — they ARE consumed by actionExecutor.ts:13 and workflowEngine.ts:32; (4) the _getFirstColumnStatus in agentManager/tasks.ts:360 is an unrelated independent implementation, not a caller — leave it alone. Run api tsc + the 244 tests to confirm.

confirmed.title: One hasIdleAgentWithRole helper instead of three pasted predicates + a dead stub branch
confirmed.description: The predicate `[...agents.values()].some(a => a.status === 'idle' && a.enabled !== false && (!role || a.role === role))` is copy-pasted three times: workflowEngine.ts:142-144, workflowEngine.ts:323-325, and agentManager/workflow.ts:28-30 (inside _evaluateCondition's own special-case for idle_agent_available). Meanwhile evaluateCondition in taskStateMachine.ts:116-121 contains a stub case for the same field that hardcodes fieldValue='false' with the comment "will be overridden by caller if needed" — unreachable through evaluateAllConditions (which intercepts the field at lines 144-147) and misleading for direct callers.
confirmed.impact: 3 copies → 1; removes a misleading dead branch; ~15 lines net
confirmed.guidance: Three corrections to the sketch: (1) Prefer placing the helper in agentSelector.ts, not taskStateMachine.ts — evaluateAllConditions already has a PARAMETER named hasIdleAgentWithRole (taskStateMachine.ts:140, a (role)=>boolean callback) and a module-level export with the same name but different arity (agents, role) would shadow confusingly inside that function. If it must live in taskStateMachine.ts, rename one of them. (2) In agentManager/workflow.ts:24-36, replace ONLY the .some() scan (lines 28-30); keep the neq inversion (line 31) and the console.log on true (line 32) — folding the whole special case into evaluateAllConditions would change logging. (3) When deleting the stub case at taskStateMachine.ts:116-121, note the field then falls through to default fieldValue='' instead of 'false' — behavior-identical for every existing caller (all intercept before reaching it) but flips the result for a hypothetical direct call with operator eq and value 'false'; leave a one-line comment in the switch pointing at evaluateAllConditions so future readers know the field is handled upstream. Also update the JSDoc @param at line 137 if the callback name changes.

confirmed.title: Extract recordReassign() and saveThenEmitTaskUpdated() — the same 12-line ritual appears 5x
confirmed.description: Two rituals are pasted across both workflow files. (a) The reassign history push `{status, at, by: 'workflow', type: 'reassign', assignee}` (with its `if (!history) history = []` guard) appears 4x: actionExecutor.ts:229-236 (executeAssignAgent), 264-271 (executeAssignAgentIndividual), 406-415 (executeRunAgent), workflowEngine.ts:561-568 (_autoAssignByColumn). (b) The deferred-emit dance `const payload = {...actualTask, agentId}; Promise.resolve(saveTaskToDb(...)).catch(() => {}).then(() => _emitTaskUpdated(...))` appears 5x: actionExecutor.ts:238-242, 272-277, 421-425, 476-480 and workflowEngine.ts:571-578 — and the workflowEngine copy re-implements _emitTaskUpdated's assigneeName/assigneeIcon enrichment by hand (572-574 duplicating actionExecutor.ts:61-68). The crucial save-before-emit ordering rationale is re-commented at each site.
confirmed.impact: ~50 lines removed; emit-after-save invariant and assignee enrichment live in exactly one place
confirmed.guidance: 1) BEHAVIOR DELTA TO ACKNOWLEDGE (the description understates this): _emitTaskUpdated also stamps task.updatedAt = new Date().toISOString() (actionExecutor.ts:60), which workflowEngine.ts:571-578 currently does NOT. Using the shared helper there adds the stamp — a deliberate consistency fix (without it the auto-assign emit can lose to stale loadTasks() merges, per the comment at actionExecutor.ts:58-60), but say so in the commit; if strict preservation is demanded, leave the workflowEngine emit as-is and only dedup the history push there. 2) Use `if (!task.history) task.history = []` in recordReassign, not `task.history ??= []` — the sketch's ??= differs for falsy non-nullish values; match the original guard exactly for free. 3) The sketch passes ONE object to both saveTaskToDb and _emitTaskUpdated; the originals make two separate spreads. saveTaskToDb defers serialization through a per-task write queue (api/src/services/database/tasks.ts:106-128), and _emitTaskUpdated mutates the payload (updatedAt, assigneeName/Icon) — the mutation lands after the save settles so it happens to be safe, but keep two spreads like the originals to stay obviously equivalent. 4) Scope guard: do NOT convert actionExecutor.ts:605-607 (emit WITHOUT save is deliberate — the comment explains the race it avoids) nor the catch path at actionExecutor.ts:566-567 (awaited save + raw _emit, different contract), nor the agentManager/tasks.ts copies (153-159, 238-248, 528 — raw this._emit, no enrichment, one is by:'user'). 5) recordReassign reads task.status at call time — call it at exactly the position the push occupied (all 4 sites push before any status mutation, so this is order-preserving).

confirmed.title: actionExecutor: merge _runTitleMode/_runSetTypeMode and extract the streaming sendMessage wrapper
confirmed.description: (a) _runTitleMode (613-639) and _runSetTypeMode (641-667) are byte-for-byte identical except for the prompt builder and the 2-line response post-processing — same maxLen slice, same try/catch with the same isUserStopError rethrow comment, same _saveExecutionLog success/failure pair, same finally agentUpdated. (b) The streaming wrapper — streamStart, accumulate fullResponse, streamChunk+thinking per chunk, workflowMeta, streamEnd+agentUpdated in finally — is pasted three times: _runRefineMode 675-700, _runDecideMode 757-776, _runExecuteMode 857-870/906-909.
confirmed.impact: ~80 lines removed from a 912-line file; the five mode handlers become visibly distinct only in what is actually distinct
confirmed.guidance:
Part (a) is good as sketched, with two constraints: (1) keep apply() inside the shared try — non-stop errors thrown by updateTaskTitle/updateTaskType must remain swallowed (best-effort, still returns { executed: true }), exactly as today; (2) keep the per-mode console messages ("title: generating" / "set_type: classifying", and the success logs) parameterized, not genericized. Note title's _saveExecutionLog(true) fires even when the parsed title is empty (no updateTaskTitle call) — the apply-table shape preserves this.

Part (b) MUST NOT use the sketched return-{result,fullResponse} shape. streamEnd/agentUpdated currently fire in a finally that wraps the per-mode post-processing, not just sendMessage: execute's try (858-909) encloses _saveExecutionLog, the completion checks, and the potentially minutes-long await _waitForExecutionComplete (888) plus its throw/early-return paths (885, 892-904); refine's try (676-700) encloses _saveExecutionLog + updateTaskText; decide's (758-776) encloses _saveExecutionLog. The sketch would emit streamEnd/agentUpdated before all of that — an observable wire-order change, and for execute a visible UI change (frontend STREAM_END at App.tsx:294-316 clears thinkingMap and arms the stream-buffer clear; today the live buffer/thinking persists through the wait loop, during which _waitForExecutionComplete emits nested stream:start/end pairs at tasks.ts:1026/1042-1043 whose final cleanup relies on the outer streamEnd firing last). Instead use a higher-order wrapper that takes the body as a callback so emit ordering is byte-identical: async function _withAgentStream(agentManager, agentId, body) { agentManager.wsEmitter.streamStart(agentId); try { return await body(); } finally { agentManager.wsEmitter.streamEnd(agentId); agentManager.wsEmitter.agentUpdated(agentId); } } — optionally plus a small chunk-collector factory for the fullResponse += chunk / streamChunk / thinking callback and the one-line workflowMeta. Each mode's entire existing try-body moves into body() unchanged.

Do not attempt to unify response extraction across modes: refine reads result?.content || fullResponse (690) while title/set_type treat the sendMessage return as a plain string (622, 650) — the return shape differs by path; leave each caller's extraction as-is.

confirmed.title: actionExecutor: one CLI-terminal runner + one waitResult interpreter instead of three pasted copies
confirmed.description: The CLI-runner branch — bindAgentRunner, sendTerminalInput({submit:true}), _waitForExecutionComplete({terminalDriven:true}), then the waitResult ladder ('error' → consume auth error + throw, 'stopped' → saveExecutionLog(false) + skipped, 'deleted' → skipped, success → saveExecutionLog(true)) — is duplicated between _runDecideMode (737-755) and _runExecuteMode (828-854). The same waitResult ladder appears a third time in the non-CLI execute wait at 888-904. Differences are tiny and easy to lose in the noise: execute throws on 'timeout' (843-845, 896-898) while decide has no timeout branch, and the error strings differ slightly ('Claude Code CLI...' vs 'Executor agent...').
confirmed.impact: ~55 lines removed; the per-mode contract differences (timeout handling) become explicit parameters instead of diff-hunting
confirmed.guidance: Corrections to the sketch: (1) The third copy (non-CLI execute, 888-904) must NOT call _saveExecutionLog(false) on 'stopped' — the log was already saved with success=true at line 872 before the wait. Add a flag (e.g. logOnStop: boolean) to _interpretWaitResult, or leave stopped-logging at the two CLI call sites; the sketch as written introduces a duplicate, contradictory execution-log entry on the non-CLI user-stop path. (2) The sketch's claim that error strings differ between decide and execute CLI branches is wrong — lines 746 and 841 use the identical 'Claude Code CLI ended in an authentication or runtime error' string; only the non-CLI copy (894) uses 'Executor agent ended in error before completing the task'. errorLabel still covers this, just pass the same label from both CLI sites. Behavior-preservation traps: (a) the decide handler must NOT return {executed:true} after the success log — it falls through to the decision-verification block at 780-811 (before/after status+textLen comparison and _decideNoDecisionCount logic); (b) decide's 'timeout' currently falls through to _saveExecutionLog(true,'decide') then the decided check — throwOnTimeout=false must return null so the caller still logs success; (c) in the non-CLI path the interpreter call must remain inside the existing try block so the finally (906-909, streamEnd + agentUpdated) still runs when it throws; (d) the success path for the non-CLI site logs nothing after the wait (already logged at 872) — the post-interpret _saveExecutionLog(true, mode) line applies only to the two CLI sites; (e) preserve the optional chaining on agentManager._consumeTaskAuthError?.(). Scope limit: do NOT fold in the fourth ladder at api/src/services/agentManager/tasks.ts:1262-1276 — it has a different contract (returns waitResult to its caller, computes log success as waitResult !== 'error' && !== 'timeout', calls _consumeTaskAuthError without ?.). The trivial console.log wording differences between the two CLI injection sites ('injecting prompt' vs 'injecting task prompt') should either stay at the call sites or be passed as a label.

confirmed.title: Unify the completedActionIdx / _completedActionIdx twin fields into one
confirmed.description: The chain-resume index lives under two names that are always written, cleared, and read as a pair: workflowEngine.ts:471-472 and 483-484 (`actualTask._completedActionIdx = i; actualTask.completedActionIdx = i;`), 525-527 (delete one, null the other), actionExecutor.ts:322-324, agentManager/tasks.ts:195-196, agentManager/conversation.ts:30-31, routes/tasks.ts:349-350 and 461-462. Every read needs the coalescing dance `task.completedActionIdx ?? task._completedActionIdx` (workflowEngine.ts:279, 417). The underscore twin is a leftover from when the field was in-memory-only; since rowToTask (database/tasks.ts:50) and saveTaskToDb both handle `completedActionIdx`, the second name only adds a 'why are there two?' question at six call sites.
confirmed.sketch: Keep only `completedActionIdx` (the persisted name). Mechanical changes: (1) each paired write becomes `task.completedActionIdx = i;`; (2) each paired clear becomes `task.completedActionIdx = null;`; (3) reads drop the `?? task._completedActionIdx` fallback; (4) the startup re-arm at workflowEngine.ts:279 and the resume gate at 417 read the single field; (5) update workflow.test.ts §7 (lines 416-500, 800) which exercises both names. Resume-on-restart behavior is unchanged because the persisted column is already the source of truth after a reboot.
confirmed.impact: ~25 lines net, 6 src files + 1 test file; eliminates the most confusing parallel structure in the workflow engine
confirmed.guidance: One correction to the proposal's premise and one behavior note for the implementer. (1) The claim 'always written, cleared, and read as a pair' has exactly one exception: the recurrence reset at agentManager/tasks.ts:765 and 788 nulls ONLY the persisted name and never deletes memTask._completedActionIdx (nor memTask._pendingOnEnter in the mirror block at 782-797). Today, with the `??` coalescing reads, a lingering underscore value survives that reset (`null ?? 1` → 1), and if the lingering _pendingOnEnter matches the reset column, _executeActionChain (workflowEngine.ts:417-419) resumes mid-chain instead of starting at action 0. After unification the reset becomes fully effective — this is a behavior change in that edge case, but it fixes a latent bug consistent with the surrounding code's intent (lines 761-770 reset every other execution marker for a fresh cycle); state this explicitly in the commit message instead of trying to preserve the buggy coalescing. (2) Mechanical details: use `= null` (not delete) as the cleared value to match the existing persisted-clear convention — reads use `??` and `typeof === 'number'`, so null vs undefined is interchangeable, and saveTaskToDb (database/tasks.ts:177) normalizes both; remove the now-dead `delete task._completedActionIdx` lines at conversation.ts:30, routes/tasks.ts:349 and 461, actionExecutor.ts:322, agentManager/tasks.ts:195; collapse the dual typeof check at workflowEngine.ts:525 to the single field. (3) The workflow.test.ts §7 tests (416-500, 800) mostly re-implement the two-field dance inline rather than driving the engine, so rewriting them to one field is safe and trivial; do not weaken the one test (419) that asserts the clear happens before change_status.

confirmed.title: workflowEngine: split 215-line recheckPendingTransitions into named phases and merge the two parallel retry maps
confirmed.description: recheckPendingTransitions (187-401) does five unrelated jobs inline: evict stale condition locks (192-205), sweep retry bookkeeping (212-221), load board transitions (223-249), the one-shot post-restart re-arm (264-285), and the per-agent/per-task/per-transition triple loop (288-400) whose body itself branches into the on_enter-retry path vs the conditional-chain path. On top of that, retry state is two parallel Maps — agentManager._onEnterRetryTimestamps and agentManager._onEnterRetryCounts — keyed by the same string `${agentId}:${task.id}:lastRetry` (the ':lastRetry' suffix is vestigial), forced to be created (345-346), read/written (348-357), swept (212-221), and cleared (487-493) in lockstep.
confirmed.impact: Function bodies become 10-40 lines each with self-describing names; ~20 lines net removed; retry state can no longer half-desync
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is sound; respect these traps: (1) Keep _recheckTask SYNCHRONOUS — the original loop body has no awaits; an awaited async helper inserts microtask boundaries between task iterations, changing interleaving with the fire-and-forget processColumnEntry/_executeActionChain promises (the _processingTasks and _conditionProcessing locks are set synchronously and must stay so). (2) Keep _reArmInterruptedChainsOnce AFTER the empty-transMap early return, as the sketch shows — _startupReArmDone is a one-shot module flag and the original leaves it false when no board has relevant transitions (return at line 249 precedes the re-arm at 264), deferring re-arm to a later tick; hoisting it would consume the one-shot prematurely. (3) _loadRelevantBoardTransitions must preserve the error path of lines 223-229: catch the getAllBoardWorkflows failure, emit the same console.error, and cause the caller to return (returning an empty transMap achieves the identical early-return). (4) When the per-transition `break`s become returns inside _recheckTask, the preceding lock cleanups (`agentManager._conditionProcessing.delete(lockKey)` at 340, 353, 374) must be kept — and a break must end ALL remaining matching transitions for that task, not fall through to the next one. (5) Map-merge key parity: line 347 builds the key from the loop `agentId` while 489/492 use `task.agentId`; these are equal because both chain entry points carry agentId on the task ({ ...task, agentId } spreads at 362 and 384), so keep each site's existing identifier when dropping ':lastRetry'. (6) Preserve the missing-entry defaults: first attempt must read count=0 / lastRetry=0 (lines 348, 351 use `|| 0`), i.e., absent merged entry behaves as { ts: 0, count: 0 }. (7) Keep the lazy-init + existence-guard pattern for the merged map (the sweep at 212 guards on map existence); optionally declare _onEnterRetry on the AgentManager type at api/src/services/agentManager/index.ts:174 next to _conditionProcessing for discoverability — the current maps are undeclared lazy properties.

confirmed.title: actionExecutor: decompose the 260-line executeRunAgent into phase helpers
confirmed.description: executeRunAgent (347-609) handles, in one body: lock acquisition (353-358), agent selection (362-375), the heartbeat timer (381-390), actionRunning flag setup + reassign + save/emit (400-426), the repo auto-switch with its own 30-line inline error path (428-493), the 5-way mode dispatch (498-518), user-stop vs real-error catch handling (521-575), and a 30-line finally cleanup (576-608). The repo-switch catch block (457-491) is the worst offender: it hand-rolls error history + actionRunning cleanup + error emit that closely mirror what markTaskError and the outer chain handler already provide, and one must read 65 lines to learn 'switch the agent to the task repo, fail the action if that fails'.
confirmed.impact: executeRunAgent shrinks to ~110 lines of visible phase structure; the repo-switch contract becomes testable in isolation
confirmed.guidance: 1) Do NOT take the description's 'closely mirrors markTaskError' literally. The switch-error block (462-481) is deliberately DIFFERENT from markTaskError (taskErrors.ts:57-110): it leaves the task in its current column (no status change, no errorFromStatus), pushes a history entry with status=currentStatus (markTaskError pushes status='error', from=prevStatus), and skips _saveExecutionLog. Extract the block as-is inside _ensureAgentOnTaskRepo; substituting markTaskError would move the task to the error column on switch failure — a behavior change. Same for the error-report payload: the switch path uses project: task.project (485) while the outer catch uses agent.project || task.project (547) — keep both as-is. 2) Fix the sketched signatures: _ensureAgentOnTaskRepo also needs mode (historyEntry.actionMode at 474) and task.agentId (save payload + _emitTaskUpdated use agentId: task.agentId, which actualTask does not carry); _markActionRunning likewise needs task.agentId. Pass them via ctx. 3) _startLockHeartbeat must keep the heartbeatStartedAt capture, the LOCK_HEARTBEAT_MAX_MS self-clear, and the (heartbeat as any).unref?.() call, and return the interval so the finally can clearInterval it. 4) Keep the user-stop/error catch (521-575) and the finally (576-608) inline in executeRunAgent — they close over execStartMsgIdx, execStartedAt, actualTask, agent, mode, heartbeat; extracting them is not in scope and would balloon parameter lists. 5) The 'saveThenEmit from the dedup proposal' reference is optional — the extraction works standalone with the existing fire-and-forget Promise chains copied verbatim (preserve the .catch(() => {}).then(emit) order: emit happens even when save fails). 6) No tests cover this path (workflow tests only hand-simulate actionRunning flags), so verify with tsc + full api suite and a careful diff read; consider adding a unit test for _ensureAgentOnTaskRepo's failure contract as part of the change.

confirmed.title: tasks.ts: queryTasks() helper for the 11 identical SELECT wrappers + one field→column map in updateTaskFields
confirmed.description: Eleven functions in api/src/services/database/tasks.ts repeat the exact same shell — getPool guard, pool.query(`${TASK_SELECT} WHERE ... `), rows.map(rowToTask), catch → console.error + fallback: getTasksByAgent (62-75), getAllTasks (77-87), getTaskById (89-100), getDeletedTasks (238-248), getDeletedTaskById (250-261), getActiveTasksByAgent (425-438), getTasksByBoard (443-456), getTasksByAssignee (487-500), getActiveTaskForExecutor (506-524), getRecurringTasks (588-602), getTasksByStatusAndBoard (720-746). Only the WHERE/ORDER clause and params differ. Separately, updateTaskFields (751-800) maintains two parallel structures that must be kept in sync: the `allowed` snake_case column list (754-762) and the `camelToSnake` rename map (764-772) — a field is writable only if its mapped name happens to appear in the other list.
confirmed.impact: ~130 lines removed from an 800-line file; adding a task query becomes a one-liner; updateTaskFields allow-list can no longer drift
confirmed.guidance: Four corrections to make it strictly behavior-preserving: (1) Log prefixes vary ('Failed to load tasks for agent' vs 'Failed to get task' vs 'Failed to get deleted tasks'...); the sketch's `Failed to load ${label}` template would change ~6 of 11 messages — pass the full message prefix per getter instead of interpolating into a fixed template. (2) In updateTaskFields, the sketch's `if (!col) continue` is weaker than the current `allowed.includes(col)` guard: a fields key like 'toString' resolves via prototype chain to a truthy function, which current code skips (not in allowed) but the sketch would inject into SQL, failing the whole UPDATE instead of skipping the key. Use Object.hasOwn(COLUMN_BY_FIELD, key) (or a null-prototype object) for the lookup, and precompute `const COLUMNS = new Set(Object.values(COLUMN_BY_FIELD))` at module level for the snake_case passthrough — which must be kept, since current code accepts already-snake_case keys like 'board_id'. (3) getTaskById/getDeletedTaskById currently map only rows[0]; queryOneTask mapping all rows then taking [0] is result-identical (id is PK) — fine, but don't add LIMIT 1 to the SQL (that would change the query text for no behavioral reason). (4) getTasksByStatusAndBoard builds its conditions inside the existing try; building (array pushes/template strings) cannot throw, so moving it before the helper call is safe. Also preserve the exact `[]`-on-no-pool / null-on-no-pool fallbacks (queryOneTask returning (await queryTasks(...))[0] ?? null already does). Prefer keeping `export async function` declarations over converting to arrow consts — smaller diff, same effect.

confirmed.title: One createJsonDocStore() factory for the four identical JSONB-document CRUD modules
confirmed.description: Four modules implement the same CRUD on an (id, data JSONB, created_at, updated_at) table: skills.ts (getAllSkills/saveSkill/deleteSkillFromDb, 41 lines), mcpServers.ts (same + encryptFields(['apiKey']), 45 lines), llmConfigs.ts (same + getById + decryptFields, 55 lines), agentSkills.ts (getAll/getById/save/delete at 3-13 and 50-87; searchAgentSkills is genuinely unique). The upsert SQL `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET data = $2, updated_at = NOW()`, the getPool guards, and the catch/log shapes are copy-pasted 12+ times with only the table name, ORDER BY, and secret-field list varying.
confirmed.impact: ~120 lines removed across 4 files; one place to fix upsert/error behavior; existing export names preserved so the database.ts barrel and callers are untouched
confirmed.guidance: Four traps to respect for strict behavior preservation: (1) Log labels differ per entity and operation ('Failed to load skills:' vs 'Failed to load MCP servers:' vs 'Failed to get LLM config:'). The sketch omits this — add a label option, e.g. createJsonDocStore('mcp_servers', { label: 'MCP server', labelPlural: 'MCP servers' }), and emit 'Failed to load ${labelPlural}:', 'Failed to save ${label}:', etc., logging err.message (not err) as today. No test asserts these, but the proposal claims behavior preservation, so keep them byte-identical. (2) Delete return unification: remove() returning rowCount>0 changes skills/mcpServers/llmConfigs deletes from undefined to boolean. Verified safe — all 8 call sites discard the return — but make it deliberate, not accidental; do not start branching on it in callers as part of this refactor. (3) agentSkills must pass orderBy: 'updated_at DESC' (others default 'created_at'); getting this wrong silently reorders UI lists. (4) The sketch detaches methods (export const getAllLlmConfigs = store.getAll), so the factory must use closures over (table, opts), never `this`. Minor details that already line up: unify getById as `const d = rows[0]?.data; return d ? dec(d) : null` — matches both llmConfigs (ternary) and agentSkills (|| null) since both coerce falsy to null; crypto.ts encryptFields/decryptFields already accept readonly string[]; skills/mcpServers simply don't export the store's unused getById. Keep searchAgentSkills (agentSkills.ts:15-48) and all of agents.ts out, as sketched.

confirmed.title: One shared Express MCP handler factory replaces 14 copy-pasted createXMcpHandler functions
confirmed.description: Every *Mcp.ts file ends with an identical ~20-line Express handler: POST-method check, read x-agent-id/x-board-id headers, new StreamableHTTPServerTransport({sessionIdGenerator: undefined}), createXMcpServer(...), connect, handleRequest, catch-log-500. 14 literal copies: gmailMcp.ts:1003-1025, outlookMcp.ts:849-870, gdriveMcp.ts:619-639, wordpressMcp.ts:528-548, githubMcp.ts:524-544, jiraMcp.ts:403-423, onedriveMcp.ts:388-410, slackMcp.ts:378-399, s3Mcp.ts:286-307, swarmApiMcp.ts:663-685, codeIndexMcp.ts:333-351, autoLearnMcp.ts:168-186, gandiDnsMcp.ts:147-165, browserMcp.ts:103-121. The only variations are the log label and which header values the server builder consumes. Any fix to this boilerplate (e.g. a header or error-shape change) currently requires 14 edits.
confirmed.impact: ~280 lines of boilerplate collapse to a ~25-line factory plus 14 one-to-three-line wrappers (net -200 lines across 14 files); the request-bridging convention lives in exactly one place
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as designed; respect these traps when implementing: (1) Header values from Express are string | string[]; current code passes the raw value through with || null and NO array normalization. Keep the `as string` cast (or pass raw) — do not "fix" repeated headers with Array.isArray(...)[0], which would change what builders receive. The || null also maps empty-string headers to null today; preserve it. (2) swarmApiMcp.ts:670-672 has a meaningful comment (X-Agent-Id injected for CLI runner agents, absent for external API-key callers) — move it onto the wrapper, and note its builder takes (agentManager, callerAgentId) ignoring boardId; the factory reading x-board-id anyway is side-effect-free and fine. (3) codeIndex/autoLearn/gandi/browser builders take no agent context — their wrappers should just ignore ctx, not grow new params. (4) Keep gmailMcp's JSDoc about runnerBridge (gmailMcp.ts:993-1002) on its wrapper, and keep the runnerBridge: RunnerExecBridge | null = null default on gmail and outlook wrappers so index.ts:226-234 stays untouched. (5) Routes are mounted with app.all (index.ts:220-272), so the 405-for-non-POST branch is live behavior — keep it first, before the try. (6) Error response must remain res.status(500).json({ error: err.message }) guarded by !res.headersSent, logged as `[<Label> MCP] Error:` — labels differ per file ('Gmail', 'Gdrive', 'WordPress', 'Swarm API', 'Code Index', 'Auto Learn', 'Gandi DNS', etc.); copy each file's existing label exactly to keep log greppability. (7) Remove the now-unused StreamableHTTPServerTransport (and any RunnerExecBridge-only-for-handler) imports per file so api tsc stays clean.

confirmed.title: Shared provider-fetch factory for the 7 near-identical {gmail,graph,drive,github,jira,wp}Fetch helpers
confirmed.description: Seven files each define a private fetch helper with the same skeleton: resolve per-agent credentials, prefix base URL unless the path starts with 'http', fetch with AbortSignal.timeout(60_000), spread options then merge Authorization + Content-Type headers, throw `<Provider> API error <status>: <text>` on !res.ok, then parse JSON-or-text by content-type. Copies: gmailFetch (gmailMcp.ts:82-106), graphFetch (outlookMcp.ts:83-114, adds 202/204→null), graphFetch again (onedriveMcp.ts:22-46, 120s timeout), driveFetch (gdriveMcp.ts:22-55, adds raw mode + conditional Content-Type), githubFetch (githubMcp.ts:8-34, extra Accept/User-Agent/Api-Version headers), jiraFetch (jiraMcp.ts:9-35, Basic auth + 204→null + different arg order), wpFetch (wordpressMcp.ts:41-74, Basic auth + creds-missing error + conditional Content-Type). Two of them are even both named graphFetch with different timeouts. Conventions (timeout, error truncation, empty-status handling) have silently drifted between copies.
confirmed.impact: ~210 lines of drifting copies become one ~45-line factory + 7 short configs (net ~-120 lines, 7 files); timeout/error/parse conventions become one decision instead of seven
confirmed.guidance: Corrections to the sketch (each is a behavior-preservation trap if implemented literally): (1) jira/wp base URLs are dynamic per-agent, not static opts.base — jiraFetch builds `https://${creds.domain}` (jiraMcp.ts:13) and wpFetch builds `${creds.siteUrl}/wp-json` with leading-slash normalization (wordpressMcp.ts:50-52); change getAuthHeader to getAuth(agentId, boardId) → { authorization, base } and keep wp's slash handling. (2) jira/wp always res.json() after the 204→null check (jiraMcp.ts:33-34, wordpressMcp.ts:72-73), they do NOT do content-type-based json-or-text like the other five — add a parse: 'json' | 'contentType' option. (3) jira/wp error paths use res.text().catch(() => '') and truncate at slice(0,300) / slice(0,400); the others use untrimmed await res.text() with no catch — preserve via per-provider maxErrorChars (or unify deliberately, but don't call it preservation). (4) outlook's error message is `Microsoft Graph error ${status}` and onedrive's is `Graph API error ${status}` — the `${name} API error` template breaks both; pass the full error label per provider instead. (5) Do not hardcode 'Content-Type': 'application/json' in the factory: githubFetch sends no default Content-Type at all, and gdrive/wp only set it when options.body is a string and the caller didn't provide one (gdriveMcp.ts:35-37, wordpressMcp.ts:62-64 — critical for WP media uploads); the sketch's jsonContentTypeOnlyWithBody flag is declared but never used in the body — actually wire it as a three-way policy (always / onlyStringBody / none). Minor: the `options.raw ? 120_000` timeout is a gdrive-only convention (onedrive needs timeoutMs: 120_000 unconditionally; only gdriveMcp.ts:152,161 pass raw), and jiraFetch/wpFetch keep their (agentId, boardId, path, options) arg order via one-line reordering wrappers. Net effect after corrections: the factory needs ~6 config knobs, slightly less slim than sketched (~60 lines, not 45), but still a clear win over seven drifting copies.

confirmed.title: Extract shared email-attachment module duplicated byte-for-byte between gmailMcp and outlookMcp
confirmed.description: gmailMcp.ts and outlookMcp.ts each carry a ~250-line attachment stack that is identical except for two constants and the final wire shape: RunnerExecBridge type (gmail 14-20 / outlook 20-26), the 40-entry MIME_BY_EXT map + guessMimeType (gmail 33-76 / outlook 35-78), shQuote (gmail 227-230 / outlook 116-118), readAttachmentViaRunner with the probe-then-base64 protocol (gmail 241-298 / outlook 120-168 — only MAX_ATTACHMENT_BYTES 20MB vs 3MB and maxOutput 32MiB vs 8MiB differ), the two-tier resolveAttachment local-fs-then-runner fallback (gmail 312-391 / outlook 184-266), and attachmentsSchema (gmail 513-523 / outlook 268-278). wordpressMcp.ts:12-36 carries a third, smaller MIME_BY_EXT + guessMimeType copy. The security-sensitive probe/fallback logic (cross-agent isolation, truncation detection) is maintained in two places; the Gmail copy already accumulated fixes (e.g. the maxOutput truncation comment at gmail 274-277) that had to be mirrored manually.
confirmed.impact: Removes ~280 duplicated lines (net) across 3 files; the cross-agent file-read security logic and the MIME table get a single owner
confirmed.guidance: Two corrections to the sketch: (1) The description's claim "identical except for two constants and the final wire shape" misses a real asymmetry in resolveAttachment's `content` branch: outlookMcp.ts:256-259 strips whitespace and validates base64 (throwing 'Attachment "X" content is not valid base64.') at resolve time, while gmailMcp.ts:382-391 returns content raw and performs the identical clean+validate later in buildRawEmail (gmailMcp.ts:481-484). The shared resolveAttachmentInput must return the content-branch payload RAW; the outlook adapter does clean+validate, and gmail keeps its existing validation in buildRawEmail. Do not unify validation into the shared helper, or gmail's error timing/origin shifts. (2) Drop wordpressMcp from this refactor (or treat it as a separate, explicitly behavior-changing follow-up): its private map (wordpressMcp.ts:12-32) has only 19 entries vs the shared 40; importing the shared guessMimeType changes the Content-Type sent to WordPress for ~18 extensions (.md, .html, .docx, .xlsx, .ppt, .odt, .gz, .tar, .7z, .ics...) from application/octet-stream to specific types, which can flip WordPress's media-upload accept/reject behavior. Remaining traps to respect: limitLabel must be applied at BOTH size-limit error sites — the runner probe (gmailMcp.ts:267-272 vs outlookMcp.ts:144-149) AND the local-fs tier-1 stat check (gmailMcp.ts:343-347 vs outlookMcp.ts:214-218) — preserving exact strings: gmail ends '...MB limit.' and outlook '...MB inline-attachment limit for Outlook.'. buildAttachmentsSchema's describe strings are wire-visible in the MCP tools/list response: per-field describes are identical between the two files, but outlook's array-level describe appends 'Outlook inline-attachment limit ≈ 3 MB per file.' (outlookMcp.ts:278) — keep via the extraDescription parameter. Keep the probe command and timeouts (10000/120000) byte-identical. RunnerExecBridge is consumed at api/src/index.ts:226-234 where the handlers are constructed, so export the type from the new module and update both handler signatures. No test coverage exists for any of this code, so review the extraction diff carefully — tsc is the only gate.

confirmed.title: mcpManager: merge _callToolWithAgentAuth/_callToolWithAgentContext and extract the call-retry-shape pattern (copied 3x)
confirmed.description: Three methods repeat the same 'callTool → if message contains 404/session/Invalid token/token/401 then drop client, reconnect, retry once' block: callTool (mcpManager.ts:466-496), _callToolWithAgentAuth (576-609), _callToolWithAgentContext (630-663). _callToolWithAgentAuth and _callToolWithAgentContext are line-for-line identical except the cacheKey and the headers object — even the comments match. The 4-line result-shaping block ({ success: !result.isError, result: extracted.text, images, raw }) is pasted 6 times (469-474, 484-489, 579-584, 601-606, 633-638, 655-660), and the fragile session-expiry sniffing condition is pasted 3 times (476, 587, 641). Any change to the retry policy or expiry heuristic requires 3 coordinated edits today.
confirmed.impact: mcpManager.ts shrinks ~853 → ~710 lines; retry policy, expiry heuristic, and result shape each have exactly one definition instead of 3/3/6
confirmed.guidance: 1) The sketch's claim "Covered by mcpManager.internal.test.ts" is FALSE — that file only tests resolveInternalMcpConfig, builtin-server registration, and skill lists; no existing test exercises callTool, the agent call paths, or the retry logic. Verify by careful diff and consider adding a small unit test for _isSessionExpired and the _callAgentClient retry path (stub agentClients/_connectAgentClient). 2) The sketch drops both reconnect console.log lines ("Agent session/token expired..." / "Agent context session expired..."); keep a log line in _callAgentClient (a unified message or a label param) — service logs are read operationally in this deployment. 3) Keep _isSessionExpired semantically identical to the original err.message?.includes chain over exactly ['404','session','Invalid token','token','401']; do not prune the redundant 'Invalid token' entry in the same change. 4) Do NOT route callTool through _callAgentClient: it uses this.clients (not agentClients), reconnects via this.connect(serverId) (which mutates server.status and persists), falls through to rethrowing the ORIGINAL err when no retry client exists, and wraps retry failures as `MCP call failed after reconnect: ${retryErr.message}` — only _shape and _isSessionExpired are shared there. 5) Preserve the exact context cacheKey format `${agentId}:${server.id}${boardId ? `:${boardId}` : ''}` and the boardId=null default on the public-facing signature of callToolByNameForAgent (unchanged).

confirmed.title: Single source of truth for internal MCP servers (URL map + hardcoded OAuth-server boolean chain)
confirmed.description: The set of internal MCP servers is encoded three times in parallel: (1) BUILTIN_MCP_SERVERS in data/mcpServers.ts declares each `__internal__*` url; (2) resolveInternalMcpConfig hardcodes a 15-entry `__internal__x → http://localhost:PORT/api/x/mcp` map (mcpManager.ts:25-41); (3) callToolByNameForAgent hardcodes which servers need per-agent context in a 8-clause boolean chain on one line (mcpManager.ts:547: server.url === '__internal__onedrive' || ... || '__internal__github'). Adding a new internal MCP requires touching all three, and forgetting (3) silently breaks per-agent OAuth (the past 'decide looped' incidents were this class of gap). The route paths also already exist a fourth time in index.ts:220-272.
confirmed.impact: Deletes the 15-line literal map and the unreadable 1-line/8-clause condition; new internal MCPs become a one-entry change instead of three scattered edits
confirmed.guidance: Corrections to the sketch: (1) Do NOT add internalPath/agentContext directly onto BUILTIN_MCP_SERVERS entries as-is — builtin defs are spread into runtime server objects that are persisted to the mcp_servers JSONB blob and returned over REST: ensureBuiltinServerRegistered {...builtin}+saveMcpServer (mcpManager.ts:148-158), seedDefaults new-server branch (191-200), and getAll/getById via createBuiltinServerEntry (74-83, 273-286). That adds fields to DB rows and API responses (wire-format change). Either export a sibling map in data/mcpServers.ts keyed by __internal__ url ({ path, agentContext }) next to BUILTIN_MCP_SERVERS, or strip the two new fields at those three spread sites. (2) internalPath must remain explicit per entry — paths are not derivable from url slugs (__internal__aws_s3 → /api/s3/mcp; underscore→dash for code-index/gandi-dns/swarm-api/auto-learn). (3) The test net is weaker than claimed: mcpManager.internal.test.ts pins only code_index and onedrive URLs; aws_s3, the dash alias __internal__code-index, and the entire agentContext set at line 547 are untested. Transcribe the agentContext set exactly as {onedrive, gmail, outlook, gdrive, slack, jira, wordpress, github} — the other 6 internal servers (code_index, gandi_dns, swarm_api, aws_s3, auto_learn, browser) must keep using the global connection. Consider extending the test to pin aws_s3 and the dash alias. (4) Keep URL construction inside resolveInternalMcpConfig using the port param (tests pass port 4123) — don't precompute full URLs at module load. (5) Benign semantic delta to be aware of: mappings[serverUrl] object lookup was truthy for prototype keys ('constructor', 'toString'); Map.get returns undefined → passthrough. Latent-bug fix, acceptable. (6) Leave the index.ts:220-272 route mounts (the 4th copy) alone — handlers take heterogeneous constructor args; unifying them would be over-abstraction.

confirmed.title: swarmApiMcp: jsonError/jsonOk helpers + shared agent-resolution kill ~15 repeated MCP-response blocks
confirmed.description: The 5-line error envelope `{ content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify({ error: ... }) }], isError: true }` is pasted ~12 times (swarmApiMcp.ts:108-115, 210-217, 225-233, 236-243, 251-261, 272-279, 355-359, 365-372, 380-409, 420-427, 545-549, 627-634), and the success envelope `JSON.stringify(..., null, 2)` another ~8 times. Resolving an agent from agent_id-or-agent_name (Array.from(agentManager.agents.values()).find(name-lowercase-match)) is copied 3 times: get_agent_status (99-105), update_task (313-318), search_tasks (540-551). The actual tool logic is buried under envelope plumbing — update_task is 200 lines of which ~70 are response wrappers.
confirmed.impact: ~80 lines removed in one file; every tool body reads as validation + action instead of envelope assembly
confirmed.guidance:
Corrections and traps for the implementer:
1. Counts: 14 plain error envelopes (not ~12) and 6 clean success envelopes (not ~8). The reviewer's "545-549" is 544-549 and "380-409" spans three separate blocks — recount when editing.
2. task_execution_complete (swarmApiMcp.ts:642-653) is NOT a plain envelope: it has `...(outcome.success ? {} : { isError: true })`. Leave it as-is, or compose as `outcome.success ? jsonOk(p) : { ...jsonOk(p), isError: true as const }` — do not run it through plain jsonOk/jsonError.
3. search_tasks (539-551) does NOT validate agent_id — an unknown agent_id is passed straight to searchTasks and returns zero results, not an error; only the agent_name branch errors on miss. Use findAgent there only for the name branch (`if (!resolvedAgentId && agent_name)`); routing agent_id through findAgent and erroring on null would change behavior. So findAgent fully replaces only get_agent_status (99-105) and update_task (313-318).
4. update_task's no-params branch (319-325) scans all agents for the task. Today a provided-but-unknown agent_id skips that scan and falls through to the DB lookup at 335-353. Keep the scan gated on "neither agent_id nor agent_name provided" — do not turn it into a fallback after findAgent returns null.
5. The 405/500 responses in createSwarmApiMcpHandler (666, 681) are HTTP JSON, not MCP envelopes — out of scope.
6. TypeScript: inline literals are contextually typed by the SDK callback signature; extracted helpers lose that. Keep `type: 'text' as const` (and `isError: true as const`) as sketched and confirm assignability to the installed @modelcontextprotocol/sdk CallToolResult before declaring done (run api tsc).
7. Error strings are regex-asserted by tests (/Invalid repo_full_name/, /Task not found/, /Agent not found/, /board_id is required/, /At least one of/, /not bound to a board/, /No agent context/) — every message must stay byte-identical, which holds as long as messages are passed verbatim to jsonError.

confirmed.title: swarmApiMcp: decompose the 200-line update_task handler and dedupe board-column validation with add_task
confirmed.description: update_task (swarmApiMcp.ts:310-509) does five jobs inline: agent resolution (313-325), task lookup with DB-fallback + in-memory rehydration setting a `boardLevel` flag (327-360), three validation blocks, then two fully divergent mutation paths — a 40-line board-level branch that hand-rolls history entries and execution-state clearing (448-486) versus the agentManager helper path (487-497). The status-against-board-columns validation (378-410) is a near-copy of the same check in add_task (249-262), including the identical `Invalid status "x" for board ... Valid columns:` error string. The boardLevel boolean threading makes the control flow hard to follow: the reader must track which of agent/task/boardLevel combinations is live at each point.
confirmed.sketch:
Extract three module-level helpers:
async function validateStatusForBoard(boardId: string, status: string): Promise<{ ok: true } | { ok: false; error: string }>  // shared by add_task and update_task
async function locateTask(agentManager, { agent_id, agent_name, task_id }): Promise<{ task; agent: Agent | null; boardLevel: boolean } | null>  // the 327-360 rehydration dance
async function applyBoardLevelUpdate(agentManager, task, { repoUpdate, storageUpdate, status }): Promise<Task>  // the 448-486 branch incl. history push + execution-state reset + _checkAutoRefine hook
update_task then reads: locate → validate inputs → validate status → (boardLevel ? applyBoardLevelUpdate : apply via agentManager helpers) → jsonOk. swarmApiMcp.test.ts pins both paths.
confirmed.impact: update_task drops from ~200 to ~80 lines; the duplicated column-validation error logic becomes one function; the board-level vs in-memory split becomes explicit instead of flag-threaded
confirmed.guidance: 1) Drop or narrow the validateStatusForBoard dedup: the two blocks are semantically divergent. add_task (swarmApiMcp.ts:249) skips validation when the board has no workflow columns and when status is falsy (incl. ''); update_task (378-398) hard-errors on missing task.boardId, hard-errors on missing columns, and validates '' (status !== undefined gating). If deduping at all, share only the genuinely identical inner part — e.g. findInvalidStatusError(board, boardLabelId, status): string | null doing the case-insensitive column match + the identical `Invalid status "x" for board "y". Valid columns: ...` string — taking the already-fetched board object (add_task has it from line 235; do not re-fetch) and keeping all gating at each call site. Do not unify with the similar-but-differently-worded checks in agentManager/tools.ts:592-624 or routes/tasks.ts:270 (wire-visible error strings differ). 2) The sketch's claim that swarmApiMcp.test.ts pins both paths is false: the boardLevel branch (448-486) has zero test coverage — the test file never mocks ./database/tasks.js getTaskById and its fake agentManager defines no saveTaskDirectly/_emit/_checkAutoRefine/_ensureTaskInMemory. Either add a pin test first (mock getTaskById to return an agentId-less task; extend the fake manager) or keep applyBoardLevelUpdate a strict cut-paste. 3) Behavior traps in applyBoardLevelUpdate: preserve the two distinct history-entry shapes (edit entries {status, at, by, type:'edit', field, oldValue, newValue} vs status-change entry {from, status, at, by}); preserve that the _checkAutoRefine guard at line 483 reads task.status AFTER mutation (i.e. it checks the NEW status !== 'error'); preserve saveTaskDirectly receiving {...task, agentId: task.agentId || null} and the _emit before _checkAutoRefine ordering; keep status==='done' => completedAt. 4) Trap in locateTask: when agent was resolved via agent_id/agent_name but the task is absent from that agent's list, the DB fallback may rehydrate and REASSIGN agent to a different owner (line 341-344) — the helper must return the possibly-reassigned agent, and the final response at line 505 uses that agent's id/name.

confirmed.title: gmailMcp: list_emails and search_emails share a duplicated fetch-metadata-and-format block
confirmed.description: list_emails (gmailMcp.ts:584-611) and search_emails (629-654) both end with the same ~25-line sequence: Promise.all over messages.slice(0, limit) fetching `?format=metadata&metadataHeaders=From&...` per id, map through formatMessage, then build the numbered `${unread} ${subject} / From / Date / snippet / ID` summary. The only difference is that list_emails also renders a star emoji (602) and the header line text. The long metadata query string is duplicated verbatim (594 and 638).
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines removed; the Gmail metadata-fields contract is stated once
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is essentially correct; respect these traps: (1) Keep the exact template `${i + 1}. ${unread}${star} ${m.subject}` — do not "normalize" spacing (e.g. `${unread} ${star}${subject}`), or starred list_emails output changes; with star='' it already matches search_emails byte-for-byte. (2) The sketch's signature takes messageIds: string[] but both call sites hold message objects from `list.messages`; either pass `messages.map(m => m.id)` or accept `{id: string}[]`. Keep `slice(0, limit)` inside before fetching so the API call count is unchanged. (3) Keep the empty-list early returns and the distinct header lines ('Found N email(s)' vs 'Search "q" found N result(s)', both using `list.resultSizeEstimate || messages.length`) in the callers — they differ and the caller still needs `list`. (4) Do NOT fold in the third format=metadata fetch at line 758 (reply flow) — it requests a different header set (Cc, Message-ID, References) for a different purpose. (5) Do not "improve" search_emails by always rendering the star — that would change output bytes for starred search results; keep the withStar flag as sketched.

confirmed.title: Shared size-capped response reader for gdriveMcp.downloadFile and onedriveMcp.read_file
confirmed.description: The defensive 'stream the body via getReader(), accumulate chunks, abort with a too-large error if the running total exceeds maxBytes, with an arrayBuffer fallback when no reader exists' loop is implemented twice with the same structure and comments: gdriveMcp.ts:165-188 (inside downloadFile) and onedriveMcp.ts:184-208 (inside the read_file tool). Both even share the `try { await reader.cancel(); } catch { /* ignore */ }` line. This is non-obvious backpressure/heap-safety code that should have one owner.
confirmed.impact: ~45 duplicated lines → one ~20-line helper; the heap-safety invariant is testable in isolation
confirmed.guidance: One subtle divergence the sketch silently papers over: the two copies format the max-size portion of the error message differently. gdrive (gdriveMcp.ts:169,183) uses (maxBytes/1024/1024).toFixed(1) → "max 5.0 MB" / ">5.0 MB"; onedrive (onedriveMcp.ts:189,202) uses MAX_READ_BYTES/1024/1024 with no toFixed → "max 5 MB" / ">5 MB". The sketched helper changes onedrive's thrown error text (e.g. ">5 MB" → ">5.0 MB"). Verified no test asserts these strings and no code parses them (they surface only as MCP error text to the agent), so harmonizing on toFixed(1) is fine — but state the message change explicitly in the commit/PR rather than calling it byte-identical. Also: (1) onedrive's separate pre-download metadata size check at onedriveMcp.ts:156-166 returns a friendly content message instead of throwing — it must NOT be folded into the helper; the helper only replaces the post-download backstop, as the sketch already does. (2) llmProviders.ts:245 also calls getReader() but is SSE streaming — leave it out of scope. (3) Keep the helper signature (res: Response, maxBytes: number) => Promise<Buffer>; gdrive passes its maxBytes parameter (default MAX_TEXT_READ_BYTES), onedrive passes MAX_READ_BYTES.

confirmed.title: slackApi: replace the inline GET-method string chain and triple fetch branches with a lookup set
confirmed.description: slackMcp.ts:14 buries the GET-vs-POST decision in one boolean expression mixing a structural condition with five hardcoded method names: `const isGet = Object.keys(params).length === 0 || method === 'users.list' || method === 'conversations.list' || ...`. Lines 17-41 then spell out three nearly identical fetch calls (GET with query string, bare GET, POST) that differ only in URL suffix and body. A reader cannot tell at a glance which Slack methods are GET by design versus GET by accident of having no params, and adding a new read method means editing a boolean chain.
confirmed.impact: 30 lines → ~18 with one fetch call instead of three; GET-by-design methods become declarative data
confirmed.guidance: Three behavior-preservation traps: (1) The GET set must contain EXACTLY the five methods from line 14 — do NOT add conversations.replies even though it is a read method (read_thread, line 154); it currently goes out as POST with a JSON body and adding it would change the wire request. Likewise keep the zero-params-implies-GET quirk (`|| Object.keys(params).length === 0`). (2) Trailing-'?' edge: the original appends `?${qs}` whenever params is non-empty, even if all values are null/undefined (empty qs → URL ends in bare '?'); the sketch's `if (qs.size)` drops that '?'. HTTP-equivalent but not byte-identical — gate on `Object.keys(params).length > 0` like the original if exactness is wanted. (3) TS typing: `init.headers['Content-Type']` fails type-check on HeadersInit; declare headers as Record<string, string> and build the fetch init from it. Non-issues confirmed: || reordering is pure; URLSearchParams(entries) vs set() loop identical since Object.entries keys are unique; URLSearchParams.size is available (@types/node ^25); no test expectations exist for this module.

confirmed.title: Delete dead (and broken) LoggingProvider / createLoggingProvider
confirmed.description: api/src/services/llmProviders.ts:1091-1183 defines LoggingProvider and createLoggingProvider. Grep over the whole repo shows zero importers — only the definitions exist. Worse, the code is broken in three ways that would mislead anyone who tries to use it: (1) line 1147 calls this._provider.stream(...) but every provider implements chatStream, not stream; (2) lines 1125/1152-1154 read usage.prompt_tokens/completion_tokens but providers return { inputTokens, outputTokens }, so the token log would always print '?'; (3) lines 1158-1159 read chunk.content/chunk.thinking but stream chunks carry { type, text }. It is a stale wrapper from a previous provider-result shape.
confirmed.sketch: Delete lines 1091-1183 (the '── Logging Wrapper ──' section and createLoggingProvider). No call sites to update; api tsc + tests stay green.
confirmed.impact: 93 lines removed; eliminates a trap that contradicts the live provider interface
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Delete lines 1091-1183 inclusive (section comment '── Logging Wrapper ──' through the closing brace of createLoggingProvider); line 1090 closes createProvider so the file ends cleanly. No call sites, no test references — api/tests/handoff.test.ts:9 only auto-mocks the module and does not touch createLoggingProvider. Do not attempt to 'fix' the wrapper instead of deleting it; per-provider logging already exists inline (e.g., the chatStream console.log at line 584).

confirmed.title: Delete dead swarmService.ts (CommonJS relic of the file-based agent store)
confirmed.description: api/src/services/swarmService.ts (120 lines) has zero importers anywhere in the repo. It is also internally inconsistent with the codebase: it uses require()/__dirname/module.exports (lines 1-7, 114-121) inside a TypeScript ESM package — importing it would throw 'require is not defined' at runtime — and it persists agents as JSON files under api/src/data/agents/, a storage model the rest of the code replaced with Postgres (services/database.ts). Functions like executeWorkflow and assignTaskToAgent duplicate (a stale version of) AgentManager/workflow-engine behavior, which is actively confusing to anyone grepping for 'workflow' or 'assign task'.
confirmed.sketch: git rm api/src/services/swarmService.ts. Nothing imports it; the swarm-config.json / agents/ dirs it manages are not referenced elsewhere.
confirmed.impact: 120 lines / 1 whole file removed; one fewer false hit when searching for workflow/task-assignment logic
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as-is: git rm api/src/services/swarmService.ts, then run api tsc + tests as a formality. Trivial nit: the file is 121 lines, not 120. No traps found — no dynamic import, no test fixture, no script references the file or its data paths.

confirmed.title: Collapse 7 copy-pasted runner-service cases in createProvider into a lookup table shared with ExecutionManager
confirmed.description: llmProviders.ts:1007-1081 has seven near-identical switch cases (claude-paid, opencode, aider, openclaw, hermes, codex, claudecode) that each build `new VLLMProvider(process.env.X_SERVICE_URL || 'http://x-service:8000', config.model, readSecret('CODER_API_KEY'), config.agentId || null, config.ownerId || null, config.permissions || null, config.llmConfig || null)` — only the env var and default URL differ (claude-paid additionally omits llmConfig and defaults the model). The exact same envvar→default-URL mapping is independently re-declared in executionManager.ts:41-59 (DEFAULT_URLS + URL_ENV_VARS). Adding a new CLI runner today requires touching 3 parallel lists.
confirmed.impact: ~80 lines down to ~25 across both files; one canonical place to register a runner service
confirmed.guidance: Three behavior-preservation traps: (1) claude-paid (llmProviders.ts:1007-1015) differs in THREE ways, not two — it defaults the model to 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', it does NOT forward config.llmConfig (always null even when set), and it shares CLAUDECODE_SERVICE_URL with claudecode. It must stay its own case; folding it into the CLI_RUNNERS set would change what the claudecode runner receives. (2) runnerServiceUrl() must read process.env at call time inside the function body — do not resolve URLs at module load (terminal.ts:38-45 does that; don't copy its pattern into the registry), since createProvider currently re-reads env on every call. (3) ExecutionManager's make() must preserve override precedence: opts?.baseUrl || runnerServiceUrl(type). Additional notes: the registry must include 'sandbox' (used only by ExecutionManager; exclude it and claude-paid from the CLI_RUNNERS set in createProvider); derive ProviderType as keyof typeof RUNNER_SERVICES to eliminate the third parallel list (the hand-written union at executionManager.ts:14 and the literal checks in _normalizeProviderType at :16-19); api/src/routes/terminal.ts:38-45 and api/src/index.ts:108 duplicate the same mapping and can be folded in as a follow-up, but terminal.ts currently resolves env at import time, so converting it changes env-read timing (benign in Docker, but call it out).

confirmed.title: Deduplicate the three OAuth login flows in middleware/auth.ts
confirmed.description: Three copies of the same machinery: (1) resolveGoogleRedirectUri (266-274), resolveMicrosoftRedirectUri (412-419) and resolveGitHubRedirectUri (562-570) are byte-identical functions — only the comments differ. (2) The find-or-create-user block is copy-pasted 3x: Google 357-372, Microsoft 504-517, GitHub 665-679 (getUserByXId → link existing by email → else countUsers/first-user-admin → createXUser → provisionNewUser). (3) The jwt.sign + res.json login payload appears 5x with the same fields: /login 147-161, /impersonate 213-231, Google 374-389, Microsoft 519-534, GitHub 681-696. Any change to the login response (e.g. adding a field like tutorialCompletedAt was) must be repeated 5 times.
confirmed.impact: ~120 lines removed from a 910-line file; login-payload changes become one edit instead of five
confirmed.guidance: Behavior-preservation corrections to the sketch: (1) Do NOT route /impersonate through sendLoginResponse as sketched — its response (lines 224-231) deliberately omits termsAcceptedAt/tutorialCompletedAt and its JWT adds impersonatedBy; the sketch's common '...terms/tutorial fields' body would add two fields to that wire response. Either leave /impersonate bespoke (recommended — it is 1 of 5 and genuinely different) or gate terms/tutorial behind an option. That also removes the impersonatedBy conditional-spread from the helper entirely. (2) avatarUrl key presence differs: /login (153-161) has no avatarUrl key; the OAuth callbacks include `avatarUrl: user.avatar_url || providerAvatarUrl` even when the value is null (Microsoft/GitHub avatars can be null). Passing extra.avatarUrl=undefined for /login works because res.json/JSON.stringify drops undefined-valued keys, but do not coerce undefined to null or the /login payload gains "avatarUrl":null. (3) In findOrCreateOAuthUser, rename the `email` param — GitHub passes a computed username (profile email OR `${login}@users.noreply.github.com`, lines 656-659), not an email. Call it loginUsername.
