(4) Preserve exact ordering inside the helper: provider-id lookup → getUserByUsername → linkXId then getUserById refetch → countUsers → role 'admin'/'advanced' (it is 'advanced', not 'basic') → createXUser → provisionNewUser(user.id).catch with the identical 'Provisioning error:' log. (5) When merging the three resolve*RedirectUri functions, consolidate the three comments — each documents its provider's frontend callback route vs plugin dispatcher path; that information should survive in the shared helper's comment. (6) No test coverage pins these payloads (no auth-route tests in api/src/services/__tests__), so verify by diff inspection, not by the green suite.

confirmed.title: Replace ExecutionManager's 7 parallel provider fields with one Map
confirmed.description: executionManager.ts declares the same 7 runner types in six parallel places: class fields (62-68), constructor assignments (79-85), ExecutionManagerOptions keys (25-31), the _normalizeProviderType equality chain (16-19), the _getProvider switch (103-113), the destroyAll list (194-205), and the closeCliTerminalSessions array (220-228). Nothing outside the class accesses the named fields (verified by grep), so the per-type fields are pure boilerplate. Adding a runner type means 6 coordinated edits.
confirmed.impact: ~50 lines removed; new runner type = 1 array entry instead of 6 edits
confirmed.guidance: Three corrections to the sketch, all behavior-preservation: (1) Do NOT use `this.providers.get(t)!` in _getProvider — the current switch has `default: return this.sandbox` (executionManager.ts:111), a runtime fallback for any value not matching a case. Although unreachable today (every call site passes a value from _normalizeProviderType or from _agentProviders, which only stores normalized values), `get(t)!` would turn that defensive arm into a silent undefined. Use `return this.providers.get(type) ?? this.providers.get('sandbox')!;`. (2) `PROVIDER_TYPES.includes(t)` won't typecheck as written: the argument is `string | undefined | null` and PROVIDER_TYPES is a readonly literal tuple; write `(PROVIDER_TYPES as readonly string[]).includes(t as string) ? (t as ProviderType) : 'sandbox'`. (3) closeCliTerminalSessions (220-240) lists providers in a different order (claudecode, codex, opencode, aider, openclaw, hermes) than PROVIDER_TYPES; since Promise.allSettled fires all calls concurrently and the function returns true if ANY fulfilled true, runtime behavior is identical under Map iteration order — the only observable difference is which provider's rejection the console.warn at 235-237 cites when multiple reject (log-only, acceptable). Keep the docstring at 211-219 explaining why sandbox is excluded. Two optional extensions: derive ExecutionManagerOptions via a mapped type `{ [K in ProviderType as `${K}Options`]?: RunnerOpts }` to kill that parallel list too, and note DEFAULT_URLS/URL_ENV_VARS (41-59) are mechanically derivable (`http://${t}-service:8000`, `${t.toUpperCase()}_SERVICE_URL` — verified true for all 7) so they can collapse into the `make` helper as well, taking the repetition count from 10 sites to 1.

confirmed.title: Extract one OpenAI-compatible stream-consumption helper used by OpenAI, VLLM and Mistral providers
confirmed.description: The chunk-consumption loop "for await chunk → abort check → yield {type:'text'} from delta.content → yield {type:'thinking'} from delta.reasoning_content → capture finish_reason → on chunk.usage yield {type:'done', finishReason, usage}" is written three times nearly verbatim: OpenAIProvider._chatCompletionStream (620-647), VLLMProvider.chatStream (833-874), MistralProvider.chatStream (944-970). VLLM adds only cost_usd and runner_session_id forwarding. MistralProvider as a whole (888-981) is essentially VLLMProvider minus custom headers — both wrap an OpenAI client pointed at a different baseURL with the same chat() body and the same usage mapping.
confirmed.impact: ~90 duplicated lines collapse to one ~20-line generator; stream-protocol fixes (e.g. a new delta field) become one edit instead of three
confirmed.guidance: Scope to the stream-consumption helper ONLY; drop or defer the optional "fold MistralProvider into an OpenAICompatProvider base" — ping() differs entirely (VLLM: raw fetch ${baseUrl}/v1/models with 5s timeout, 877-885; Mistral: client.models.list(), 973-980), VLLM's constructor carries X-Agent-Id/X-Owner-Id/X-Agent-Permissions/X-LLM-Config header machinery (753-770), chat() differs on cost_usd and X-Task-Id, and the factory reuses VLLMProvider for claude-paid. Traps for the helper: (1) VLLM's runnerSessionId must stay at the TOP LEVEL of the done event while costUsd goes INSIDE usage (864-872) — the sketch's top-level ...mapUsage(chunk) spread is correct, but VLLM's mapper must return { usage, runnerSessionId? }, not fold both into usage; (2) fix the sketch's signature/call mismatch — mapUsage is typed (u: any) but called with the whole chunk; VLLM needs chunk.usage.cost_usd and chunk.usage.runner_session_id (both on chunk.usage), so pass chunk.usage consistently; (3) do NOT absorb _responsesChatStream (650-695), it consumes a different event protocol; (4) keep the done-yield inside the for-await loop as sketched; (5) note the one deliberate normalization: OpenAI's chunk.choices[0] (623) becomes choices?.[0] — safe in practice (OpenAI sends choices: [] on the usage-only chunk) but call it out in the commit; (6) preserve the exact abort error message 'Agent stopped by user'.

confirmed.title: Deduplicate OllamaProvider chat/chatStream request building and 404-auto-pull retry
confirmed.description: OllamaProvider.chat (153-204) and chatStream (206-243) duplicate two blocks verbatim: the request body construction including the num_ctx if/else (154-170 vs 207-222, differing only in stream:true/false), and the fetch → if 404 → _pullModel() → identical second fetch → if !ok throw sequence (172-191 vs 224-243). Six copies of the same fetch options literal exist in this one class.
confirmed.impact: ~50 lines removed; the 404-pull recovery behavior is stated once
confirmed.guidance: 1) CRITICAL: do NOT use `options.contextLength ?? 8192` as the sketch suggests — use `options.contextLength || 8192`. The original `if (options.contextLength)` is a truthiness check, and contextLength === 0 is a real value in this codebase (api/src/services/agentManager/chat.ts:984, chat.ts:1054, crud.ts:41, index.ts:270/288 all default it to 0). With `??`, Ollama would receive num_ctx: 0 instead of 8192 — a runtime behavior change. 2) Keep the helper call awaited inside the chatStream generator body so the request still fires lazily on first next(), not at generator creation. 3) Preserve the `options.signal || null` normalization (undefined -> null) when passing the signal to ollamaFetchWithRetry. 4) Keep JSON.stringify(body) inside the doFetch closure so both attempts stringify per-call as today (body is not mutated between attempts, so this is belt-and-braces). 5) Do not fold the _pullModel fetch (lines 132-136) into the shared helper — it targets /api/pull with plain fetch and no retry wrapper; only the four /v1/chat/completions fetches are true duplicates.

confirmed.title: socketHandler: merge the twin task-execute handlers and flatten the voice-management switch
confirmed.description: Two patterns bloat the 567-line connection callback: (1) REQ_TASK_EXECUTE (300-320) and REQ_TASK_EXECUTE_ALL (323-343) are line-for-line identical (lookup agent → STREAM_START → call manager with chunk-forwarding callback → STREAM_END/STREAM_ERROR) except for the manager method called; the voice REQ_VOICE_DELEGATE (346-403) and REQ_VOICE_ASK (406-452) repeat the same find-target-by-name → not-found-emit → streamStart/sendMessage/streamEnd skeleton. (2) The REQ_VOICE_MANAGEMENT switch (478-580) repeats `const target = findAgent(args.agent_name); if (!target) { result = `Agent "..." not found`; break; }` in 6 of its 11 cases.
confirmed.impact: ~70 lines removed; each voice function reads as a 3-5 line entry instead of a case buried in a 100-line switch
confirmed.guidance: Corrections and traps: (a) The sketch's table ctx {target, args} is insufficient — list_agents, get_available_agent, and clear_all_* need userAgents and agentId (self-exclusion), and most cases need agentManager/ws; define the table inside the REQ_VOICE_MANAGEMENT handler so entries close over them (per-event allocation is negligible) rather than widening ctx. (b) Not-found must be emitted as { error: null, result: 'Agent "X" not found' } — never throw from the dispatcher; a throw would route to the catch at socketHandler.ts:583 and flip the frontend (VoiceSessionContext.tsx:419-423) into its error path, changing what the Realtime LLM receives. (c) Dispatcher must `result = await entry.run(ctx)` because cases mix sync and async (clear_context, list_projects, clear_all_chats await). (d) Keep all result strings, per-case console.log lines, and the default `Unknown management function: ${functionName}` message byte-identical — the voice LLM consumes them. (e) streamTaskToSocket must use socket.emit (this socket only) and keep STREAM_START inside the try; keep per-handler destructuring and the canAccessAgent/taskId guards outside the helper. (f) Do NOT extend the merge to REQ_VOICE_DELEGATE/REQ_VOICE_ASK despite the description calling them "the same skeleton": ASK has a busy-check (423), extra sendMessage args (441-442), and no ws.thinking in its chunk callback; DELEGATE calls addTask (375) and uses a different prefix; both use the broadcast ws.streamStart/streamChunk/streamEnd emitter, not socket.emit. Fusing them would change behavior or require flag soup — leave them as-is, as the sketch already does.

confirmed.title: Split board/project authorization helpers out of middleware/auth.ts
confirmed.description: middleware/auth.ts is a 910-line god file holding three unrelated concerns: (a) login/OAuth/terms HTTP routes (116-738), (b) the authenticateToken/requireRole middleware (740-764), and (c) the IDOR resource-authorization layer — Permission, checkBoardAccess, authorizeBoardAccess, checkProjectAccess, authorizeProjectAccess (766-908). The authz layer has nothing to do with login routes; it is imported by routes/{boards,projects,tasks,agents}.ts and tested independently (services/__tests__/authorization.test.ts, projectTenantIsolation.test.ts). Finding checkBoardAccess today means scrolling past 700 lines of OAuth plumbing.
confirmed.sketch: New api/src/middleware/authz.ts containing lines 766-908 verbatim (Permission, PERMISSION_LEVELS, BoardAccessResult, checkBoardAccess, authorizeBoardAccess, ProjectAccessResult, checkProjectAccess, authorizeProjectAccess) plus its database.js imports. Update the 7 importing files (routes/tasks.ts, routes/agents.ts, routes/projects.ts, routes/boards.ts, the two tests, and re-export from auth.ts temporarily if preferred). auth.ts drops to ~740 lines (~600 after the OAuth dedup proposal).
confirmed.impact: auth.ts shrinks ~145 lines; authorization logic becomes a discoverable, separately-testable module
confirmed.guidance: Corrections and traps: (1) TEST MOCK TRAP — projectTenantIsolation.test.ts:60-66 uses node:test mock.module('../../middleware/auth.js') to stub requireRole + checkBoardAccess + checkProjectAccess before dynamically importing routes/projects.js and routes/tasks.js. If route files repoint their check* imports to authz.js, this test MUST add a parallel mock.module('../../middleware/authz.js', { namedExports: { checkBoardAccess, checkProjectAccess } }) while keeping requireRole in the auth.js mock — otherwise routes bind the real implementations (failure is loud, 403-vs-404 assertion mismatch, but fix it in the same commit). (2) authorization.test.ts:67 imports check* from '../../middleware/auth.js'; repoint to authz.js (the no-op OAuth stubs at its lines 41-55 then become removable, but leaving them is harmless). (3) Prefer a hard move + updating all 6 importers over a permanent re-export from auth.ts: node:test mock.module keys on the resolved module specifier, so two paths exporting the same symbols invites future mock drift. (4) After the move, drop getBoardById/getBoardShare/getProjectById/hasProjectBoardAccess from auth.ts's import at line 10 — they are unused outside 766-908. (5) Move authorizeBoardAccess (835-851) and authorizeProjectAccess (892-908) VERBATIM even though they are exported-but-never-imported anywhere in the repo (vestigial — routes call the check* functions directly); deleting them is a defensible separate follow-up, not part of this behavior-preserving move. (6) Importer count is 6 (4 route files + 2 tests), not 7. (7) New module needs the NodeNext-style '.js' extension in imports ('../middleware/authz.js') to match the codebase's ESM convention.

confirmed.title: Table-drive the 13 MCP handler mounts in index.ts
confirmed.description: index.ts:220-268 repeats the same two-line pattern 13 times: `const xMcpHandler = createXMcpHandler(); app.all('/api/x/mcp', authenticateToken, (req, res) => xMcpHandler(req, res));` (onedrive, gmail, outlook, gdrive, slack, jira, wordpress, github, s3, code-index, gandi-dns, auto-learn, browser, swarm-api). The `(req, res) => handler(req, res)` lambda is also a no-op wrapper — the handler could be passed directly. Only gmail/outlook (exec bridge) and code-index/gandi-dns/swarm-api (constructor args) differ, and only in how the handler is created.
confirmed.impact: ~48 lines down to ~20; adding an MCP endpoint becomes one array entry
confirmed.guidance: Corrections/notes for the implementer: (1) It is 14 authenticateToken mounts, not 13 (onedrive, gmail, outlook, gdrive, slack, jira, wordpress, github, s3, code-index, gandi-dns, auto-learn, browser, swarm-api internal), plus the separate authenticateApiKey /api/swarm/mcp mount which must stay outside the table. (2) Preserve the existing registration order in the array (paths are disjoint so it is not behavior-critical, but it is free to keep). (3) Keep the two separate createSwarmApiMcpHandler(agentManager) instances (index.ts:267 internal-JWT vs :271 external-API-key) — do not collapse them into one shared instance even though it would likely be safe. (4) Carry over the explanatory comments: the runner-bridge rationale at index.ts:223-225 (attach it to the shared execBridge const) and the internal-vs-external swarm comments at :266 and :270. (5) Type the table as Array<[string, express RequestHandler-compatible fn]> or use `any` as sketched — three factories (jira, wordpress, outlook) annotate (req: any, res: any), so a strict RequestHandler tuple may need the loose typing the sketch already uses.

confirmed.title: Use authenticateToken middleware in /accept-terms and /complete-tutorial instead of hand-rolled JWT parsing
confirmed.description: auth.ts:705-720 (/accept-terms) and 723-738 (/complete-tutorial) each re-implement the auth-header split + jwt.verify + JsonWebTokenError/TokenExpiredError handling that authenticateToken (741-753) already provides — the same try/catch body duplicated twice, differing only in which DB function is called and which field is echoed back. (/verify at 169-198 also parses manually, but it legitimately differs: it fetches fresh user data and reports impersonation, so leave it.)
confirmed.impact: ~16 lines removed; one JWT-validation path instead of three in the same file
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Two notes: (1) the reviewer's caveat is resolved — frontend matches on status/generic toast, never on the 'No token provided' vs 'Access denied' message text, so the wording change is safe; the only user-visible effect is toast text in the degenerate no-token-in-localStorage case. (2) When applying, drop the JsonWebTokenError/TokenExpiredError branch from the handler catch entirely (as the sketch does) — after the middleware runs, jwt errors can no longer reach the handler, so keeping that branch would be dead code. Keep the per-route console.error prefixes ('Accept terms error:' / 'Complete tutorial error:') to preserve log greppability.

confirmed.title: Small vestigial cleanups: unused isGoogleConfigured, both-branches-identical ternary in skillManager, unmounted cookieSecurity middleware
confirmed.description: Three independent one-liners: (1) auth.ts:242-244 isGoogleConfigured() is defined but never called (routes call getGoogleOAuthConfig() directly) — delete. (2) skillManager.ts:84-91: `const ownerId = skill.ownerId === undefined ? (isBuiltin ? null : null) : skill.ownerId;` — the inner ternary returns null on both branches, so isBuiltin is consulted for nothing; the whole thing is `skill.ownerId ?? null` (and the preceding comment about built-ins being 'system-owned' no longer matches what the code does). (3) cookieSecurity.ts:61-76 exports a cookieSecurity() middleware whose docstring says it 'enforces secure flags on any Set-Cookie header' — it has a full test suite (services/__tests__/cookieSecurity.test.ts) but is never mounted in index.ts, so it currently protects nothing. Owner decision needed: mount it (one app.use line, mild behavior change) or delete middleware+tests; as written the docstring is misleading.
confirmed.sketch: (1) Delete auth.ts:242-244. (2) In normalizeSkill: `const ownerId = skill.ownerId ?? null; const shared = skill.shared === undefined ? skill.builtin === true : !!skill.shared;` and drop the isBuiltin temp/stale comment. (3) Either add `app.use(cookieSecurity());` near the security-headers middleware in index.ts (~line 128) or remove cookieSecurity.ts + its test file (~170 lines).
confirmed.impact: ~15-170 lines removed depending on the cookieSecurity decision; removes a misleading 'protection in place' impression
confirmed.guidance: Two refinements. (a) skillManager.ts:82-83 comment is only half stale: 'globally shared by default' still accurately describes the shared computation at line 88-90 — reword the comment (e.g. 'Built-in plugins are globally shared by default; ownerId defaults to null') rather than deleting it, so the still-true rationale for the shared default isn't lost. (b) cookieSecurity: do NOT silently choose the mount option — mounting wraps res.setHeader on every request and is a (mild) behavior change, even though no cookies are issued today; only deletion or docstring-fix is strictly behavior-preserving. If the owner opts to keep-but-not-mount, at minimum amend the cookieSecurity.ts:3-19 docstring to state it is not currently mounted. Deleting removes cookieSecurity.ts (77 lines) + services/__tests__/cookieSecurity.test.ts (11 tests), which lowers the api test count from the 244 baseline — expected, not a regression.

confirmed.title: Extract shared fileRecord/symbolRecord/vectorDoc builder — same indexing block pasted in 3 places
confirmed.description: The symbol-to-vector-doc construction `{ id, vector: createHashedEmbedding(this.createEmbeddingText(rec), this.embeddingDimension), fields: { kind, filePath } }` appears verbatim three times: codeIndexService.ts:481-488 (_indexFolderLocked), :732-739 (_ensureMemoryVectors), :876-883 (_applyFileUpdates). On top of that, the whole extract-symbols → build fileRecord → push symbolIds → build vectorDocs sequence is duplicated between _indexFolderLocked (lines 467-491) and _applyFileUpdates (lines 862-889). Any change to the embedding payload or record shape must be made in 2-3 places and they can silently drift.
confirmed.impact: ~45-50 lines removed in a 928-line file; the on-disk/in-vector record shape gets a single source of truth used by full index, incremental update, and memory rebuild
confirmed.guidance: 1) Keep vector-store I/O OUT of the helpers: _indexFolderLocked must keep its single bulk upsert after resetCollection (lines 498-502) while _applyFileUpdates keeps its per-file upsert guarded by vectorDocs.length > 0 (lines 887-889). 2) Inside _indexFileContent, prefer keeping the original interleaved loop (buildSymbolRecord → fileRecord.symbolIds.push → vectorDocs.push) verbatim rather than the sketch's map-then-reassign form — it is known to compile in this loosely-typed file (reassigning symbolIds: [] risks a never[] inference error) and removes any reordering question, though the reordering itself is provably output-identical since buildSymbolRecord reads only fileRecord.path/language. 3) At the _indexFolderLocked call site, accumulate with a for-of push loop instead of symbols.push(...symbolRecords) to avoid V8 spread argument limits. 4) The helper signature (relativePath, content, size) works for both callers: pass file.relativePath/file.size in _indexFolderLocked and filePath/fileSize in _applyFileUpdates; the size-computation and skip/removed++ logic in _applyFileUpdates (lines 836-859) stays in the caller. 5) Net line reduction is ~25-35, not 45-50 — immaterial to the value.

confirmed.title: Flatten _applyFileUpdates guard chain: dead fallback, duplicate size check, triple skip-pattern
confirmed.description: codeIndexService.ts:836-859 is a check-then-act maze. (1) Line 842: `fileSize = stat?.size ?? (content ? Buffer.byteLength(content) : 0)` — the `??` fallback is dead code because line 843 `if (!stat || ...) continue` exits whenever stat is null, so the fallback value is never read. (2) The maxFileSize check runs twice for the disk path: line 843 (`stat.size > this.maxFileSize`) and again line 856 (`fileSize > this.maxFileSize`, where fileSize === stat.size). (3) The pattern `if (oldFileIdx >= 0) removed++; continue;` is repeated three times (lines 844, 852, 857). Bonus: lines 524-525 and 902-903 both do `this._invalidateCache(repoId); this._setCachedRepo(repoId, repo);` — the invalidate is redundant since _setCachedRepo overwrites the entry.
confirmed.impact: ~15 lines removed, one guard instead of four scattered exits; updated/removed/added accounting is covered by codeIndexService.test.ts:127-235
confirmed.guidance: Two traps. (1) Do NOT take the sketch's fallback option ("if keeping stat is preferred... remove the second size check") literally: line 856 (`fileSize > this.maxFileSize`) is the ONLY size enforcement for the provided-content path (entry.content set, fileSize from Buffer.byteLength at line 848). Removing it while keeping the stat-based check at 843 would silently index oversized inline content — a behavior change. Either use the primary combined-guard sketch (size check applies to both paths) or, if keeping stat, delete only the dead `??` fallback at 842 and leave line 856 in place. (2) The primary sketch changes fileRecord.size (line 866, surfaced via the file tree at line 600) for disk-path files from stat.size to Buffer.byteLength of the utf8-decoded content — identical for valid UTF-8 files, divergent only for invalid-UTF8 content (replacement-char re-encoding), and the inline-content path already records byteLength, so this unifies the function; the full-index path (discoverFiles, line 365) still records stat.size. Accept that delta or keep the stat call for the size value. Also: keep the _invalidateCache calls at line 804 (error-path cache purge) and line 921 (invalidate()) — only the ones at 524 and 902, immediately before _setCachedRepo, are redundant. Preserve the exact skip semantics: every guard failure increments removed only when oldFileIdx >= 0 (old entry was already spliced at lines 823-833 before the guards), including the entry.content === '' case asserted by codeIndexService.test.ts:182-206.

confirmed.title: codeGraphAnalyzer: pushEdge helper to kill 4x direction if/else, merge featureName twins, drop dead stats placeholder
confirmed.description: buildGraph (codeGraphAnalyzer.ts:260-319) repeats `if (direction === 'ui-to-service') edges.push({from: a, to: b}); else edges.push({from: b, to: a});` four times (lines 279-280, 288-289, 293-294, 304-305). The comment at line 297 ('still surface them in service-to-ui mode') is stale — the code surfaces unlinked routes in BOTH directions. Line 316 fills graph.stats with a zeros placeholder that the caller unconditionally overwrites at lines 106-111. And featureNameFromUiPath / featureNameFromServicePath (lines 416-426) are near-identical basename-strippers differing only by `.py` in the regex.
confirmed.impact: ~20 lines removed; the direction semantics become impossible to get wrong in one of four copies; removes a misleading comment and a dead placeholder
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as written; three traps to respect. (a) The featureName merge is a deliberate micro behavior change, not pure refactor: ui-layer node ids/labels for .py files under UI dirs change (ui:main.py -> ui:main), which propagates to mermaid output and the LLM-refinement prompt. Safe (ephemeral cache, no tests, no persistence) but name it in the commit; if strict preservation is ever demanded, keep two one-line wrappers around a shared helper parameterized by the extension regex instead. (b) When removing the stats placeholder, keep the llm: null field wherever the CodeGraph object is assembled (currently line 317) — CodeGraph.stats and the llm field are part of the route response shape, and refineWithLlm reads graph.direction/.nodes/.edges, so assemble the full object before the llmConfigId block at line 113. Passing precomputed stats into buildGraph is the lower-churn option versus restructuring the return type. (c) Keep pushEdge's call order identical to the current push order — edge order feeds dedupEdges and then the mermaid line order, which the cached response exposes verbatim.

confirmed.title: extractJavaScriptSymbols: one pushSymbol helper instead of three identical 10-line blocks
confirmed.description: symbolExtractor.ts:249-307: the function/arrow/method branches each repeat the same block — compute startLine, call findBlockEndJs, push { name, qualifiedName, kind, signature: line.trim(), startLine, endLine, source: createSource(...) } (lines 255-267, 271-283, 295-306). Also line 252 calls findInnermostClass (a filter+sort over all classes) for EVERY line, but classContext is only needed in the method branch at line 286 — both a readability and an O(lines x classes) issue.
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines removed from a 472-line file; the per-line loop body fits on one screen; classContext lookup moves next to its only use
confirmed.guidance: Implementer must keep the method-path guards at lines 292-293 (RESERVED_METHOD_NAMES.has(methodName) and line.trim().startsWith('class ')) before calling pushSymbol — the sketch omits them. Keep an explicit `if (!classContext) continue;` guard in the method path after relocating the findInnermostClass call (its order relative to the methodRegex match is output-irrelevant since both are pure). Use the conditional spread for parentName exactly as sketched — do NOT emit `parentName: undefined`, which would change Object.keys/`in` semantics on function symbols. The class push at lines 238-246 targets classSymbols (a different array) and is correctly out of scope for the closure-based helper; do not force it in. Minor: type `extra` as `{ qualifiedName?: string; parentName?: string }` instead of `any` if convenient.

confirmed.title: InMemoryVectorStore.query: duplicated min-rescan block and a comment claiming a heap that isn't one
confirmed.description: vectorStore.ts:95-134: the 9-line 'find the minimum' scan is pasted twice (lines 110-118 and 122-129), and the comment at line 99 says 'Use a min-heap approach' when it is a linear min-tracking array — actively misleading for the next reader. The whole top-K maintenance is 35 lines for what one small helper expresses.
confirmed.impact: ~12 lines removed, the duplicated rescan collapses to one helper, comment stops lying about the data structure
confirmed.guidance: One behavior-preservation trap in the sketch as written: replacing the minScore = -Infinity sentinel with minIdx = -1 changes behavior for topK <= 0 or fractional topK < 1 — original returns one element (the running max) via the score > -Infinity branch; the sketch throws TypeError on results[-1].score. Unreachable via current callers (only call site is codeIndexService.ts:755 with validated int topK >= 1 from routes/codeIndex.ts:44 and codeIndexMcp.ts:260), but to be strictly faithful either (a) keep a minScore variable updated as minScore = results[minIdx].score after each findMinIndex call and keep the score > minScore comparison, or (b) keep the sketch and accept the unreachable-path difference, noting it in the commit message. Also keep the original typed signature (collectionName: string, vector: number[], topK: number = 10): Promise<QueryResult[]> — the sketch elides the types. Replacement comment text in the sketch is accurate; use it.

confirmed.title: Extract search-hit formatter shared by searchSymbols / searchText / searchSemantic
confirmed.description: Three search methods each hand-build the same result object: codeIndexService.ts:678-689 (searchSymbols), :708-717 (searchText, drops signature/summary for no documented reason), :769-779 (searchSemantic, adds vectorScore). The shape { id, filePath, kind, qualifiedName, signature, summary, score, preview: createPreview(symbol.source, query) } is the API response contract repeated three times — adding a field today means three edits and searchText already drifted.
confirmed.impact: ~20 lines removed; one place defines what a search hit looks like across the REST route and MCP tool surfaces
confirmed.guidance: Do NOT ship the sketch's additive default — adding signature/summary to searchText is a wire-format change (REST /search-text body and MCP search_text JSON dump both change; codeIndexMcp.ts:286 embeds formatJson(results) verbatim in LLM-visible text). Use the preserving form: toSearchHit(symbol, query, scores, { withDoc = true } = {}) with ...(withDoc ? { signature: symbol.signature, summary: symbol.summary } : {}), and call it with withDoc: false from searchText. Two more traps: (1) searchSemantic emits vectorScore BEFORE score (codeIndexService.ts:776-777) and MCP output is raw JSON text, so pass the scores object as { vectorScore, score } in that order to keep serialized bytes identical; (2) keep the Number(...toFixed(6)) computations at the searchSemantic call site as the sketch already does. Place ...scores after the withDoc spread and before preview to match all three current key orders.

confirmed.title: toMermaid groups nodes by layer twice into two parallel structures
confirmed.description: codeGraphAnalyzer.ts:335-380: `layers` (lines 343-346, GraphNode[] per layer) and `byLayer` (lines 372-373, safeId strings per layer) are two passes over the same nodes building parallel groupings, followed by four copy-pasted `if (byLayer.X.length) lines.push(...)` statements (lines 374-377) whose class names ('apiclient' vs 'api-client') only line up by convention with the classDef strings at 367-370.
confirmed.impact: ~15 lines removed; layer label, color, and css class live in one row instead of four scattered string literals
confirmed.guidance: One correction to the sketch: do NOT iterate `layerOrder` for the classDef/class section. `layerOrder` reverses when direction === 'service-to-ui' (lines 348-351), but current output emits classDefs and class lines in fixed ui/apiclient/route/service order regardless of direction, with all four classDef lines grouped before any class line. The sketch would reorder and interleave them for the reversed direction — semantically identical to mermaid but not byte-identical. Instead, declare LAYER_STYLE keys in ui/api-client/route/service order and run two consecutive loops over Object.entries(LAYER_STYLE): first push all classDef lines (unconditionally — today they are emitted even for empty layers), then push the `class` lines guarded by `layers[layer].length`. Also keep the empty-layer `continue` (line 354) in the subgraph loop, and keep using `layerOrder` there since subgraph order IS direction-dependent.

confirmed.title: Rename the repo.repo double: loadRepo returns the whole index document, not the repo
confirmed.description: Throughout codeIndexService.ts, the persisted document { repo: {...metadata}, stats, files, symbols } is held in locals named `repo`, producing repo.repo.id (line 283), repo.repo.rootPath (lines 642, 811), repo.repo.filesIndexed (line 896), and a return of `repo.repo` (line 527). Every reader must mentally untangle which level of 'repo' a given expression means; saveRepo(repo) with repo.repo.id inside (lines 282-285) is the worst offender. The on-disk JSON shape must NOT change — only identifiers.
confirmed.sketch:
Mechanical, file-local rename (loadRepo/saveRepo have zero external callers — verified by grep):
- loadRepo -> loadIndex, saveRepo -> saveIndex (or keep method names, rename only locals)
- locals `repo` holding the document -> `index` (or `repoIndex`), so call sites read index.repo.id, index.symbols, index.files, return index.repo
- _getCachedRepo/_setCachedRepo doc params follow suit.
Touches roughly lines 266-294, 450-528, 542-564, 567-607, 610-622, 625-665, 667-717, 748-783, 799-906. tsc + the 2 codeIndex test files gate the rename.
confirmed.impact: 0 lines removed but disambiguates ~40 expressions in the file's hottest paths; eliminates the repo.repo head-scratcher for every future reader
confirmed.guidance: Two traps, both avoidable: (1) The rename must be per-identifier (IDE/semantic rename or careful manual edits), never a textual find-replace of "repo" — the property key `repo` is wire/disk format and appears in the document literal at lines 504-514 ({ repo: {...}, stats, files, symbols }), the response literals at 619 and 634 (`repo: repo.repo` must become `repo: repoIndex.repo`, keeping the key), and the spread at 553 (`...repo.repo`). (2) Do not rename the locals to bare `index`: getFileTree already uses `let index` as a loop counter at line 581, and saveRepo has `indexPath` at 285 — use `repoIndex` (or `doc`) instead, as the sketch's parenthetical suggests. Minor sketch correction: the cited line ranges omit nothing material (findReposByProject at 913+ goes through listRepos, not loadRepo), and effort is closer to small than medium. Method renames loadRepo->loadIndex / saveRepo->saveIndex are safe (zero external callers, no test mocks, no string dispatch), but keeping method names and renaming only locals is an equally valid lower-touch variant.

confirmed.title: Dead code: unreachable python branch in extractGenericSymbols + three exports nothing imports
confirmed.description: (1) symbolExtractor.ts:428-430: extractGenericSymbols has `language === 'python' ? Math.min(lines.length, startLine + 20) : findBlockEndJs(...)` — unreachable, because extractSymbolsFromContent (lines 461-466) routes python to extractPythonSymbols before the generic path can ever see it. The branch implies generic extraction handles python, which misleads readers. (2) Unused exports (verified by repo-wide grep): detectLanguage (symbolExtractor.ts:450) is only called inside its own module; tokenizeForEmbedding (embedding.ts:16) and normalizeVector (embedding.ts:60) are only used inside embedding.ts. No test imports them either.
confirmed.sketch: In extractGenericSymbols, replace lines 428-430 with `const endLine = findBlockEndJs(lines, index);` and drop the now-unused `language` parameter if createSummary is given the literal (it still needs language for the comment style — keep the param, just delete the ternary). Remove the `export` keyword from detectLanguage, tokenizeForEmbedding, and normalizeVector. tsc will confirm nothing breaks.
confirmed.impact: ~5 lines removed; the module's public surface shrinks to what is actually consumed (extractSymbolsFromContent; normalizeText/createHashedEmbedding/cosineSimilarity/EMBEDDING_DIMENSION)
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct but one parenthetical is confusingly worded: do NOT drop the `language` parameter of extractGenericSymbols — it is still needed by the createSummary(lines, symbol, language) call at symbolExtractor.ts:441 (comment-style selection in extractLeadingComment). Keep the param, delete only the ternary, i.e. `const endLine = findBlockEndJs(lines, index);`. Everything else in the sketch is good as-is: remove `export` from detectLanguage, tokenizeForEmbedding, normalizeVector; leave EMBEDDING_DIMENSION, normalizeText, createHashedEmbedding, cosineSimilarity, extractSymbolsFromContent exported.

confirmed.title: api.ts: replace ~250 hand-rolled fetch blocks with get/post/put/patch/del verb helpers
confirmed.description: Nearly every one of the ~140 members in the api object repeats the same 4-6 line incantation: `apiFetch(`${API_BASE}/...`, { method, headers: getHeaders(), body: JSON.stringify(x) }).then(handleResponse)`. Compare api.ts:103-104 (GET), 156-160 (POST no body), 396-401 (POST with body), 461-466 (PUT), 270-274 (PATCH), 276-280 (DELETE) — identical scaffolding hundreds of times over 1100 lines. The only variations are: long timeout (`signal: AbortSignal.timeout(LONG_TIMEOUT_MS)`, e.g. 140, 176, 202) and three unauthenticated endpoints that pass a bare Content-Type header (login:44-49, OAuth callbacks:67-72/81-86/95-100, submitContact:1035-1040). Also inconsistent: getHealth (40-41) uses `.then(res => res.json())` instead of handleResponse, silently skipping error mapping.
confirmed.impact: ~550-600 lines removed (1132 → ~500); every endpoint reads as method+path+payload at a glance; long-timeout and public-endpoint exceptions become explicit flags instead of buried option diffs
confirmed.guidance: Four corrections/traps: (1) Seven GETs currently send NO headers at all — getHealth (40-41), googleStatus (62), googleAuthUrl (64-65), microsoftStatus (76), microsoftAuthUrl (78-79), githubAuthStatus (89-90), githubAuthUrl (92-93). The sketched `get` always sends getHeaders() (adds Content-Type + Authorization when a token exists); give `get` the same auth:false escape (sending no headers) or leave those 7 raw to preserve behavior on these public routes. (2) Do NOT silently convert getHealth to handleResponse as part of the dedup: /api/health (api/src/index.ts:276-282) always returns 200 and App.tsx:403-410 try/catches it, so the change is practically harmless, but it alters error type/resolution semantics — make it a separate deliberate change or keep res.json(). (3) The bonus delegation is a landmine: inside the api object literal, bare `updateTask(...)` resolves to the module-level export at line 1072 (PUT /tasks/:taskId, board-level), NOT api.updateTask at 324 (PATCH /agents/:agentId/tasks/:taskId); arrow members have no usable `this`. Delegate via api.updateTask(...) or a distinctly named local helper (e.g. patchAgentTask), and keep updateTaskRepo/updateTaskStorage's null-normalization (lines 335, 342) in the wrappers. (4) There are zero frontend tests, so tsc cannot catch a wrong verb or typo'd path string across ~150 entries — do the transformation in small reviewable chunks (or script it) and diff carefully. Minor: realistic line savings is ~400-500, not 550-600, because ~25 query-string-building GETs (488-601, 887-977) only shrink by one line each.

confirmed.title: api.ts: generate the 10 copy-pasted per-provider integration endpoint trios from a factory
confirmed.description: The agentId/boardId → querystring builder is copy-pasted 15 times verbatim (lines 488-494, 496-503, 512-518, 520-526, 535-541, 543-549, 558-564, 566-572, 581-587, 589-595, 887-893, 910-916, 933-939, 956-962, 964-970) and the `{ ...(agentId && { agentId }), ...(boardId && { boardId }) }` disconnect body 9 times (508, 531, 554, 577, 600, 906, 929, 952, 976). OneDrive/Gmail/Outlook/GDrive/Slack/GitHub each define an identical status/auth-url/disconnect trio differing only in the path segment; Jira/WordPress/S3 share the status/connect/disconnect shape. The login-OAuth trio (google 61-72, microsoft 75-86, github 89-100) is a third identical pattern. Adding the next provider today means pasting 25 lines and changing one word.
confirmed.impact: ~160 lines → ~45 in the integrations section plus ~30 → ~10 for login OAuth; the next provider becomes a 4-line addition; eliminates the chance of the query/body builders drifting apart
confirmed.guidance: Four behavior-preservation traps and one description correction: (1) The sketch's `get`/`post` helpers do not exist in api.ts — implement the factory with the existing apiFetch/getHeaders/handleResponse primitives, or add those helpers first; do not change timeout or response handling. (2) Header asymmetry: the login-OAuth GETs (lines 61-65, 75-79, 89-93, paths /auth/{provider}/status|url) send NO headers at all, and the callback POSTs send only Content-Type (no Authorization), whereas all integration endpoints send getHeaders(). The loginProvider factory must reproduce this exactly — do not add getHeaders() to login routes. (3) GitHub appears in BOTH families: login trio githubAuthStatus/githubAuthUrl/githubAuthCallback (/auth/github/*) and integration trio getGitHubStatus/getGitHubAuthUrl/disconnectGitHub (/github/*); both sets of flat names must survive. (4) Keep abBody's key order agentId-then-boardId as sketched (matches all 9 originals byte-for-byte). Correction to the description: Jira/WordPress/S3 do NOT share a connect shape — connectJira (895-900: domain/email/apiToken), connectWordPress (918-923: siteUrl/username/applicationPassword), connectS3 (941-946: accessKeyId/secretAccessKey/region/endpoint) all differ; only their status + disconnect pairs are factory-able, keep the three connect functions bespoke. Also note OneDrive's status (488-494) and disconnect (505-509) fit the standard factory — only its authUrl (496-503, consumer flag) needs to stay custom, as the sketch already says.

confirmed.title: api.ts: delete 28 unused endpoint wrappers (verified zero callers)
confirmed.description: An exhaustive grep over frontend/src (with no dynamic `api[...]` indexing anywhere in the codebase) shows these api members are never referenced outside api.ts: getAgent (115), getAgentStatus (118), getSwarmStatus (121), getAgentStatuses (124), getAgentsByProject (129), getProjectSummary (132), updateAllProjects (246), toggleTask (270), clearTasks (282), transferTask (288), updateTaskText (317), addTaskCommit (345), clearActionLogs (359), setPluginShared (416), addPluginMcp (424), removePluginMcp (430), createMcpServer (454), updateMcpServer (461), deleteMcpServer (468), githubAuthStatus (89), getBoard (791), updateBoardMcpAuth (835), getBoardAuditLogs (867), getTasksByAssignee (870), getBoardRepos (685), getBoardStorages (689), plus standalone exports bulkMoveTasks (1100-1105) and getBoards (1126-1127). The standalone getBoards duplicates api.getBoards (785-786) byte-for-byte and its comment '(standalone export for TaskModal)' is stale — no TaskModal imports it; all 8 consumers use api.getBoards.
confirmed.sketch: Delete each listed member after a per-name confirmation grep (the API server keeps its routes — this only removes the unused client wrappers). Remove the stale '/* Boards (standalone export for TaskModal) */' block at 1125-1127 entirely. If some are kept deliberately as API surface, group them under a clearly-labeled '// currently unused — kept for parity with server routes' section instead of interleaving them with live code.
confirmed.impact: ~140 lines removed; readers stop wondering which of two same-named exports (getBoards, updateTask, deleteTask) is the live one
confirmed.guidance: Three corrections. (1) The impact claim 'readers stop wondering which of two same-named exports (getBoards, updateTask, deleteTask) is the live one' is overstated: this deletion list only resolves the getBoards pair. api.updateTask (api.ts:324, agent-scoped /agents/:id/tasks/:taskId) and standalone updateTask (api.ts:1072, /tasks/:id) are BOTH live and are different endpoints — do NOT delete api.updateTask (4 callers in frontend/src/components/tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx:113,182,666,820). Consider a one-line comment distinguishing the two routes instead. (2) Bonus: api.deleteTask (api.ts:276, agent-scoped) has zero api.deleteTask( callers and can be added to the deletion list after the same per-name grep; the live one is standalone deleteTask (api.ts:1079), imported as deleteTaskById in TasksBoard.tsx. (3) Reject the sketch's fallback option of keeping members under a 'kept for parity with server routes' section — plain deletion is strictly better; wrappers are trivially re-added if a route gains a consumer. Trivial nit: 'all 8 consumers use api.getBoards' is actually 7 call sites across 6 files.

confirmed.title: VoiceSessionContext: unify the three near-identical pending-result handlers (delegate/ask/management)
confirmed.description: handleDelegation (268-329), handleAsk (331-392) and handleManagement (394-442) are three copies of the same 60-line ritual: guard missing socket/agent, build a `pending` record, define `settle()`, register a result handler filtered by agentId, arm a timeout that checks `activeAgentIdRef`, add to pendingResultsRef, then `sock.on(...)` + `sock.emit(...)`. delegate and ask differ only in event names, payload field, and message strings; management additionally matches on functionName and skips the DELEGATING status. This is the most delicate logic in the file (listener leaks, stale-agent timeouts) and it exists in triplicate — a fix to one copy (like the pendingResultsRef bookkeeping) must be hand-mirrored to the other two.
confirmed.impact: ~175 lines → ~80; the settle/timeout/stale-agent invariants live in exactly one place
confirmed.guidance: Four corrections to the sketch, all required for strict behavior preservation: (1) ORDERING — drop onStart from the helper. In all three originals the UI pre-work (setStatus(DELEGATING)/setDelegationTarget/setCurrentFunction/addEvent at 276-279, 339-342, 395-396) runs BEFORE the socket/agentId guard (283-286, 346-349, 400-403); a missing socket today leaves status=DELEGATING and delegationTarget set. The sketch guards first and calls onStart after, silently fixing that quirk. Keep the pre-work (and the missing-field guards at 268-274/331-337) inline in each wrapper before calling the helper. (2) SUCCESS vs TIMEOUT STATUS — success handlers do unconditional setStatus(STATUS.CONNECTED) (302, 365) while timeouts do conditional setStatus(prev => prev === DELEGATING ? CONNECTED : prev) (320, 383); management does neither. The single onSettle?.(data)/onSettle?.(null) callback must branch on its argument to reproduce both, or use two callbacks (onResult/onTimeout). (3) TIMEOUT STRINGS — originals use three distinct strings per flow (setCurrentFunction 321/384/434, addEvent 322/385/435, sendFunctionOutput 323/386/436); the sketch reuses one describe.timedOut for the first two. describe needs timedOutStatus/timedOutEvent/timedOutOutput separately. (4) STRING COERCION — delegate/ask call resultText.slice(0,200) directly (308, 371) while management uses String(resultText).slice (423); the sketch's String(text) everywhere changes the non-string-result path for delegate/ask (today it would throw). Either keep per-wrapper formatting of the addEvent line or accept and note the (benign) difference. Invariant to respect: the pending record must remain exactly { sock, event, handler, timer } because cleanupConnection destructures those fields (141-145). Also: register the handler with sock.on before sock.emit, as the originals do, and keep helper deps at [addEvent, sendFunctionOutput] so handleToolCall's dep list stays correct.

confirmed.title: App.tsx: extract agents/stream socket wiring into a useAgentsSocket hook
confirmed.description: App.tsx interleaves two unrelated concerns: auth/session lifecycle and the agent-streaming socket protocol. The protocol half — agents/thinkingMap/streamBuffers state (19-31), the stale-thinking sweep effect (166-179), SOCKET_EVENTS (185-190, recreated every render since it lives in the component body) and the 165-line initSocket (192-358) with its STREAM_START/CHUNK/END/RESUME/ERROR choreography and the streamEndedAgents/activeStreamAgents ref dance — is self-contained and carries the file's densest comments (159-165, 272-276, 301-304). Readers tracing a login bug must scroll past all of it, and vice versa.
confirmed.impact: App.tsx 651 → ~430 lines; the stream-buffer protocol (the part with the subtlest ordering invariants) gets its own reviewable module; fixes the per-render SOCKET_EVENTS allocation
confirmed.guidance: Corrections and traps: (1) The description's "agents/thinkingMap/streamBuffers state (19-31)" over-claims — lines 20-23 (templates/projects/skills/mcpServers) are unrelated data state that stays in App; only lines 19, 24-31 move. (2) activeStreamAgents (App.tsx:30, written at 260/265/280/295/320) is WRITE-ONLY — nothing ever calls .has on it, contradicting its own comment ("source of truth for whether to keep a streamBuffer alive"). Move it verbatim; deleting it or wiring it up is a separate dead-code finding, not part of this behavior-preserving split. Same for the no-op eviction loop in STREAM_RESUME (285-289, empty body) — move as-is. (3) In App, declare showToast/showToastRef BEFORE the useAgentsSocket call (showToastRef currently sits at line 182, after the stream state); the ref-sync effect stays in App since the window-event effect at 495-511 also uses showToastRef. (4) Split the verify-effect cleanup correctly: `cancelled = true` stays in App; only the SOCKET_EVENTS.forEach(sock.off) loop becomes hook.teardown(). Keep the hook's initSocket on [] deps so App's [initSocket, loadData] effect still runs exactly once. (5) Carry the load-bearing comments (159-165, 272-276, 301-304) into the new file — they document the ordering invariants that motivated this extraction. (6) frontend/src/hooks/ does not exist yet; create it. The new file has no JSX so .ts is fine.

confirmed.title: App.tsx: consolidate the four copy-pasted login completion flows and the OAuth path switch
confirmed.description: The user-object construction from an auth payload is copy-pasted four times (368-376 verify, 445-452 OAuth callback, 471-478 handleLogin, 518-524 handleImpersonate) — the impersonate copy already silently dropped termsAcceptedAt/tutorialCompletedAt, showing the copies are drifting. The post-login sequence `loadData(); initSocket(token); checkDbHealth()` repeats three times (377-380, 453-455, 479-481). The OAuth callback effect (421-439) has three branches identical except for path and api function. Finally, `googleLoading` (33) is misleadingly named: it is also set for Microsoft (428) and GitHub (434) logins.
confirmed.impact: ~55 lines removed; adding a fourth login provider becomes a one-line table entry; the impersonate drift bug class disappears
confirmed.guidance: 1) Drop the "impersonate drift bug" framing: the backend impersonate response (api/src/middleware/auth.ts:224-231) intentionally omits termsAcceptedAt/tutorialCompletedAt, and App.tsx:636 gates the welcome modal on !user.impersonatedBy, so nothing is broken today. Applying toUser there is still safe (fields become null; gate short-circuits), but it is unification, not a bug fix. 2) Do NOT route the initial-verify effect (App.tsx:360-401) through completeLogin: it has a load-bearing `if (cancelled) return;` at line 378 between `await loadData()` and `initSocket(token)` (StrictMode/unmount guard), and it must not be lost. Use only toUser there, or thread an isCancelled callback. 3) checkDbHealth asymmetry: the OAuth path awaits it (455) so the .finally's setLoading(false) runs after the health result; handleLogin (481) does not await. For strict preservation, have completeLogin return the checkDbHealth promise and await it only at the OAuth call site (or accept a one-request spinner-timing shift and document it). 4) handleImpersonate (513-528) must not use completeLogin — it disconnects the socket before initSocket, never calls checkDbHealth, and does not await loadData; apply only toUser there. 5) The rename also touches frontend/src/components/LoginPage.tsx:553-556 (prop name in signature/type) and line 858 — add LoginPage.tsx to the files list. 6) Type the toUser parameter loosely (the verify path feeds data.user, the others feed the flat response) or accept both shapes explicitly; the field names are identical across all payloads, verified against the backend.

confirmed.title: App.tsx: one delete-key reducer instead of nine inline copy/delete blocks
confirmed.description: The pattern `setX(prev => { if (!(id in prev)) return prev; const copy = { ...prev }; delete copy[id]; return copy; })` is written out nine times: thinkingMap at 168-178 (loop variant), 213-219, 237-243, 248-253, 296-300, 332-337; streamBuffers at 224-229, 308-314, 338-342. Each occurrence is 5-7 lines of noise around a one-word intent ('clear this agent's entry').
confirmed.impact: ~45 lines → ~12; socket handlers shrink to their actual intent (pairs naturally with the useAgentsSocket extraction)
confirmed.guidance: Sites are NOT all identical as described — three variants exist. (1) Guarded copy/delete at 213-219, 237-243, 248-253, 332-337; (2) UNGUARDED copy/delete at 224-229, 296-300, 308-314, 338-342; (3) loop sweep with `changed` flag at 168-178. Standardize on the GUARDED helper as sketched: `if (!(key in obj)) return obj;`. Do NOT drop the guard — that would regress the four guarded sites with wasted re-renders on hot AGENT_STATUS/AGENT_UPDATED events. Adding the guard to the four unguarded sites only skips no-op re-renders (identical UI); safe. For the sweep effect (166-179), fold per key: `let next = prev; for (const id of Object.keys(prev)) if (!busyIds.has(id)) next = withoutKey(next, id); return next;` — this preserves the existing `changed ? copy : prev` identity bailout exactly because withoutKey returns the same reference when the key is absent. Keep all surrounding logic untouched: streamEndedAgents.current.has/delete at 222-223 and 307-308, the setTimeout at 306-315, status!=='busy' checks, and the else branch of AGENT_THINKING (255). Define withoutKey at module scope (file is TSX — type it `<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(obj: T, key: string): T`); clearThinking/clearStreamBuffer are safe inside the deps-free initSocket useCallback because setState setters are stable.

confirmed.title: VoiceSessionContext: drop dead context aliases (agentId, isConnected) and unify muted/isMuted
confirmed.description: The context value exposes `activeAgentId` plus an alias `agentId: activeAgentId` (916-917) and `muted` plus `isMuted: muted` (918-919), and computes `isConnected` (933). Grep shows `agentId` and `isConnected` have zero consumers — vestiges of the pre-purge API. `isMuted` is consumed only by VoiceChat.tsx (25, 97-101) while VoiceChatTab.tsx and ActiveVoiceIndicator.tsx use `muted` — two names for one value forces readers to confirm they are the same thing.
confirmed.sketch: In the value memo: remove `agentId: activeAgentId`, `isMuted: muted`, and the `isConnected` line (its expression duplicates isActive minus CONNECTING and nothing reads it). In VoiceChat.tsx replace the `isMuted` destructure and its three usages with `muted`.
confirmed.impact: 3 dead lines + one alias removed; the context surface matches what is actually consumed
confirmed.guidance: Two corrections. (1) VoiceChat.tsx is itself dead: grep shows nothing imports its default export — AgentDetail.tsx imports only VoiceChatTab and ExternalVoiceChatTab. Prefer deleting frontend/src/components/VoiceChat.tsx entirely instead of renaming isMuted→muted inside it; that makes the isMuted alias zero-consumer by construction. If the owner wants to keep the file for a future tab, the sketched rename is also safe as written. (2) The rationale's claim that isConnected "duplicates isActive minus CONNECTING" is backwards-ish: isActive (line 907) = status !== DISCONNECTED && status !== ERROR, while isConnected additionally excludes CONNECTING, so they are NOT equivalent — deletion is still safe (zero consumers) but do not justify it as a duplicate of isActive. No traps otherwise: the useMemo dependency array (lines 935-953) already omits the aliases, so no dep edits are needed; keep `muted` in the deps.

confirmed.title: App.tsx: remove the dead no-op eviction loop in the STREAM_RESUME handler
confirmed.description: In the STREAM_RESUME handler (281-292), the loop `for (const id of Object.keys(prev)) { if (!activeIds.has(id) && !(id in next)) { /* intentionally omit — buffer gets evicted */ } }` has an empty body. Eviction already happens by construction because `next` is built fresh from the server list; the loop iterates and does nothing. Worse, its comment implies it performs the eviction, sending readers hunting for a side effect that does not exist.
confirmed.impact: 8 misleading lines → 2 accurate ones in the trickiest reconnect path
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as written. Two notes: (1) do NOT 'fix' the eviction to spare buffers for agents in streamEndedAgents.current (the anti-flash mechanism at lines 301-315) — current behavior drops them on STREAM_RESUME and must stay that way; (2) the header comment at lines 272-276 ('...AND evict any local buffers...') remains accurate after the change (eviction by construction) and can be left untouched.

confirmed.title: VoiceSessionContext: single resetSessionState helper for the four state-reset litanies
confirmed.description: The same 8-10 setter litany (setStatus, setActiveAgentId + activeAgentIdRef, setMuted, setError, setDelegationTarget, setCurrentTranscript, setCurrentResponse, setCurrentFunction, setEvents) is repeated with slight variations in disconnect (811-823), reconnect (825-846), the socket-loss effect (884-899), and the connect error path (798-806). Spotting which variant resets which fields (reconnect clears events, disconnect does not; the error path keeps activeAgentId null but sets error text) takes a diff in your head.
confirmed.impact: ~50 lines → ~20; the deliberate differences between the four resets become explicit named options instead of omissions
confirmed.guidance: Two behavior-preservation traps in the sketch: (1) reconnect (lines 825-846) deliberately does NOT null setActiveAgentId/activeAgentIdRef — the sketched helper nulls both unconditionally, which during the 100ms setTimeout window flips VoiceChatTab.tsx:7-8 (isSessionForAgent/isOtherAgentActive) and ActiveVoiceIndicator.tsx:5-14 rendering, and changes the gating of the effects at lines 873-882 and 884-899. Add a keepAgent: true option and pass it from reconnect. (2) The connect catch path (798-806) does NOT setMuted(false) — a user can toggle mute after the mic stream attaches (line 588) but before SDP completes, and current code preserves muted=true on failure; the helper must not reset muted on that path (keepMuted option, or have the helper skip muted by default and only the other three sites reset it). Safe non-issues: setDelegationTarget(null) added to the catch path is a no-op (already null from line 562, dc messages cannot interleave with connect's sync tail, React bails out on same-value sets); setter ordering differences across sites are unobservable under React 18 automatic batching; the helper's useCallback with [] deps is correct (setters and refs are stable). Keep addEvent calls OUTSIDE the helper (disconnect: addEvent('system','Session ended'); catch: addEvent('error', msg); reconnect and socket-loss: no event). Do NOT fold in the fifth litany at connect() start (560-569) — it has a different shape (CONNECTING, sets agentId, clears events) and folding it would over-parameterize the helper.

confirmed.title: Use lib/safeStorage everywhere instead of three re-implemented try/catch localStorage wrappers
confirmed.description: lib/safeStorage.ts exists precisely for storage-blocked browsers, yet the same try/catch is re-implemented inline in api.ts getHeaders (15-16), socket.ts auth callback (28-29), and LanguageContext.tsx which redefines its own private safeGet/safeSet pair with the identical comment (14-20). App.tsx already imports the lib version — the other three predate it.
confirmed.sketch:
api.ts: import { safeGet } from './lib/safeStorage'; getHeaders uses `const token = safeGet('token')`.
socket.ts: same for the auth callback.
LanguageContext.tsx: delete the local safeGet/safeSet (lines 13-20) and import from '../lib/safeStorage'.
confirmed.impact: ~12 duplicated lines removed across 3 files; one canonical place documents the storage-blocked rationale
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as written. Two minor notes: (1) LanguageContext.tsx lines 13-14 carry a provider-specific rationale ("this provider wraps the whole app, so degrade instead of crashing the tree") not present in the lib header — optionally keep that single comment line near the import; the lib header at safeStorage.ts:1-3 covers the general rationale either way. (2) The proposal's claim that only App.tsx imports the lib understates it — ThemeContext, Dashboard, TasksBoard, and AgentDetail do too, which strengthens the case. socket.ts:56's comment referencing localStorage remains accurate after the swap; leave it.

confirmed.title: Delete four unimported components and dead prop chain (~500 lines)
confirmed.description: Grep across frontend/src finds zero imports of RagTab (123 lines), HandoffTab (124), ExecutionLogEntry (202), and AgentTabs (29) — only their own definitions match. RagTab and HandoffTab are stale older copies of the RAG section (ContextTab.tsx:266-424) and Handoff section (ContextTab.tsx:426-509); HandoffTab.tsx:23-30 even contains the OLD leak-prone socket.once handoff logic that ContextTab deliberately fixed (ContextTab.tsx:160-191 with cleanup ref + timeout), so keeping it actively misleads readers about how handoff works. ExecutionLogEntry duplicates HistoryDetailModal's MODE_LABELS/MODE_COLORS and duration formatting. Additionally: the `agents` prop is threaded TasksBoard.tsx:922 -> KanbanColumn.tsx:6/245 -> TaskCard.tsx:8 but TaskCard never reads it (it uses task.assigneeName/assigneeIcon); and TasksBoard.tsx:115 has the no-op ternary `activeBoard?.share_permission || (activeBoard ? 'admin' : 'admin')` which always yields 'admin'.
confirmed.sketch: 1) `git rm` the four files. 2) Remove `agents` from TaskCard and KanbanColumn props and from the JSX at TasksBoard.tsx:922 and KanbanColumn.tsx:245. 3) Replace TasksBoard.tsx:115 with `const boardPermission = activeBoard?.share_permission || 'admin';`. Verify with `tsc` + vite build (no imports exist, so both pass).
confirmed.impact: ~480 lines deleted outright, plus 3 prop-threading sites; removes a stale contradictory implementation of handoff
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is accurate as written; two precision notes. (1) Keep the `agents` prop on TasksBoard itself — it is genuinely used at TasksBoard.tsx:213, 355, 806-807, 971, 1001, 1018, 1034; remove only the pass-through at line 922 plus the `agents` entries in KanbanColumn.tsx:6/245 and TaskCard.tsx:8. (2) The components are plain (not React.memo), so dropping the prop cannot alter re-render behavior — no trap there. Minor citation correction: ContextTab's fixed handoff handler spans roughly lines 152-201 (the proposal cites 160-191), with cleanup ref at 159-168 and the timeout at 185-189; substance of the claim is correct.

confirmed.title: Extract shared plugin-UI module for PluginsTab and BoardPluginsTab
confirmed.description: The two plugin screens are verbatim copies of each other in four places: MCP_CONNECTOR_MAP (PluginsTab.tsx:18-28 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:14-24, 9 connector imports duplicated in both files), getPluginMcpIds (PluginsTab.tsx:30-39 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:26-35), categoryColors/getCategoryClass (PluginsTab.tsx:142-151 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:77-85), and the assigned-plugin card with embedded connector widgets + category filter pills + available-plugin row (PluginsTab.tsx:165-307 vs BoardPluginsTab.tsx:127-238). Today adding a new MCP connector (e.g. a Notion plugin) requires editing two files identically; missing one silently breaks one of the surfaces.
confirmed.sketch:
New `frontend/src/components/plugins/pluginShared.tsx` exporting:
- `MCP_CONNECTOR_MAP: Record<string, ComponentType<{agentId?; boardId?; onStatusChange}>>`
- `getPluginMcpIds(plugin): string[]`
- `getCategoryClass(cat: string): string`
- `<CategoryFilterPills categories value onChange />`
- `<AssignedPluginCard plugin connectorProps onRemove extraActions?>{badges}</AssignedPluginCard>` (renders icon/name/category/MCP badges + connector widgets keyed off connectorProps `{agentId}` or `{boardId}`)
- `<AvailablePluginRow plugin onAdd addLabel badges? />`
PluginsTab keeps only its activation-modal flow and the partagé/privé/auth-requise badges (passed as children); BoardPluginsTab keeps only its load/assign/remove state.
confirmed.impact: ~150 duplicated lines removed; new connectors registered once; both files drop under 220 lines
confirmed.guidance: Corrections and traps: (1) The description's "verbatim copies in four places" overstates the fourth — the assigned-card/available-row JSX shares identical wrappers but diverges in badges (partagé/privé, auth requise), the KeyRound "Modifier mes accès" button (PluginsTab.tsx:193-199), needsCreds button label, handleAssign(plugin) vs handleAssign(plugin.id), connector props, and empty-state text; the sketch's props/children design handles this, but implement it as parameterization, not unification. (2) Badge DOM-order trap in AvailablePluginRow: in PluginsTab the partagé/privé badge renders BEFORE the MCP-count badge (267-278) and "auth requise" AFTER it (284-288) — a single badges slot cannot preserve order; use two slots (beforeMcp/afterMcp) or let the call site render the MCP badge. Same trap in AssignedPluginCard: the "partagé" badge sits between category and MCP badges (180-189). (3) Keep connectors keyed by mcpId in both surfaces to avoid remounting OAuth widgets with in-flight state. (4) Do not retype MCP_CONNECTOR_MAP as ComponentType<{agentId?; boardId?; onStatusChange}> unless all 9 connectors' prop types accept optional ids — keep the existing Record<string, any> for this pass; TS strictness is out of scope. (5) Preserve the differing error handling: PluginsTab's handleAssign/handleRemove set actionError while BoardPluginsTab's have no try/catch — leave both as-is in call sites. (6) Bonus: also point BroadcastPanel.tsx:9-17 (third verbatim categoryColors/getCategoryClass copy) at the new shared module; moving it from component-body scope to module scope is behavior-identical since it is a pure object. (7) pluginNeedsCredentials and the activation-modal flow must stay PluginsTab-only as sketched. (8) Impact claim "both files under 220 lines" is optimistic for PluginsTab (347 lines, modal stays; ~250-270 realistic) — immaterial to the decision.

confirmed.title: useBoardRepos / useBoardStorages hooks for the thrice-duplicated plugin-resource fetch
confirmed.description: The pattern "if no boardId clear list; else fetch api.getBoardAvailableRepos/Storages, store list, store error message on failure" is copy-pasted: repos at TaskDetailModal.tsx:52-59, CreateTaskModal.tsx:33-39, AgentDetail.tsx:78-85; storages at TaskDetailModal.tsx:62-69 and CreateTaskModal.tsx:56-62. Each call site re-declares the same two useState pairs (availableRepos/repoLoadError etc.). Only AgentDetail has the `cancelled` guard against late responses after board switch — the other four copies silently lack it, an inconsistency a shared hook would erase.
confirmed.impact: ~45 lines removed across 3 files; cancellation behavior becomes uniform; 4 useState pairs disappear from TaskDetailModal/CreateTaskModal
confirmed.guidance: Corrections and traps for the implementer: (1) The AgentDetail copy differs in more than the cancelled guard — it also normalizes the response with `Array.isArray(repos) ? repos : []` and swallows errors (no error state). The hook should keep the Array.isArray normalization (strictly defensive) and always track error; AgentDetail simply ignores the returned `error`, preserving its silent-failure behavior. (2) Preserve distinct fallback messages per resource: 'Failed to load repos' vs 'Failed to load storages'. (3) In the modal copies, the falsy-boardId early return does NOT clear a previously-set error (e.g. TaskDetailModal.tsx:54 returns before line 55's setRepoLoadError(null)); a hook will naturally clear it — acceptable normalization since modal boardIds never transition set→unset mid-lifetime, but make it deliberate, not accidental. (4) Adding the cancelled guard to the four modal copies is a behavior change in the strict sense (late responses after a boardId change are now dropped instead of overwriting fresh state); it fixes a latent race and should be named as such in the commit message. (5) CreateTaskModal's dependent effects (lines 42-49, 65-72) and handleSubmit (line 103) must consume the hook's returned arrays under the same local names — `const { repos: availableRepos, error: repoLoadError } = useBoardRepos(boardId)` — so no other lines change. (6) Optional tightening: implement one generic `useBoardList(boardId, fetcher, fallbackMsg)` internally with `useBoardRepos`/`useBoardStorages` as two-line wrappers, so the hook body itself isn't duplicated. Note frontend/src/hooks/ does not exist yet; creating it for this is fine.

confirmed.title: Shared RecurrenceFields component + recurrence payload/label helpers
confirmed.description: Three duplications around recurrence: (1) the editor form — period select, custom-minutes input, purge-after-days input — is near-verbatim in TaskDetailModal.tsx:606-649 and CreateTaskModal.tsx:278-321 (same labels, same clamping `Math.max(0, Math.min(3650, parseInt(...)))`); (2) the payload construction `{enabled, period, intervalMinutes: custom ? n : RECURRENCE_PERIODS.find(...).minutes || 1440, historyRetentionDays: d > 0 ? d : null}` is duplicated at TaskDetailModal.tsx:174-181 and CreateTaskModal.tsx:94-101; (3) TaskDetailModal.tsx:559-565 re-implements period display labels as a 5-branch ternary chain ('hourly' ? 'Every hour' : ...) instead of looking up RECURRENCE_PERIODS, so adding a period means touching 3+ places.
confirmed.impact: ~85 duplicated lines removed; period definitions live in exactly one table
confirmed.guidance: Two behavior-preservation traps to respect: (1) ACCENT COLOR — the duplicated forms are NOT pixel-identical: TaskDetailModal uses focus:border-teal-500 (lines 614/629/645), CreateTaskModal uses focus:border-indigo-500 (lines 285/300/318). RecurrenceFields must take an accent prop (e.g. focusClass or accent='teal'|'indigo') or the visual identity changes in one modal. (2) DISABLED-BRANCH WIRE FORMAT — the else branches differ deliberately: TaskDetailModal.tsx:181 sends `{ enabled: false }` (explicit disable on updateTask PATCH) while CreateTaskModal.tsx:101 sends `undefined` (field omitted from the positional addTask call at line 105). buildRecurrence must build ONLY the enabled-true payload; drop the `enabled` param from the sketched signature and keep each call site's own ternary (`recEnabled ? buildRecurrence(...) : { enabled: false }` / `recurring ? buildRecurrence(...) : undefined`). Unifying the falsy branch would change the addTask request body. Minor: copy the intervalMinutes expression verbatim (`custom ? n : RECURRENCE_PERIODS.find(...)?.minutes || 1440`) to preserve the `|| 1440` falsy fallback ordering, and keep recurrenceLabel's custom-check before the table lookup so 'Custom interval' (the table label for custom) is never displayed. taskConstants.ts is loosely typed JS-in-TS; matching that style avoids tsc friction.

confirmed.title: TaskDetailModal: collapse five copy-pasted inline-edit metadata rows into an EditableSelectRow
confirmed.description: TaskDetailModal (1253 lines, ~30 useState hooks) renders five metadata rows that are the same widget copy-pasted: Repo (697-748), Storage (750-801), Type (803-869), Assignee (871-940), Board (942-992). Each repeats: an editingX boolean, a savingX boolean, a label row with lucide icon, a view mode (colored badge or italic 'None' + Edit3 pencil), and an edit mode (autoFocus select with defaultValue + onChange-saves + X cancel button). The save handlers handleRepoChange (134-150) and handleStorageChange (152-168) are also structurally identical, and Type/Assignee inline their save logic inside JSX onChange (814-828, 882-896), which is why the file reads as a wall. 10 of the 30 state hooks exist only to serve these rows.
confirmed.impact: ~230 lines removed from TaskDetailModal (1253 -> ~1000), 10 state hooks eliminated, all five rows guaranteed to behave identically
confirmed.guidance: 1) Exclude the Board row (942-992) from EditableSelectRow, or support its confirm-flow explicitly: its select onChange does NOT save (sets boardMoveTarget → confirmation modal 1194-1235; actual save in handleBoardMoveConfirm 205-221 which also calls onClose), the select stays open behind the modal, disabled={task.actionRunning} not a saving flag, no empty option, defaultValue falls back to activeBoardId, cancel resets editingBoard AND boardMoveTarget. Extract only Repo/Storage/Type/Assignee. 2) Let each row's onSave wrapper own the no-op guard and refresh, because they differ: repo/storage `await onRefresh()` before closing edit; type/assignee fire `onRefresh?.()` un-awaited. And the guards are not equivalent: repo/storage compare `(v || null) === (task.x || null)` (134-135, 152-153) while type/assignee compare `null === (task.x || '')` (816, 884), meaning selecting None/Unassigned when already empty currently DOES hit the API (updateTask {taskType:''} / setTaskAssignee(null)). Do not normalize the guard inside the shared component — replicate per-row, or change it only as a deliberate, called-out fix. 3) The pencil-replacement error states differ and must be a prop: repo shows amber 'github plugin off' with title={repoLoadError} (735-736); storage shows dark 'No drive connected' without title (788-789); type/assignee always show the pencil. 4) Select disabled conditions differ: repo/storage disable when saving OR options list empty (709, 762); type/assignee only while saving (829, 897). 5) Preserve cosmetics per-row via props: storage uses amber border/hover accent vs indigo elsewhere; widths none vs w-36; option key shapes (storage key `${s.provider}:${s.path}`); assignee badge is clickable (onNavigateToAgent + onClose, 920-926) and its empty label is 'Unassigned' not 'None'. 6) Keep the uncontrolled `defaultValue` + autoFocus select semantics; do not switch to a controlled value. 7) Correct the impact claims: file is 1213 lines (not 1253); with Board excluded, savings are ~150-180 lines and 8 state hooks (not 230/10).

confirmed.title: TasksBoard: extract one optimistic moveTaskToColumn used by drop, touch-drop, and batch move
confirmed.description: handleDrop (496-551), handleTouchDrop (554-598) and handleBatchMove (601-620) re-implement the same fragile optimistic-update protocol: find task / guard actionRunning / inFlightTaskIds.current.add / optimistic setDbTasks status change / updateTaskById({column}) / apply server response / revert to prevStatus on error / setTimeout 2000ms in-flight release. The subtle clock-skew rationale is written once at 518-522 and then referenced by 'See handleDrop' comments at 573 and 605 — a sign the logic itself should be shared, not the comment. handleDrop and handleTouchDrop differ only in payload parsing and where the card is inserted (dropIdx vs append).
confirmed.impact: ~70 lines removed; the clock-skew/in-flight invariants are encoded once instead of three commented copies; TasksBoard drops toward 1000 lines
confirmed.guidance: Scope the helper to handleDrop + handleTouchDrop only. (1) Keep handleTouchDrop's same-column no-op: it must early-return (or pass insertIdx undefined) before reaching the helper's same-column branch, because it always supplies a defined insertIdx (tasksByColumn[col.id].length) and the sketched helper would otherwise turn a same-column touch drop into a reorder-to-end plus a reorderTasks API call — current behavior is a silent no-op (lines 569-570). (2) Do NOT map handleBatchMove over the per-task helper: it has no per-task actionRunning guard (helper would skip running tasks), its error path is refreshAll() not per-task status revert, Promise.all over raw updateTaskById is fail-fast while the error-swallowing helper never rejects, and the helper's success-path refreshAll() would fire once per task instead of once total. The shared optimistic/in-flight bracket there is ~6 lines; leave handleBatchMove untouched. (3) Keep an outer try/catch in the callers (or helper) — current code at 502-550/556-597 swallows synchronous throws from lookup/reorderColumnTasks; the sketch's helper has no outer catch, so a throw in the same-column reorder branch would become an unhandled rejection. (4) Console labels differ ('Drop API failed' vs 'Touch drop API failed'); pass a label param if log greppability matters. (5) isReadOnly guard stays in callers (helper signature lacks it). Impact restated: ~40-45 lines, not ~70, since batch move stays.

confirmed.title: TaskDetailModal: extract TaskTimeline and unify history-entry labels with HistoryDetailModal
confirmed.description: TaskDetailModal.tsx:995-1153 is a 158-line IIFE inside JSX that builds the merged history+commit timeline and renders each entry via a six-branch nested ternary chain (1079-1141: execution/edit/reassign/error/stopped/status-change, each picking its own icon + color + label). HistoryDetailModal.tsx:56-82 (renderEntryIcon/renderEntryTitle) plus its MODE_LABELS/MODE_COLORS tables (11-12) encode the same entry-type -> icon/label/color mapping a second time, and TaskDetailModal line 1083 re-implements MODE_LABELS as an inline ternary (`item.h.mode === 'refine' ? 'Refine' : item.h.mode === 'decide' ? 'Decide' : ...`). Two parallel renderings of the same domain concept guarantee drift (e.g. the 'restored' type exists in HistoryDetailModal:62 but not in the TaskDetailModal timeline).
confirmed.sketch:
1) Move MODE_LABELS/MODE_COLORS into taskConstants.ts and add `HISTORY_ENTRY_META: Record<type, {Icon, color, label(entry)}>` covering execution/edit/reassign/error/stopped/restored/status.
2) New `tasks/TaskTimeline.tsx` with props `{history, commits, onOpenCommit, onRemoveCommit, onOpenEntry, createdAt}` containing the merge-and-sort (current lines 996-1006) and per-entry row rendering as a lookup over HISTORY_ENTRY_META instead of the ternary chain.
3) HistoryDetailModal's renderEntryIcon/renderEntryTitle become thin wrappers over HISTORY_ENTRY_META.
confirmed.impact: TaskDetailModal loses ~160 lines; one source of truth for entry icons/labels/mode names; new history types automatically consistent in both views
confirmed.guidance: Narrow the scope. KEEP: (a) extract TaskTimeline.tsx from TaskDetailModal.tsx:995-1153 with props {history, commits, createdAt, onOpenEntry, onOpenCommit (accepting hash|null to cover both the per-commit click at 1040 and the 'View all diffs' button at 1026), onRemoveCommit (wrapping the async api.removeTaskCommit -> onRefresh at 1052-1055)}; (b) move MODE_LABELS into taskConstants.ts and replace the inline ternary at TaskDetailModal:1083 with MODE_LABELS[item.h.mode || 'execute'] || 'Execution' (exactly equivalent). DROP: the shared HISTORY_ENTRY_META consumed by both views. Reasons it breaks behavior preservation: (1) execution label color — timeline is success ? text-blue-300 : text-red-300 for ALL modes (line 1082) but the modal uses MODE_COLORS[mode] (violet for refine, amber for decide, teal for title, pink for set_type; HistoryDetailModal:71); a single color field restyles one view.
