(2) labels and label colors diverge by design — timeline: 'edited {fields}' in text-dark-200 (1093), 'reassigned' in text-dark-200 (1101), error snippet .slice(0,80) (1117), msg counts (1086-1088), from -> status with ArrowRight (1130-1136); modal: 'Field edited' in amber-300, 'Reassigned' in indigo-300, 'Status change' (HistoryDetailModal:76-81). A single label(entry) cannot serve both; a two-variant meta table is over-abstraction. (3) adding 'restored' to the timeline is a visible UI change: restored entries currently render via the default status-change branch (showing their status field, see api/src/services/agentManager/tasks.ts:553); under the meta they'd render a teal RotateCcw 'restored' row — the proposal's 'automatically consistent in both views' impact is a behavior change, not a refactor. (4) icon table is not fully shared either: timeline default branch has no leading icon; modal default uses ArrowRight dark-400; modal has RotateCcw for restored, timeline has none. Extraction traps: preserve concat order [...historyItems, ...commitItems] before the stable sort so equal timestamps keep history-before-commit ordering; preserve React keys h-${i} and c-${c.hash || i}; keep the empty-timeline 'Created' fallback (1007-1019); keep the entries' missing-date fallback (treats falsy at as epoch 0, lines 1003-1004). Optionally TaskTimeline may internally use a lookup table instead of the ternary chain — fine, as long as it is private to the timeline and reproduces the exact per-branch markup. MODE_COLORS only has one consumer (HistoryDetailModal); moving it to taskConstants is optional and adds nothing.
confirmed.note: KEEP-set only per guidance: TaskTimeline extracted + MODE_LABELS centralized; HISTORY_ENTRY_META label unification deliberately dropped (would change behavior)

confirmed.title: PermissionsTab: extract buildPerms/buildHookRules and a markDirty helper
confirmed.description: Two non-trivial merge expressions are duplicated verbatim: the 4-section DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS spread-merge appears at the useState initializer (184-191) and again in the agent.id effect (211-218); the BUILTIN_RULES overlay merge (`BUILTIN_RULES.map(builtin => override ? {...builtin, ...override} : {...builtin}).concat(existing.filter(...))`) appears at 200-206 and 221-227. If a fifth permission section is added, both copies must change identically or initial mount and agent-switch diverge. Also the pair `setHasChanges(true); setSaved(false);` is repeated in five handlers (237-238, 281, 286, 295, 300), and `!BUILTIN_RULES.find(b => b.id === ...)` does an O(n) scan per rule render (501).
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines removed; mount and agent-switch initialization provably identical; rule-origin check becomes a Set lookup
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Four traps to respect: (1) keep useState initializers lazy — useState(() => buildPerms(agent)) / useState(() => buildHookRules(agent)) — eager calls would re-run the merges every render; (2) do NOT route the effect's reset (lines 228-229: setHasChanges(false); setSaved(false)) or handleSave's setSaved(true); setHasChanges(false) (248-249) through markDirty — different semantics; markDirty applies only to the five cited handlers; (3) buildPerms must retain the top-level ...agent.permissions spread before the four section merges so unknown extra permission keys survive the save round-trip via api.updateAgent; (4) keep params loosely typed ((agent), (r: any)) consistent with the file's untyped-props style so frontend tsc stays at 0 errors. Set-vs-find at line 501 is semantically equivalent (string ids, truthy objects). Also reuse BUILTIN_RULE_IDS inside buildHookRules' filter, replacing both find-based scans.

confirmed.title: ChatTab: one image-ingestion helper for file picker and clipboard paste
confirmed.description: The image policy (accepted mime regex `^image\/(png|jpeg|gif|webp)$`, 10MB cap, FileReader -> dataURL -> `^data:(image\/[^;]+);base64,(.+)$` extraction -> onAddImages([{mediaType, data, preview}])) is implemented twice: handleFileSelect at ChatTab.tsx:238-261 and the textarea onPaste handler inlined in JSX at ChatTab.tsx:478-499. The paste copy is buried 25 lines deep inside the textarea props, making the input JSX hard to scan, and any policy change (new format, size limit) must be applied twice.
confirmed.impact: ~30 lines removed; the textarea JSX becomes readable; one place defines accepted formats/size
confirmed.guidance: Two corrections to the sketch, both behavior-preservation traps: (1) preventDefault ordering — the original onPaste (line 481-483) calls e.preventDefault() when any clipboard ITEM has an image mime, BEFORE getAsFile(); the sketch defers it until after getAsFile() null-filtering, changing the edge case where getAsFile() returns null. Keep original order: filter imageItems by item.type, early-return if empty, preventDefault(), then map to files and call ingestImageFiles. (2) In the helper's onload, keep the existing `if (typeof dataUrl !== 'string') return;` guard verbatim instead of the sketch's String() coercion, and use dataUrl as preview — this matches both originals exactly and keeps TS narrowing. Also preserve: the `if (!supportsImages) return;` early return at the top of onPaste; `e.target.value = ''` reset only in handleFileSelect; and the per-file single-element onAddImages([{...}]) calls (do not batch files into one call — readers complete asynchronously and out of order). The mime re-check on file.type inside the helper for pasted files is a harmless redundancy (File.type equals the DataTransferItem.type for clipboard images). Optional bonus, not required: derive the input accept attr (line 402) from the same const list so the format policy truly lives in one place.

confirmed.title: ChatMessage: extract the triplicated truncate ('Restart from here') button
confirmed.description: The exact same 9-line button — `{!isLast && onTruncate && (<button onClick={() => onTruncate(index - 1)} className="absolute -right-1 top-1 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 ..." title="Restart from here"><Scissors/></button>)}` — appears three times in the same file: in the tool-result branch (195-204), the delegation-task branch (232-241), and the default branch (312-320). The 200-char className is repeated character-for-character.
confirmed.impact: ~20 lines removed; styling/behavior of the truncate affordance defined once
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is correct as-is. Three constraints to respect: (1) define TruncateButton at module scope (like DelegationCallBlock), not inside ChatMessage, to avoid per-render remounts; (2) keep it rendered inside each `group relative` wrapper — group-hover visibility and absolute positioning depend on that ancestor; (3) keep the full className as one static string literal so Tailwind's content scanner still sees the classes. Optionally pass {isLast, onTruncate, index} and guard inside the component (`if (isLast || !onTruncate) return null`) instead of a `show` prop — equivalent and slightly less boilerplate at the three call sites.

confirmed.title: api.addTask: replace 11 positional params with an options object
confirmed.description: api.ts:254 declares `addTask(agentId, text, status, boardId, repoFullName, recurrence, taskType, isManual, repoProvider = 'github', storagePath = null, storageProvider = 'onedride')`-style with 11 positional params. The two call sites show the cost: CreateTaskModal.tsx:105 passes an unreadable run of 11 mixed values where `'github'` is hardcoded in position 9 (even though availableRepos carries a per-repo provider — TaskDetailModal.tsx:139-141 derives it properly, so the inconsistency is invisible at the call site); AllCommitsDiffModal.tsx:327 passes EIGHT consecutive `undefined`s. The wire format (POST body) is already a named object built inside addTask, so this is purely an internal signature fix.
confirmed.sketch:
```ts
addTask: (agentId, text, opts: { status?; boardId?; repoFullName?; repoProvider?; recurrence?; taskType?; isManual?; storagePath?; storageProvider? } = {}) => apiFetch(..., { body: JSON.stringify({ text, ...(opts.status && {status: opts.status}), ... }) })
```
CreateTaskModal: `api.addTask(defaultAgentId, trimmed, { status, boardId, repoFullName: repoFullName || undefined, repoProvider: availableRepos.find(r => r.fullName === repoFullName)?.provider, recurrence, taskType: taskType || undefined, isManual: isManual || undefined, storagePath: storagePath || null, storageProvider })`. AllCommitsDiffModal: `api.addTask(agentId, taskText)`. (Keeping repoProvider 'github' as the default preserves behavior; deriving it is an optional one-line follow-up.)
confirmed.impact: Both call sites become self-describing; eliminates the 8x-undefined call; positional-ordering bugs become impossible
confirmed.guidance: 1) The sketch contradicts its own parenthetical: the proposed CreateTaskModal call passes `repoProvider: availableRepos.find(r => r.fullName === repoFullName)?.provider`, which DERIVES the provider — a behavior change to the POST body for non-github repos (current code always sends 'github'). For the behavior-preserving refactor, pass `repoProvider: 'github'` explicitly (or omit it and rely on the default); do the derivation as a separate follow-up commit if the owner wants the bug fix. 2) Keep the body construction at api.ts:258-267 verbatim; just destructure: `const { status, boardId, repoFullName, recurrence, taskType, isManual, repoProvider = 'github', storagePath = null, storageProvider = 'onedrive' } = opts;`. In particular preserve the coupling that repoProvider/storageProvider only ship when repoFullName/storagePath are truthy (`...(repoFullName && { repoFullName, repoProvider })`). 3) Delete the now-stale comment at AllCommitsDiffModal.tsx:324-326 ("api.addTask declares ... required params ... passing undefined is runtime-identical") when the call becomes `api.addTask(agentId, taskText)`. 4) Minor: the proposal typos the default as 'onedride'; actual code is 'onedrive'. 5) CreateTaskModal must keep passing `storagePath: storagePath || null` and its computed `storageProvider` (lines 102-104) unchanged.

confirmed.title: TerminalTab: move Claude OAuth link reconstruction into its own module
confirmed.description: TerminalTab.tsx is 735 lines mixing two unrelated concerns: the PTY/WebSocket/resize lifecycle (the actual terminal) and ~135 lines of Claude OAuth URL reconstruction (constants at 41-43, openExternalLink 95-103, getTerminalLine/firstNonWhitespaceIndex/buildClaudeOAuthLink/createClaudeOAuthLinkProvider/reconstructClaudeOAuthUrlFromBuffer at 105-175). A reader tracing a reconnect bug has to scroll past pure link-parsing code first. Notably ChatMessage.tsx:118-148 contains a second, independent implementation of the same concept (reconstructWrappedOAuthUrls, joining a wrapped claude.com/cai/oauth URL across lines) for plain text instead of the xterm buffer — colocating both in one module makes the parallel visible and keeps the CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX constant single-sourced.
confirmed.sketch: New `frontend/src/components/agentDetail/claudeOAuthLinks.ts` exporting `CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX`, `openExternalLink(uri)`, `createClaudeOAuthLinkProvider(term)`, `reconstructClaudeOAuthUrlFromBuffer(term, uri)` (xterm flavor) and `reconstructWrappedOAuthUrlsInText(text)` (moved from ChatMessage). TerminalTab imports the three xterm helpers (used at lines 375-383); ChatMessage imports the text flavor in linkifyRawUrls (line 153). No behavior change — pure relocation.
confirmed.impact: TerminalTab drops to ~590 lines focused on terminal lifecycle; the two OAuth-rewrapping implementations live side by side with one shared prefix constant; helpers become unit-testable
confirmed.guidance: Behavior-preservation traps for the implementer: (1) Do NOT unify the two implementations — they differ deliberately. The xterm flavor caps continuation at CLAUDE_OAUTH_MAX_CONTINUATION_LINES=32 (TerminalTab.tsx:124-128) and gates on the https-only CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX via indexOf/includes (lines 114, 156, 165, 170); the text flavor (ChatMessage.tsx:118-148) is uncapped (consumes lines until blank/EOF) and matches an `https?` regex with no prefix gate, so it rewraps http:// URLs the xterm flavor ignores. Colocate only. (2) Do not derive the `https?:\/\/claude\.com\/cai\/oauth\/authorize\?code=\S*` regexes from CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX or vice versa — the constant is https-only while both regexes accept http; deriving either direction changes matching behavior. (3) Keep the `typeof text !== 'string'` guard in the moved text function (ChatMessage.tsx:119) — callers pass seg.content which may be non-string and tsconfig is non-strict, so there is no compile-time protection. (4) Move all three constants (lines 41-43) plus the private helpers getTerminalLine/firstNonWhitespaceIndex/buildClaudeOAuthLink into the new module; they can stay unexported there. (5) Minor description inaccuracy, not a blocker: CLAUDE_OAUTH_PREFIX is not currently duplicated in ChatMessage (it uses an inline regex, not the constant), so "single-sourced constant" overstates the dedup — exporting it from the new module changes nothing for ChatMessage and the import should not replace ChatMessage's regex.

confirmed.title: Delete dead VoiceChat.tsx component (unimported, superseded by VoiceChatTab)
confirmed.description: frontend/src/components/VoiceChat.tsx (155 lines) is imported nowhere — a repo-wide grep for 'VoiceChat' shows only AgentDetail.tsx importing VoiceChatTab and ExternalVoiceChatTab, never VoiceChat. It is the old voice UI: its own statusColor/statusLabel switch (VoiceChat.tsx:5-45) duplicates STATUS_LABELS already exported by VoiceSessionContext. It is also the sole consumer of currentTranscript/currentResponse/currentFunction exposed by VoiceSessionContext (VoiceSessionContext.tsx:104-106, 924-926, 943-945), so those context fields are vestigial too.
confirmed.sketch: 1) Delete frontend/src/components/VoiceChat.tsx. 2) Optionally (follow-up, context file is shared scope): remove currentTranscript/currentResponse/currentFunction state and their entries from the context value object in VoiceSessionContext.tsx if no other consumer exists (grep confirms none). Run tsc + vite build to confirm.
confirmed.impact: -155 lines immediately (plus ~15 more in VoiceSessionContext); removes a misleading second voice UI that no longer reflects the real voice flow
confirmed.guidance: Step 1 (delete VoiceChat.tsx) is safe as-is. Corrections for step 2: (a) the impact estimate of "~15 more lines" in VoiceSessionContext is wrong — the reviewer's case-sensitive grep missed ~45 setter call sites (setCurrentTranscript/setCurrentResponse/setCurrentFunction at VoiceSessionContext.tsx:245-898) interleaved with WebRTC/data-channel/delegation logic; full removal is a ~50+ line diff and several handler branches (e.g., the realtime-event switch at 480-521) contain only a setter call, leaving empty branches to tidy. (b) Removing those useState updates reduces provider re-render frequency and context-value identity churn for VoiceChatTab/ExternalVoiceChatTab — invisible since no consumer reads the fields, but not strictly identical render timing. Keep step 2 as a separate optional follow-up commit (as the sketch already says) or skip it; do not bundle it with the file deletion. Citation nit: VoiceChat.tsx:5-19 is statusColor, 36-45 is the statusLabel memo that duplicates STATUS_LABELS.

confirmed.title: Collapse the 6 OAuth connect widgets into one OAuthConnectWidget + useConnectStatus hook
confirmed.description: Six files (222+230+218+248+233+231 = 1,382 lines) are near-clones: diff(GmailConnect, GoogleDriveConnect) = 54 differing lines out of 230. Every file repeats: status state + onStatusChangeRef + pollRef (GmailConnect.tsx:14-27 = GitHubConnect.tsx:6-17 = OneDriveConnect.tsx:14-27), fetchStatus (GmailConnect.tsx:29-45), the postMessage OAuth-callback listener (GmailConnect.tsx:48-63, OneDriveConnect.tsx:48-71, GitHubConnect.tsx:37-58 — same semantics, different message type/service filter), the popup-open + popup-closed polling block (GmailConnect.tsx:65-104 = OneDriveConnect.tsx:73-112 = GitHubConnect.tsx:60-96, byte-identical except popup name), handleDisconnect, and four render states (loading / statusError+Retry / not-configured / connected card) — GmailConnect.tsx:118-229 vs OneDriveConnect.tsx:126-247. Per-provider deltas are pure data: 3 api methods, message type + optional service field, icon pair, label, button color/label, badge detail field (email/login/teamName), env-var hint text, and OneDrive's extra 'personal' consumer button.
confirmed.impact: ~1,380 lines -> ~410 (shared widget+hook ~260 + 6 thin config files); adding the next OAuth provider becomes a 20-line config instead of a 230-line copy-paste; bug fixes (e.g. the popup-poll cleanup) land once instead of six times
confirmed.guidance: 1) MAIN TRAP — the message listeners are NOT 'same semantics' as claimed; there are two variants. Variant A (GmailConnect.tsx:48-63, GoogleDriveConnect.tsx:41-55): guards `if ('success' in event.data)`, does NOT setConnecting(true)/setError(null) on message receipt, no try/catch. Variant B (GitHubConnect.tsx:37-58, SlackConnect.tsx:46-67, OneDriveConnect.tsx:48-71, OutlookConnect.tsx:48-70): no 'success' guard, sets connecting(true) and clears error immediately, try/catch/finally. Practical deltas: B shows the 'Connecting...' spinner during the post-callback fetchStatus while A does not; B clears a stale error on callback; A silently ignores a message lacking `success` while B would show 'OAuth failed'. Recommended unification: keep the `'success' in event.data` guard for everyone AND adopt B's setConnecting(true)/setError(null). This changes Gmail/GDrive only by a sub-second spinner + error-clear (UX-neutral or better) and changes B providers only for malformed messages that the server-rendered oauth-redirect page never sends. Do this normalization deliberately and note it in the commit message; do NOT add a per-config flag for it (over-engineering). Also note: B's try/catch around fetchStatus is dead code — fetchStatus never throws (it has its own try/catch/finally at e.g. GmailConnect.tsx:29-41) — safe to drop. 2) Config needs up to THREE icon slots, not an 'icon pair': main icon, off/error-state icon, and disconnect-button icon (Gmail: Mail/MailX/MailX; GitHub: Github/Github/Unplug; GDrive: HardDrive/HardDrive/XCircle; OneDrive: Cloud/CloudOff/CloudOff; Outlook: Mail/MailX/MailX; Slack: MessageSquare/XCircle/XCircle). Preserve these exact pairings — e.g. GitHub uses Unplug only on the disconnect button. 3) Keep popup names as per-config data verbatim — OneDrive uses 'microsoft-oauth' while Outlook uses 'outlook-oauth' despite sharing the Azure app; do not 'fix' that inconsistency. 4) extraConnectActions must be a render prop receiving { connect: (opts?) => void, connecting: boolean } so OneDrive's 'personal' button (OneDriveConnect.tsx:219-226) can call connect({ consumer: true }) and share the disabled state. 5) Type the hook's status as { configured: boolean; connected: boolean; [k: string]: any } — SlackConnect.tsx:180 reads (status as any).teamName and badge accessors differ per provider (email/login/teamName/none; OneDrive shows plain 'Connected'). 6) Preserve the two-variant connect hint (agentId vs global text, e.g. GmailConnect.tsx:222-225) via the sketched connectHint(agentId?) function, and derive the loading label / console.error prefix from config.name ('Checking Google Drive status...', 'Gmail status check failed:').

confirmed.title: Collapse the 3 credential-form connect widgets (Jira, S3, WordPress) into one CredentialConnectWidget
confirmed.description: Same clone family as the OAuth widgets but with an inline credentials form instead of a popup: JiraConnect.tsx:13-110, S3Connect.tsx:5-104 and WordPressConnect.tsx:13-110 are line-for-line identical (state, fetchStatus, handleConnect validate-then-api-then-clear, handleDisconnect), and the loading/statusError/connected-card renders repeat again (JiraConnect.tsx:79-152 = WordPressConnect.tsx:79-152). Only the field definitions differ: Jira domain/email/apiToken (JiraConnect.tsx:154-188), S3 accessKeyId/secretAccessKey/region/endpoint (S3Connect.tsx:148-195), WordPress siteUrl/username/applicationPassword (WordPressConnect.tsx:154-188) — each field is just {label, placeholder, type, required, helpText}.
confirmed.impact: ~650 lines -> ~280 (widget ~140 + 3 configs); together with the OAuth proposal the 9 connect widgets shrink from ~2,030 to ~700 lines
confirmed.guidance: Sketch is sound but incomplete on five behavior-preservation traps: (1) Tailwind JIT: do NOT interpolate `bg-${color}-500/5` etc. — classes must appear literally in source or they get purged. Implement color as a lookup table mapping 'blue'|'orange'|'sky' to full literal class strings for each slot (card bg/border, icon, badge, connect button, focus:border-*). (2) S3 validation differs in two ways the sketch glosses over: only accessKeyId+secretAccessKey are required (region/endpoint are not, despite region being sent), and the validation error message differs — S3Connect.tsx:41 says 'Access Key ID and Secret Access Key are required' while Jira/WP say 'All fields are required'. Add a `requiredError?: string` (or derive message from required field labels) and drive both the pre-submit check and the submit button's disabled expression from the same required-field set. (3) S3 region initializes to 'us-east-1' and resets to 'us-east-1' (not '') after successful connect (S3Connect.tsx:15,51) — reset each field to its `initial ?? ''`, not blanket ''. (4) S3's IAM-console help paragraph (S3Connect.tsx:190-195) is a sibling of the field divs at form level, unlike Jira/WP whose help nests under a field; attaching it as the endpoint field's `help` changes DOM placement and spacing slightly — add an optional `formFooter?: ReactNode` to the config to preserve layout exactly. (5) WordPress badge detail is `status.siteUrl ? new URL(status.siteUrl).host : 'Connected'` (WordPressConnect.tsx:125) — new URL() can throw on malformed input; keep that exact expression inside the WP badgeDetail closure (don't wrap in try/catch — preserve existing behavior). Also: keep per-provider connect closures calling the existing api functions verbatim — api.connectS3 has the surprising arg order (agentId, secretAccessKey, accessKeyId, region, boardId?, endpoint?) and the widget passes `endpoint || undefined` (S3Connect.tsx:47, api.ts:941); preserve the `agentId || ''` / `boardId || undefined` coercions. Field `type` must be configurable per field (email for Jira email, password for all secrets, text otherwise). Finally, the sketch depends on useConnectStatus from the separate OAuth proposal — if that one isn't done first, inline the hook (it's only the fetchStatus/loading/statusError/onStatusChangeRef block) rather than coupling the two changes.

confirmed.title: Split the 1,437-line AdminPanel into one file per tab
confirmed.description: AdminPanel.tsx is a god component: ~30 useState hooks for 4 independent tabs are interleaved at the top (users 34-39, settings 42-56, reset 53-56, llm 58-62, voice-test 64-87, boards 89-97), loaders at 99-197, handlers at 199-398, then four giant conditional render blocks: Settings 473-907, Users 910-1150, LLM 1153-1236, Boards 1239-1431. No state is shared across tabs except showToast (the Settings tab re-loads llmConfigs itself at 196-197, proving independence). Bonus smells: the 4 tab buttons at 423-466 are copy-pasted markup differing only in icon/label (ProjectDetailModal.tsx:10-17 already shows the TABS-array pattern), and ToggleLeft/ToggleRight/UserCheck are imported (line 4) but never used.
confirmed.sketch:
New frontend/src/components/admin/ directory:
  UsersTab.tsx({ showToast, onImpersonate, onClose })   // users state + handleCreate/Update/Delete/Impersonate + ROLE_CONFIG + timeAgo
  SettingsTab.tsx({ showToast })                          // settings, reminderConfig, resetRoles, voiceTest, loads its own llmConfigs
  LlmConfigsTab.tsx({ showToast })                        // llmConfigs + LlmConfigModal wiring
  BoardsTab.tsx({ showToast })                            // boardsList + counts + board form/columns editor
AdminPanel.tsx keeps only the shell (~120 lines): overlay, header, a TABS = [{id,label,icon}] array mapped to buttons, and {activeTab === id && <Tab .../>}. Pure cut-and-paste moves — each tab's state/handlers/JSX are already disjoint blocks.
confirmed.impact: 1,437-line file -> ~120-line shell + 4 tabs of 150-450 lines each; each tab's state lives next to its JSX instead of 800 lines above it; ~40 lines of duplicated tab-button markup and dead imports removed
confirmed.guidance: Behavior-preservation traps the sketch glosses over: (1) Today all tab state lives in the always-mounted AdminPanel, so switching tabs preserves it; the sketched {activeTab === id && <Tab/>} unmounts tabs and resets their state. Concretely lost on a tab round-trip: half-filled create-user form (showCreateForm/createForm/editingUser, lines 36-39), an open board edit form (boardEditingId/boardCreating/boardForm, lines 94-96 — loadBoards re-runs on activation today but does NOT reset the form), and voice-test result banners (voiceTest, line 65). Settings/reminder edits are ALREADY discarded on return (line 149 re-fetches), so those are unaffected. To preserve behavior exactly: keep all four tab components mounted inside the shell and toggle visibility with CSS, passing an `active` prop whose effect drives the activation loads (reproducing lines 149/181/195/197); otherwise accept the state-reset as a deliberate minor UX change, not an invisible one. (2) loadUsers (line 111) runs once on AdminPanel mount and never on tab switches; naive conditional mounting adds a users re-fetch on every return to the Users tab — preserved exactly by the always-mounted variant. (3) Correction to the description: llmConfigs IS shared between Settings (lines 627, 656) and the LLM tab; the sketch's per-tab loadLlmConfigs is fine because line 197 already re-fetches on activation, but duplicate the ~12-line loader (or a tiny useLlmConfigs hook) — do not try to keep it in the shell. (4) Keep AdminPanel.tsx at frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx as the default-exported shell so Dashboard.tsx:24's lazy(() => import('./AdminPanel')) and its chunk boundary are untouched. (5) timeAgo (line 10) and ROLE_CONFIG (line 26) move into UsersTab.tsx; do NOT merge timeAgo with the different timeAgo in frontend/src/components/tasks/taskConstants.ts:55 (different signature/output). (6) UsersTab needs { showToast, onImpersonate, onClose } — handleImpersonate (line 379) calls both onImpersonate and onClose.

confirmed.title: ExternalVoiceChatTab reimplements lib/externalVoiceClient.ts inline (worklet, VAD, STT/TTS sockets duplicated)
confirmed.description: lib/externalVoiceClient.ts opens with 'Reusable browser-side STT + TTS pipeline shared by the text chat and the external-voice tab' (lines 1-3) — but the external-voice tab does NOT use it; only agentDetail/ChatTab.tsx does. ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx carries its own byte-identical copies: WORKLET_CODE (ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx:38-76 = externalVoiceClient.ts:22-59), the four VAD constants (31-34 = 15-18), the STT socket open/message/VAD-finalize logic (openStt 259-313 + onWorkletFrame 316-337 vs SttSession.start 89-161 + onFrame 218-239), and PCM playback (enqueuePcmChunk 160-183 vs TtsPlayer). The stale comment actively misleads: a fix to the lib's VAD will silently not apply to the tab.
confirmed.sketch:
Two options, pick by appetite:
A (full): extend SttSession with a 'keepMicAcrossUtterances' mode + reconnect-retry (the tab's only real behavioral additions, ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx:297-312 and 339-380), then rewrite the tab to drive SttSession/TtsPlayer via callbacks — tab drops to ~250 lines of pure UI + turn loop.
B (minimal, near-zero risk): export WORKLET_CODE, SILENCE_MS/MIN_SPEECH_MS/RMS_SPEECH/RMS_SILENCE, and a shared createPcmWorkletNode(ctx, targetRate) + decodePcm16ToBuffer helper from externalVoiceClient.ts and import them in the tab; fix the lib header comment to state who actually uses what.
Either way the contradiction between comment and reality disappears.
confirmed.impact: Option A: -≈220 lines and one source of truth for the audio pipeline; option B: -≈80 lines but eliminates the dangerous double-maintained worklet/VAD constants
confirmed.guidance: Do Option B only; treat Option A as out of scope for a behavior-preserving pass. Concretely: export WORKLET_CODE, SILENCE_MS/MIN_SPEECH_MS/RMS_SPEECH/RMS_SILENCE, and a decodePcm16ToBuffer(ctx, arrayBuf, sampleRate) helper from frontend/src/lib/externalVoiceClient.ts; import them in frontend/src/components/ExternalVoiceChatTab.tsx; rewrite the lib header comment to state that SttSession/TtsPlayer are used only by agentDetail/ChatTab.tsx while the external-voice tab shares only the low-level audio primitives. When sharing WORKLET_CODE, keep the lib's version (the tab's two extra inline comments are the only difference). Traps if anyone attempts Option A later: (1) tab's onWorkletFrame checks mutedRef before sending PCM AND before VAD — mute freezes VAD state; (2) enqueuePcmChunk drops chunks when speakerOff at both enqueue and play time; (3) tab treats STT 'error' messages as non-fatal (keeps listening), SttSession tears down; (4) SttSession arms a 10s finalize timeout the tab lacks; (5) TtsPlayer creates/closes an AudioContext per reply, the tab reuses one per session; (6) status timing differs (lib fires 'listening' before getUserMedia, tab only on WS open). No frontend test coverage exists for these paths — Option A requires manual verification with live STT/TTS services.

confirmed.title: Dashboard: remove phantom 'processes'/'about' views and unify the duplicated valid-view lists
confirmed.description: Dashboard.tsx:38 accepts hash views ['agents','tasks','projects','budget','processes','about'], and the hashchange handler at line 104 accepts ['agents','tasks','projects','budget','about'] — two out-of-sync inline lists. But the render switch (lines 394-573) only handles tasks/projects/budget/agents: navigating to #about or #processes shows a header over a blank main area. Nothing in frontend/src links to either hash (repo grep). These are leftovers from removed views.
confirmed.sketch: const VALID_VIEWS = ['agents', 'tasks', 'projects', 'budget'] as const; use it in both the useState initializer (line 38) and the hashchange effect (line 104). Delete 'processes' and 'about'.
confirmed.impact: Removes 2 dead view ids and a silent blank-screen path; the two lists can never drift again
confirmed.guidance: One implementation trap in the sketch: `as const` will fail the frontend tsc gate. With `const VALID_VIEWS = ['agents','tasks','projects','budget'] as const`, the call `VALID_VIEWS.includes(hash)` is a type error because `hash` is `string` and a readonly literal tuple's `.includes` expects the literal union. Either declare it without `as const` (inferred `string[]`), annotate `const VALID_VIEWS: string[] = [...]`, or cast at the call sites `(VALID_VIEWS as readonly string[]).includes(hash)`. Also hoist the constant to module scope (outside the component) so the hashchange effect at Dashboard.tsx:101-108 can reference it without adding a dependency to its empty deps array. Behavior note to accept knowingly: hand-typed `#about`/`#processes` URLs currently yield a blank main area; after the change they fall back to 'tasks' (on load) or are ignored (on hashchange) — intended, since nothing in the repo emits those hashes.
